For the exact APY boost percentages for each pairing, please refer to the specific savings account documentation.

title: Moving Money Between Your Rho-Bank Accounts
content:
One of the many conveniences of being a Rho-Bank customer is the ability to easily move money between your accounts. Whether you're transferring funds from your checking account to your savings account to build up your emergency fund, moving money from savings to checking to cover an upcoming expense, or simply reorganizing your finances across multiple accounts, Rho-Bank makes it simple and straightforward.

## Self-Service Options

The fastest way to transfer money between your Rho-Bank accounts is through our digital banking platforms. Using the Rho-Bank mobile app or online banking at rhobank.com, you can initiate transfers instantly, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Simply log in, navigate to the transfer section, select your source and destination accounts, enter the amount, and confirm. It's that easy, and there's no fee for transferring money between your own Rho-Bank accounts.

## Need Assistance? We're Here to Help

If you prefer to have a customer service representative handle the transfer for you, or if you're having any trouble with the digital platforms, our team is happy to assist. Simply call us at 1-800-RHO-BANK or chat with us through the mobile app, and we can process the transfer on your behalf. This can be especially convenient if you're setting up a new savings account and want us to transfer the opening deposit from your checking account, or if you need to consolidate balances across multiple accounts.

## A Few Things to Keep in Mind

Both the source account (where the money is coming from) and the destination account (where the money is going) must be open and in good standing. The source account must have sufficient funds to cover the transfer amount—if the balance is too low, the transfer won't go through. Internal transfers between your own Rho-Bank accounts are always free of charge.

At Rho-Bank, we want managing your money to be as effortless as possible. If you have any questions about transferring funds or need any other assistance with your accounts, please don't hesitate to reach out. We're always here to help.

title: Need Your Transaction History? Our Team Can Help
content:
At Rho-Bank, we believe in complete transparency when it comes to your money. We understand that there are many reasons why you might need to access a detailed record of your transaction history—whether you're preparing your taxes, keeping track of business expenses, reconciling your budget, disputing a charge, or simply trying to understand where your money has been going. Whatever your reason, our customer service team is here to help you get the information you need quickly and easily.

While you can always view your recent transactions through the Rho-Bank mobile app or online banking portal, our customer service representatives have access to comprehensive tools that can provide you with even more detailed information. When you contact us, we can pull your complete transaction history for any checking or savings account, including:

- All posted and pending transactions
- Detailed transaction descriptions and dates
- Fee breakdowns and explanations
- Rebates and credits that have been applied
- Interest payments
- Transfer details between accounts

This can be especially helpful if you're looking for a specific transaction from several months ago, need documentation for a particular time period, or want to understand the details behind a specific fee or credit on your account.

## Contact Us

To request your transaction history or ask questions about specific transactions, simply call us at 1-800-RHO-BANK, chat with us through the Rho-Bank mobile app, or visit our online help center at rhobank.com/help. Our friendly and knowledgeable representatives are standing by and ready to assist you. At Rho-Bank, helping you stay informed and in control of your finances is always our top priority.

title: Notice Something Wrong on Your Statement? We Can Help
content:
At Rho-Bank, we understand that managing your finances can sometimes be stressful, and we want you to know that we're here to support you every step of the way. If you've recently reviewed your account statement and noticed something that doesn't look quite right, please don't hesitate to reach out to our dedicated customer service team. We take accuracy very seriously, and our representatives are fully equipped and trained to investigate any discrepancies on your behalf.

Our team can help you with the following types of issues:

## Missing Rebates

Sometimes, rebates that you're eligible for may not appear on your account right away, or in rare cases, they may not be applied at all due to system timing or processing delays. If you believe you should have received a rebate that hasn't shown up on your statement, our customer service representatives can review your account history, verify your eligibility, and apply the appropriate credit if the rebate was indeed missed.

## Fees That Were Incorrectly Charged

We know how frustrating it can be to see an unexpected fee on your statement. Whether it's a monthly maintenance fee that should have been waived because you met the minimum balance requirement, an ATM fee that should have been refunded under your account's benefits, or any other fee that seems incorrect, our team is ready to help. Simply contact us, and we'll thoroughly review the charge in question. If we determine that the fee was applied in error, we'll promptly credit your account for the full amount.

## Contact Us

To get started, you can reach our customer service team by calling 1-800-RHO-BANK, or by visiting our online help center at rhobank.com/help. You can also chat with us directly through the Rho-Bank mobile app. We're available Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 8 PM, and Saturday 9 AM to 5 PM. We're committed to making things right, because your trust and satisfaction are what matter most to us at Rho-Bank.

title: Understanding Regulation E: Your Debit Card Consumer Protections
content:
Regulation E is a federal regulation implemented by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) that governs electronic fund transfers (EFTs) and provides important consumer protections for debit card transactions. As a Rho-Bank customer, understanding these protections can help you know your rights when issues arise with your debit card.

## What Regulation E Covers

Regulation E applies to electronic fund transfers including:
- Debit card purchases (both PIN and signature transactions)
- ATM withdrawals and deposits
- Direct deposits
- Automatic bill payments
- Person-to-person (P2P) transfers
- Recurring electronic payments

## Your Key Protections Under Regulation E

### 1. Limited Liability for Unauthorized Transactions

If someone uses your debit card without permission, your liability is limited based on how quickly you report it:
- **Within 2 business days**: Maximum $50 liability
- **Within 60 days**: Maximum $500 liability
- **After 60 days**: You may be liable for the full amount

### 2. Right to Dispute Errors

You have the right to dispute any error on your account, including:
- Unauthorized transactions
- Incorrect transaction amounts
- Missing deposits or transfers
- Computational errors
- Transactions that weren't completed as instructed

### 3. Investigation Requirements

When you report an error, Rho-Bank must:
- Investigate promptly (typically within 10 business days)
- Report results to you within 3 business days of completing the investigation
- Correct any confirmed errors within 1 business day of determination

### 4. Provisional Credit

For qualifying disputes, Rho-Bank must provide provisional (temporary) credit within 10 business days if the investigation takes longer than 10 business days. This ensures you're not left without access to your funds during the investigation.

### 5. Documentation Rights

You have the right to:
- Receive written confirmation of error resolution
- Request copies of documents used in the investigation
- Receive advance notice before provisional credit is reversed

## How to Exercise Your Regulation E Rights

To dispute an unauthorized or erroneous transaction:
1. Contact Rho-Bank customer service as soon as you notice the issue
2. Provide details about the transaction(s) in question
3. Follow up with a written statement if requested
4. Keep records of all communications

## Important Notes

- These protections apply specifically to debit card and electronic transactions, not credit cards (which are covered by different regulations)
- Business accounts may have different protections than personal accounts
- Promptly reviewing your statements helps you identify issues quickly and maximize your protections

## Decline Codes Related to Lost, Stolen, or Fraudulent Cards

When your debit card is declined due to security concerns, you may see one of the following decline codes. These codes are directly related to the protections described in this document.

### CODE 41 - Lost Card

Card was previously reported lost. Look up the debit card information to confirm - the card will have issue_reason = 'lost' or status will indicate lost.

1. Check if customer actually reported it: 'I see this card was reported lost. Did you report it lost?'

2. If customer says YES and found the card:
   - The old card CANNOT be reactivated once reported lost.
   - Check if replacement card was ordered by looking for another card in the response. If a replacement exists with status PENDING, guide through activation with the internal protocol.
   - If no replacement ordered, offer to help order one.

3. If customer says NO (they didn't report it):
   - SECURITY CONCERN: Someone else may have reported it.
   - Say: 'For your security, I need to verify some additional information.'
   - Ask security questions. If verified, this may indicate account compromise.
   - Review recent transaction history to check for suspicious activity.
   - Follow lost debit card protocol to help the customer.

### CODE 43 - Stolen Card (SECURITY SENSITIVE)

Card was reported stolen. This code requires EXTRA CAUTION.

1. DO NOT immediately offer to unfreeze or reactivate.

2. Verify customer identity using ENHANCED verification (not standard):
   - Full name, date of birth, AND
   - Last 4 of SSN, AND
   - Recent transaction verification - review transaction history and ask about 2-3 recent transactions

3. After enhanced verification, explain: 'This card was reported stolen. For security, stolen cards cannot be reactivated. I can order you a replacement card with a new number.' Follow protocol to order a new one.

4. If customer insists they never reported it stolen:
   - This is a MAJOR security flag. Someone may have access to their account.
   - Say: 'I understand this is frustrating. For your protection, I need to transfer you to our security team who can investigate this further.'
   - Use transfer_to_human_agents. Do NOT attempt to resolve this yourself.

### CODES NOT TO DISCLOSE (INTERNAL USE ONLY)

The following codes indicate fraud or security concerns. DO NOT tell the customer the specific code or reason:

- **CODE 04 - Capture Card (Pick Up)**: Say: 'I'm sorry, but I'm unable to process transactions on this card. For assistance, please visit a Rho-Bank branch with valid ID.' Use transfer_to_human_agents if customer presses for details.

- **CODE 07 - Pick Up Card (Special)**: Same response as Code 04.

- **CODE 34 - Suspected Fraud**: Say: 'There's a security matter affecting your card that requires in-person verification. Please visit a branch with your ID.' Do NOT attempt to clear or explain.

- **CODE 59 - Suspected Fraud**: Same as Code 34.

**NEVER** tell the customer their card has been flagged for fraud over the phone. This could tip off a fraudster who has stolen the card.

title: What To Do If You Notice an Unauthorized Debit Card Transaction
content:
Discovering an unfamiliar charge on your debit card can be alarming, but don't worry—Rho-Bank is here to help you every step of the way. Because debit card transactions draw directly from your checking account, we take these matters very seriously and will work quickly to investigate and resolve the issue.

## Time is Important

With debit card disputes, how quickly you report the issue can affect your liability. Federal law provides strong protections, but they depend on timely reporting:

- **Report within 2 business days**: Your maximum liability is just $50
- **Report within 60 days**: Your maximum liability is $500
- **Report after 60 days**: You may not be able to recover the funds

If you notice something suspicious on your account, please contact us right away. Don't wait to see if additional charges appear.

## Types of Issues We Can Help With

You should contact us if you experience any of the following:

- **Unauthorized Transactions**: Charges you didn't make or approve
- **ATM Problems**: Cash not dispensed, wrong amount dispensed, or deposits not credited
- **Duplicate Charges**: The same transaction appearing multiple times
- **Incorrect Amounts**: Being charged more than you expected
- **Subscription Issues**: Being charged for a subscription you already cancelled
- **Missing Purchases**: You paid for something but never received it

## What Happens When You File a Dispute

When you report an issue to us, here's what you can expect:

1. **We'll gather information**: Our representative will ask you questions about the transaction and what happened
2. **Provisional credit**: For many types of disputes, we'll temporarily credit your account while we investigate, so you're not out of pocket. Provisional credit is typically issued within 10 business days.
3. **Investigation**: We'll research the transaction, which may involve contacting the merchant or ATM network
4. **Resolution**: We'll notify you of our findings, typically within 10 to 45 business days depending on the complexity

## What You'll Need

To help us process your dispute quickly, please have the following ready:

- Your account information
- Details about the transaction (date, amount, merchant name if known)
- When you first noticed the issue
- Whether you still have your physical debit card
- Any communication you've had with the merchant

## How to Reach Us

To report an unauthorized transaction or file a dispute, contact us immediately:

- **Phone**: 1-800-RHO-BANK (available 24/7 for fraud reports)
- **Mobile App**: Use the "Report Issue" feature on any transaction
- **Online**: Visit rhobank.com/disputes

For suspected fraud, we recommend also filing a police report, especially for amounts over $500. This can help with the investigation and may be required for certain claims.

At Rho-Bank, protecting your money is our priority. Don't hesitate to reach out if something doesn't look right—we're here to help.

title: What to Know Before Closing Your Account
content:
We're sorry to hear you're considering closing your Rho-Bank account, and we want to make sure you have all the information you need to make the best decision for your financial situation. While we'd love to keep you as a valued customer, we understand that circumstances change, and we're here to help make the process as smooth and straightforward as possible if you do decide to move forward with closing your account.

Before you proceed, here are some important things you should know:

## Early Closure Fees

Depending on your account type and when you opened it, there may be an early closure fee if you close your account within a certain timeframe after opening. These fees vary by account tier and are designed to offset the administrative costs of account setup. Entry-level accounts typically have lower fees and shorter early closure windows, while premium and elite accounts may have higher fees and longer windows. If you're unsure whether an early closure fee applies to your account, our customer service team can look up your specific account details and let you know exactly what to expect.

## Notice Periods

Some account types require advance notice before closure can be processed. This notice period gives us time to ensure all pending transactions have cleared and that your account is in good standing for closure. The required notice period varies by account tier, ranging from same-day processing for basic accounts to several weeks for premium business accounts.

## Pre-Closure Requirements

Before we can close your account, a few conditions must be met. Your account balance must either be zero or, if an early closure fee applies, must be at least equal to the fee amount (since the fee is deducted directly from your balance). Additionally, there cannot be any pending transactions on the account, as these need to clear first. For business savings accounts, you'll also need to ensure any linked accounts are properly addressed.

## Contact Us

If you have any questions about the closure process or would like to discuss your options, please don't hesitate to reach out to our customer service team at 1-800-RHO-BANK. We're here to help guide you through every step, and who knows—we might even be able to find a solution that makes staying with Rho-Bank the right choice for you.

title: After the Free Period: What Changes and What Stays
content:
## What Changes
- The monthly maintenance fee becomes $25.00.
- Billing updates automatically; you will see the fee on your next statement after the transition.

## What Stays the Same
- Interest continues to accrue at 1.25% according to your account terms.
- Your existing users, cards, controls, and analytics remain active without interruption.

## About Eligibility Post-Onboarding
- Eligibility criteria tied to company age are evaluated when you open the account; the threshold is 4.
- Your account status after the free period does not require re-checking this onboarding criterion.

title: API Rate Limits and Technical Specifications
content:
## Monthly call allocation
- Your integration is allocated $100,000 API calls per month. Track consumption and design polling strategies that respect this limit.

## Real-time behavior
- Balance updates are Yes. Favor event-driven or on-change sync patterns to reduce unnecessary calls while maintaining current data.

## Design recommendations
- Cache reference data and only request deltas for transactions and balances.
- Implement exponential backoff and idempotency for retried calls to prevent duplication and wasted consumption.
- Monitor error rates and throttle proactively if you approach your monthly call allocation.

## Observability
- Instrument your integration with counters for successful calls, error responses, and retries so you can tune usage before limits are reached.

title: Beige Account: Enterprise ATM Benefits
content:
## What’s included with Enterprise ATM access

- No Rho out-of-network ATM fee: You pay $0.00 per out-of-network ATM transaction.
- Monthly rebates for ATM operator surcharges: Receive up to $50 in rebates each month for fees charged by out-of-network ATM operators.
- International ATM withdrawals: A Rho fee of 1% of the withdrawal amount applies at foreign ATMs, with a minimum charge of $2.00 per withdrawal.

## Fee summary

| Scenario | Rho fee | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Out-of-network ATM usage (Rho fee) | $0.00 per transaction | ATM operators may charge their own surcharges. |
| Rebates for out-of-network ATM operator surcharges | Up to $50 per month | Rebates are capped monthly; once reached, additional surcharges are your responsibility. |
| Foreign (international) ATM withdrawal | 1% of withdrawal amount, minimum $2.00 | This Rho fee may apply in addition to any ATM operator surcharge. |

## Examples

- Domestic, out-of-network ATM
  - You withdraw $200 and the ATM operator charges a $3 surcharge.
  - Rho out-of-network fee: $0.00.
  - The $3 operator surcharge counts toward your monthly rebate limit of $50.

- Foreign ATM withdrawal
  - You withdraw $400 abroad.
  - Rho foreign ATM fee: 1% of $400 = $4.00 (meets the $2.00 minimum).
  - If the ATM operator adds its own surcharge, it may count toward your monthly rebate limit of $50 for out-of-network usage.

## How to maximize your benefits

- Consolidate cash needs into fewer withdrawals to reduce multiple operator surcharges.
- Track your cumulative ATM operator-fee rebates against the $50 monthly cap.
- When traveling internationally, factor in the 1% (minimum $2.00) fee on each withdrawal.

## FAQs

- Is there a Rho fee for using an out-of-network ATM?
  - No. The Rho fee is $0.00 per transaction. ATM operators may still charge their own surcharges.

- What happens after I reach the monthly rebate cap?
  - Once you reach $50 in rebated out-of-network ATM surcharges for the month, additional surcharges are not rebated.

- Do foreign ATM withdrawals have a separate fee?
  - Yes. Each foreign ATM withdrawal incurs a Rho fee of 1% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $2.00 per withdrawal.

title: Beige: Balance Aggregation and Sub-Account Management
content:
## Connecting external accounts for balance aggregation
- Link external accounts to centralize visibility and reporting across institutions.
- You can connect up to 30 external accounts; prioritize primary operating, payroll, and reserve accounts first.
- After linking, assign each external account to the appropriate entity or department for reporting clarity.

## Creating and organizing sub-accounts
- Create sub-accounts to tag activity by department, project, business unit, or cost center.
- You can create up to 50 sub-accounts. Use a consistent naming convention to streamline reconciliation and audit.

## Recommended setup practices
- Establish a standard taxonomy for sub-accounts that mirrors your internal reporting structure.
- Restrict permissions so teams can only post or view within their assigned sub-accounts.
- Use roll-up views to review consolidated balances while preserving departmental detail.

## Review and reconciliation
- Validate that aggregated balances match institution statements after initial setup.
- Schedule periodic reviews to retire unused sub-accounts and reassign external accounts if ownership changes.

title: Beige: Configuring Auto-Fund Sweeps
content:
## Sweep objective
- Maintain target liquidity in operating accounts and move excess or deficit funds automatically based on a defined threshold.

## Threshold configuration
- Set the trigger threshold to $50,000. When an account falls below or rises above this level, the sweep initiates a transfer according to your direction.

## Source and destination eligibility
- Choose from your connected accounts, with a maximum of 30 eligible external sources or targets for sweep rules.

## Rule design
- Define whether sweeps top up to a target amount or move only the variance from the threshold.
- Specify which accounts can provide funds and which accounts can receive excess balances.
- Determine the evaluation timing, such as end of day, to align with your reconciliation cycle.

## Safeguards and testing
- Enable a dry-run mode if available to preview proposed movements without execution.
- Review proposed sweeps against recent transactions to avoid unintended overdrafts or duplicate funding.
- After enabling, verify that actual movements match the rule logic and reconcile the next day’s opening balances.

title: Beige: Your Dedicated Account Management Team
content:
## Team structure
- You are assigned 2 dedicated account managers who coordinate onboarding, implementation, and ongoing optimization.

## How they support you
- Implementation planning: define milestones, success criteria, and required resources.
- Treasury workflows: configure entitlements, reporting, and automated movements to reflect policy.
- Issue resolution: triage incidents, coordinate internal teams, and track remediation to closure.
- Reviews: schedule periodic reviews to assess account performance and recommend improvements.

## Engagement best practices
- Establish a single point of contact on your side for efficient coordination.
- Share your treasury calendar so the team can prepare for peak activity periods.
- Document change requests and approvals to expedite configuration updates.

title: Claiming AWS Credits and Stripe Discounts
content:
## AWS Credits
- Value: $2,750.

### How to Claim
- Open your perks portal and locate the AWS benefit.
- Follow the redemption flow to associate your AWS account.
- Confirm credit application status in your perks portal after submission.

title: Cobalt Blue Account: ATM Fees and Out-of-Network Usage
content:
## Summary of ATM Fees

- Domestic out-of-network ATM withdrawal: $2.00 per transaction
- International ATM withdrawal: 2.5% of the withdrawal amount (minimum $4.00 per transaction)
- Monthly ATM fee rebates: Up to $15 in out-of-network ATM fee rebates per month

Note: ATM owners/operators may charge additional surcharges that you will see at the terminal before you complete the transaction.

## Domestic Out-of-Network ATM Usage

When you use an ATM outside the in-network system within the United States:
- You are charged $2.00 per withdrawal.
- Some ATM owners add their own surcharge. You can choose to cancel the transaction if you do not want to accept an operator surcharge.
- You can receive up to $15 in ATM fee rebates per month for out-of-network usage. These rebates can offset qualifying out-of-network ATM fees you incur, up to the monthly cap.

Tips to minimize domestic fees:
- Combine cash needs into fewer withdrawals to reduce per-transaction fees.
- Check the ATM’s on-screen disclosure for any operator surcharges before accepting.

## International ATM Withdrawals

For withdrawals at foreign (international) ATMs:
- You are charged 2.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $4.00 per transaction.
- Local ATM owners may add their own surcharges at the terminal.
- Exchange rates and any third-party conversion choices at the ATM can affect your total cost.

Examples:
- Withdraw $200: 2.5% of $200 = $5.00; the fee is $5.00 (exceeds the $4.00 minimum).
- Withdraw $100: 2.5% of $100 = $2.50; the $4.00 minimum applies, so the fee is $4.00.

## Monthly ATM Fee Rebates

- You can receive up to $15 in ATM fee rebates per month for out-of-network ATM usage.
- The rebate limit applies on a monthly basis; once you reach $15, additional out-of-network ATM fees will not be rebated that month.

Example:
- If you make two domestic out-of-network withdrawals, your bank-assessed fees would be 2 × $2.00. Up to $15 of qualifying out-of-network ATM fees may be rebated that month.

title: Cobalt Blue: No Overdraft Fees — How It Works
content:
## What you are charged when your balance goes negative
- Overdraft fee: $0.00

## How transactions are handled
- If a transaction would take your available balance below zero, it may be declined or returned
- You will not be charged an overdraft fee of any kind: the fee is $0.00

## Practical tips to avoid declines
- Enable low-balance alerts so you can transfer funds before authorizing new payments
- Review pending card authorizations that may temporarily reduce your available balance
- Schedule outgoing payments for dates when incoming funds are expected to arrive first

## If a payment is returned or declined
- Check your available balance and pending items
- Resubmit the payment after funds are available
- Consider splitting a large outgoing payment into multiple payments scheduled around expected deposits (when feasible for your payee)

## Monitoring tools to stay in control
- Use real-time balance and transaction alerts
- Review daily activity to ensure sufficient funds are available before initiating new payments

title: Cobalt Blue: Payments and Transfers Explained
content:
## ACH transfers
- Standard ACH transfers are available with no fee
- Same-day ACH transfers are available with a fee of $0.00
- Submission and approval cutoffs apply for same-day processing; initiate early to improve the likelihood of same-day delivery

## Domestic wire transfers
- Outgoing domestic wire transfer fee: $20.00
- Use wires when you need confirmed, rapid delivery to U.S. financial institutions

## Incoming wires
- You get 5 incoming domestic wire transfers per month at no charge
- Additional incoming domestic wires beyond this included amount may incur a fee

## Choosing the right method
- Prefer ACH for routine payments where timing is flexible
- Use same-day ACH when faster delivery is needed at the same-day ACH timetable
- Select domestic wires for time-sensitive or high-assurance payments

## Tracking and confirmations
- Review transfer status in your activity feed
- Save beneficiary details to reduce entry errors and speed up future payments

## Referral bonus requirements
If you're referring another business to Cobalt Blue, here are the qualification details:
- Qualifying deposit: The referred business must deposit at least $7,500 to trigger the referral bonus
- Time window: This deposit must be made within 90 days of account opening
- Your eligibility: To participate, your earliest-opened Rho-Bank checking account must have reached 60 days of age.

title: Configuring Team Permissions on World Blue
content:
## Plan your permission structure
- Map your roles across up to 4 tiers to align access with responsibilities
- Define which actions each tier can take, such as viewing balances, preparing payments, approving disbursements, or administering settings

## Create tiers and assign capabilities
- Name each tier to reflect its function and risk level
- Specify allowed actions for each tier, including access to accounts, payment creation, approval, and user management
- Set approval flows so sensitive actions require appropriate review before execution

## Add users and enforce least privilege
- Assign each team member to the lowest tier that enables their work
- Limit administrative access to a small group and separate payment preparation from approval across different tiers

## Ongoing governance
- Review tier memberships regularly and adjust when responsibilities change
- Use activity logs to confirm that approvals and changes align with your internal policy

title: Connecting Your ERP for Beige Account: API Integration Guide
content:
## Prerequisites
- Confirm that your ERP is among the 18 supported platforms or prepare a custom API integration plan.
- Provision service credentials and restrict them to least-privilege access needed for synchronization and automation.

## Connection steps
- From your ERP, configure the banking connector or custom integration module.
- Authorize data access and select the accounts and entities that should sync.
- Map internal chart of accounts and vendor master data to bank account identifiers for reconciliation.

## Real-time data behavior
- Balance information is updated in real time: Yes.
- Design downstream processes to rely on current balances for posting, reconciliation, and exception handling.

## Throughput planning
- Plan your synchronization cadence to remain within $100,000.
- Use incremental sync patterns and cache stable reference data to reduce unnecessary calls.

## Testing and validation
- Perform read-only tests for balances, transactions, and payee data.
- Execute a limited end-to-end payment test and confirm ERP status updates reflect bank confirmation.
- Monitor call consumption against your monthly limit and tune polling intervals accordingly.

## Ongoing maintenance
- Rotate credentials on a regular schedule and monitor error logs for authentication or mapping issues.

title: Hunter Green Account: ATM Fees and Out-of-Network Usage
content:
## Fee Summary

- Out-of-network (non‑Rho) ATM transactions (domestic): $2.00 per transaction
- International ATM withdrawals: 2.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $4.00 per withdrawal
- Monthly ATM fee rebates for out-of-network usage: Up to $20 per month

Note: ATM owner/operator surcharges are set by the ATM and are separate from Rho fees.

## How Fees Apply

### Domestic out-of-network usage
- Each cash withdrawal at a non‑Rho ATM incurs a $2.00 fee.
- Any surcharge displayed by the ATM owner/operator will be charged in addition to the Rho fee.
- Eligible out-of-network ATM fees are automatically rebated until you reach the monthly cap of $20.

### International ATM withdrawals
- Each withdrawal outside the U.S. incurs a fee of 2.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum charge of $4.00 per withdrawal.
- If the international withdrawal is made at a non‑Rho ATM, the $2.00 out-of-network fee also applies.
- ATM owner/operator surcharges at foreign ATMs may also be charged.
- Eligible out-of-network ATM fees are rebated up to the monthly maximum of $20.

## Calculating Your Total Cost

For each ATM withdrawal:
1. Add any ATM owner/operator surcharge shown at the terminal.
2. If the ATM is non‑Rho, add $2.00.
3. If the withdrawal is international, add 2.5% of the withdrawal amount (minimum $4.00).
4. Subtract any applicable rebates, up to a total of $20 per month.

## Examples

- Domestic, non‑Rho ATM, $100 withdrawal:
  - Rho out-of-network fee: $2.00
  - Plus any ATM owner surcharge
  - Rebated up to your remaining monthly $20 cap

- International ATM, $100 withdrawal:
  - International fee: 2.5% of $100 = $2.50, but minimum applies → $4.00
  - Out-of-network fee (if the ATM is non‑Rho): $2.00
  - Plus any ATM owner surcharge
  - Rebated up to your remaining monthly $20 cap

- International ATM, $400 withdrawal:
  - International fee: 2.5% of $400 = $10.00
  - Out-of-network fee (if the ATM is non‑Rho): $2.00
  - Plus any ATM owner surcharge
  - Rebated up to your remaining monthly $20 cap

## Posting and Visibility

- Fees and any related rebates appear as separate line items on your account activity and statements.
- You can track how much of the $20 monthly rebate you have used by reviewing your statement or recent transactions.

title: Hunter Green: Interest and APY Explained
content:

content:

title: Lime Green Account: ATM Access and Fee Rebates
content:
## ATM Access

- You can use out-of-network ATMs for cash withdrawals. Each out-of-network ATM transaction is assessed a fee of $1.00.
- International (foreign) ATM withdrawals incur a fee equal to 1.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $2.50 per withdrawal.

### Quick reference

| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Out-of-network ATM transaction fee (domestic) | $1.00 per transaction |
| Foreign ATM withdrawal fee | 1.5% of amount; minimum $2.50 |
| Monthly ATM fee rebate cap (out-of-network usage) | Up to $25 per month |

## ATM Fee Rebates

- Your account provides monthly rebates of eligible fees from out-of-network ATM usage, up to $25 per month.
- Rebates are applied until the monthly cap is reached; any eligible fees above the cap are your responsibility.
- You do not need to take additional action for eligible rebates—fees and corresponding credits will appear as separate line items on your account activity once processed.

### What counts toward the monthly cap

- Fees assessed for out-of-network ATM usage that are charged to your account are counted toward the $25 monthly rebate limit.

## How fees are calculated

- Domestic out-of-network usage: Each withdrawal is charged $1.00.
- Foreign ATM withdrawals: The fee is 1.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $2.50. If the percentage-based amount is less than $2.50, the $2.50 minimum applies.

## Examples

- Domestic, multiple withdrawals:
  - If you make several out-of-network withdrawals in a month, each is charged $1.00. Eligible out-of-network ATM fees will be rebated up to a total of $25 for that month.

- Foreign withdrawal:
  - For a single international withdrawal, your fee is calculated at 1.5% of the withdrawn amount, subject to a $2.50 minimum. The applicable amount is charged at the time the transaction is processed.

## Tips to reduce fees

- Consolidate cash needs into fewer withdrawals to limit per-transaction charges of $1.00.
- Monitor your monthly total of out-of-network ATM fees to understand how close you are to the $25 rebate cap.

title: Navy Blue Account: ACH Transfers and Processing Times
content:
## Standard ACH timing
- Standard ACH transfers typically complete in 2 business days, depending on the receiving institution and submission time.

## Same-day ACH
- If available on your profile, you can request same-day ACH for an added fee of $5.00.
- Same-day requests must be submitted before the same-day cutoff displayed in your transfer workflow.

## Submitting ACH transfers
- Verify recipient details carefully prior to submission to avoid delays.
- Choose standard or same-day speed based on your timing needs and the fee $5.00.
- Monitor transfer status in your activity view; processing steps will update as the item is accepted and settled.

## Tips to avoid delays
- Schedule transfers earlier in the business day when possible.
- Ensure sufficient available balance before submission.
- For recurring payments, set up automated schedules to align with expected settlement windows.

title: Navy Blue Account: ATM Fees and Out-of-Network Usage
content:
## Where ATM fees apply for Navy Blue
- Out-of-network usage: Any transaction at a non‑Rho ATM is assessed a per‑transaction fee of $2.50.
- International usage: ATM cash withdrawals at foreign (international) ATMs are assessed a fee of 3% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $5.00.
- Operator surcharges: The ATM owner may charge an additional surcharge at the terminal. This surcharge is separate from Rho fees.

## Fee schedule
| Scenario | Fee | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic out‑of‑network ATM transaction | $2.50 per transaction | Applies at non‑Rho ATMs in the U.S. |
| International ATM cash withdrawal | 3% of withdrawal amount (min. $5.00) | Applies at foreign ATMs outside the U.S. |
| Monthly rebates for out‑of‑network usage | Up to $10 per calendar month | Rebates are capped at the monthly limit. |

## How international ATM withdrawal fees are calculated
- Calculation: The fee equals the greater of:
  - 3% of the cash withdrawal amount, or
  - $5.00 (minimum)
- Threshold for the minimum to apply: The minimum applies when the withdrawal amount is less than or equal to $5.00 divided by 3%.

## When fees can combine
- If you use an international ATM that is also out‑of‑network, both the $2.50 out‑of‑network fee and the international withdrawal fee of 3% (minimum $5.00) can apply.
- ATM operator surcharges, if any, are in addition to the above.

## Monthly rebate details
- Rho rebates up to $10 in out‑of‑network ATM fees per calendar month.
- The monthly cap is the maximum total rebated; fees above this amount are your responsibility after the cap is reached.

## Tips to minimize ATM costs
- Use Rho ATMs whenever possible to avoid the out‑of‑network fee of $2.50.
- For international cash needs, consider making larger, less frequent withdrawals to reduce the impact of the $5.00 minimum relative to the withdrawal amount.
- Always review any on‑screen ATM operator surcharge disclosures before completing a transaction.

title: Navy Blue Account: Interest Explained
content:
## Your interest rate
- APY on your balance: 0.5%

## How interest is calculated
- Interest is compounded daily, which helps your balance grow as earned interest is added to your principal.

## Earning and visibility
- Interest begins accruing once funds are available in your account.
- You can track accrued and credited interest in your account activity and monthly statements.

## Rate changes
- The APY 0.5% may change at any time in accordance with your account terms and prevailing conditions.

## Tips to maximize earnings
- Keep funds on deposit to benefit from daily compounding.
- Monitor your balance and activity to ensure you maintain eligibility for interest accrual.

title: Navy Blue Account: Mobile Deposits and Daily Limits
content:
## Mobile check deposits
- Daily mobile deposit limit: $5,000
- Endorse checks according to the app instructions and submit clear images to avoid delays.

## Digital transfer limits
- Daily digital transfer limit: $25,000
- If your planned transfer exceeds $25,000, schedule it in multiple submissions across business days.

## ATM fee rebates
- Monthly cap on ATM fee rebates for out-of-network usage: 10
- Rebates are applied to qualifying ATM surcharge amounts up to the monthly cap.

## Best practices
- Deposit earlier in the business day to allow for review and availability under standard timelines.
- Retain original checks until you confirm the deposit has posted.
- Plan outgoing transfers with the daily ceiling of $25,000 in mind.

title: Navy Blue Account: Overdraft Protection and Fees
content:
## Overdraft options
You can choose whether transactions are covered when your available balance is not sufficient.

### If an overdraft occurs
- Overdraft fee per occurrence: $0.00
- The fee is assessed when a covered transaction settles into a negative available balance.

### Overdraft protection via linked account
- When enabled, funds can be transferred automatically from a linked eligible account to cover a shortfall.
- Transfer fee for overdraft protection: $0.00

### How to manage your settings
- In your online or mobile banking, navigate to account settings and choose your overdraft preference.
- If you enable protection, confirm the linked funding source and authorize automatic transfers.
- You can change your preference at any time before a transaction settles.

### When fees do not apply
- If a transaction is declined due to insufficient funds, no overdraft occurs and $0.00 does not apply.
- If overdraft protection fully covers the shortfall, only the protection transfer fee $0.00 may apply.

### Disputes and adjustments
- If you believe an overdraft was charged in error, contact support promptly with the transaction details.
- Reversals are evaluated case by case based on posting order and available balance at the time of settlement.

title: Navy Blue Account: Paper vs E-Statements
content:
## E-statements
- E-statements are delivered electronically at no cost and are accessible in your online banking portal.
- You can download, search, and archive statements securely without waiting for mail delivery.

## Paper statements
- Monthly paper statement fee: $0.00
- Paper delivery requires a valid mailing address and may take additional time to arrive.

## Managing your preference
- In your document settings, select e-statements to receive statements electronically.
- If you switch from paper to electronic, future statements will be delivered online; historical documents remain available based on your retention settings.

## Recommendations
- Choose e-statements to streamline recordkeeping and reduce mailed documents.
- If you require mailed copies for internal processes, enable paper statements and review the monthly fee $0.00.

title: Navy Blue Account: Wire Transfer Pricing
content:
## Domestic wire fees

### Outgoing domestic wires
- Fee per outgoing domestic wire: $15.00

### Incoming domestic wires
- Free incoming domestic wires each month: 5
- Additional incoming domestic wires beyond the monthly free allotment may incur a fee.

## How fees are applied
- Outgoing wire fees are assessed when you submit the transfer.
- Incoming wire credits post to your account; any applicable fee after the free allotment is assessed separately.

## Tips to manage costs
- For frequent receipts, track your monthly count to make full use of the 5 free incoming wires.
- For payments you send regularly, consider scheduling patterns that align with your cash flow to minimize multiple outgoing submissions.

title: Navy Blue Business Checking: Complete Guide
content:
## Key Account Costs and Limits

- Monthly maintenance fee: $0.00
- Annual Percentage Yield (APY) on balances: 0.5%
- Daily digital transfer limit: $25,000

### What you can expect day to day
- No minimum balance requirement, so you can use the account flexibly.
- Unlimited digital transfers within the daily limit of $25,000.
- Optional overdraft settings are available if you want coverage on eligible transactions.
- E-statements are included at no additional cost.

### Practical tips for managing your account
- Use e-statements to keep records organized and accessible.
- Plan larger transfers with the daily limit of $25,000 in mind; schedule across days if needed.
- Keep an eye on your balance to maximize earnings at 0.5%.

### Quick reference
| Item | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Monthly maintenance fee | $0.00 |
| APY | 0.5% |
| Daily digital transfer limit | $25,000 |

## Business Referral Program

Grow your network and earn rewards by referring other businesses to Navy Blue:

| Referral Detail | Value |

| Your referral bonus | $100 |
| New business welcome bonus | $75 |
| Maximum referrals per year | 10 |

To qualify: The referred business must open a Navy Blue account and deposit at least $5,000 within 90 days of account opening. Referrer eligibility requires having been a Rho-Bank checking customer for 60 days or more.

title: Paperless Banking: Why Hunter Green Requires E-Statements
content:
## Requirement and intent
- E-statements are required for this account: Yes. This ensures a fully digital document trail aligned with the account’s sustainability commitments.

## Benefits to you
- Faster access to statements and notices without waiting for delivery.
- Centralized, searchable archives that you can download or share securely.
- Reduced environmental impact through the elimination of paper mailings.

## Sustainability reporting cadence
- Your sustainability impact report is delivered monthly, and it is available alongside your statements in the digital documents center.

## Make sure you receive e-statements
- Confirm your email and notification preferences in your profile so you are alerted when new documents are posted.
- Whitelist bank sender addresses and check spam filters to avoid missing notices.
- Periodically download and store copies in your internal records if your policy requires offline retention.

title: Reading Your Sustainability Impact Report
content:
## When you receive it
- Your environmental impact report is delivered monthly.

## What to look for
- Carbon offsets: the report shows the portion of your monthly maintenance fees directed to verified carbon projects at 18.0%.
- Reforestation: tree planting is calculated at 3 trees for every $10,000 in average monthly balance.

## Estimating your metrics
- To approximate tree impact, divide your average monthly balance by $10,000 and multiply by 3.
- To understand offset funding, apply 18.0% to your monthly maintenance fee total shown on your statements.

## Access and exports
- Locate the latest report in your digital documents center alongside your statements.
- Save or export the report for ESG disclosures and stakeholder updates.

title: Security: Dual Authorization and Transaction Controls
content:
## Dual authorization threshold
- Dual authorization is required for transactions above $10,000. Configure approvers and backup approvers to align with your treasury policy.

## Control configuration
- Assign initiation, review, and approval roles so no single person can create and release a high-value transaction.
- Apply dual authorization to payment types that match your risk profile, including wires and high-value ACH where applicable.

## Real-time validation
- With real-time balance information set to Yes, approvers can confirm available funds at the moment of review.
- Use real-time checks to prevent releasing transactions that would otherwise result in insufficient funds.

## Monitoring and audit
- Review approval logs to confirm that required approvers participated before release.
- Periodically test controls by simulating requests above the threshold and verifying that approval gates function as intended.

title: Sending International Payments: SWIFT and SEPA Options
content:
## Choose the appropriate network
- Use SWIFT for global counterparties where cross-border transfers are needed
- Use SEPA when paying euro-denominated recipients within the SEPA zone

## Fees and included transfers
- Outgoing SWIFT wires are $15.00 each, with 3 free outgoing SWIFT wires included per month
- Outgoing SEPA wires are $5.00 each

## Prepare and submit your payment
- Confirm recipient details and ensure the destination supports the chosen network
- Select SWIFT or SEPA, review the network-specific fee, and verify you have remaining free SWIFT wires this month if applicable
- Submit the payment and retain the confirmation reference for reconciliation

## Tips to minimize costs
- Schedule higher-priority SWIFT payments first to utilize your included 3 free allotment
- When paying euro-area recipients, prefer SEPA to leverage the lower fee of $5.00

title: Sky Blue Account: ATM Fees and Out-of-Network Usage
content:
## Domestic out-of-network ATM usage (U.S.)

- When you withdraw cash at a non‑Rho (out‑of‑network) ATM in the U.S., you are charged an out‑of‑network ATM fee of $1.50 per transaction.
- ATM operators may impose their own surcharges at the terminal. These are separate from Rho’s fees and are disclosed on the ATM screen before you complete the withdrawal.

### Ways to minimize domestic fees
- Use in‑network ATMs when possible to avoid the out‑of‑network fee of $1.50.
- If you anticipate multiple withdrawals, consider taking out cash less frequently in larger amounts to reduce the number of per‑transaction fees.

## International ATM withdrawals

- For cash withdrawals at foreign (international) ATMs, you are charged 2% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum fee of $3.00 per withdrawal.
- Local ATM operators may assess additional surcharges. These are set by the operator and are separate from Rho’s fees.

### Practical tips for international use
- Because a minimum fee of $3.00 applies, fewer, larger withdrawals may reduce the effective fee rate on small cash needs.
- Review the ATM’s on‑screen disclosures before confirming a withdrawal to see any operator surcharges.

## Monthly ATM fee rebates for out-of-network usage

- You are eligible for up to 15 in ATM fee rebates per month for out‑of‑network ATM usage.
- The rebate cap is monthly. If total eligible fees for out‑of‑network usage exceed 15 in a given month, any amount above the cap is your responsibility.

## Common scenarios

- Two domestic out‑of‑network withdrawals in one month:
  - You are charged $1.50 per withdrawal. If the total eligible out‑of‑network fees for the month remain within 15, they are rebated up to that monthly cap.

- International withdrawal of a small amount:
  - The fee is 2% of the withdrawal, subject to the minimum of $3.00 if the calculated percentage is lower.

## If something looks off

- Keep your ATM receipt and check your statement. If a fee or rebate does not appear as expected, contact support and include the date, location, and amount of the withdrawal so we can review it.

title: Sky Blue: Burn-Rate Analytics and Runway Projections
content:
## Access and Inclusion
- Burn-rate analytics are included at no extra cost: Yes.

## Using the Dashboard
- Open the analytics section to review monthly spend, inflows, and net burn.
- Categorize transactions to improve accuracy and trend visibility.

## Runway Projections
- The runway tool forecasts up to 18 months based on your historical burn and current balance assumptions.
- Adjust categorization and filters to reflect one-time or planned changes and refine the projection.

## Best Practices
- Reconcile transactions frequently so burn and runway reflect current operations.
- Annotate material changes such as new hires or major vendor shifts to inform stakeholders.
- Export visuals for sharing during planning sessions or board updates.

title: Sky Blue: Preparing Investor Reports
content:
## Included Tools
- Investor-ready reporting tools are included: Yes.
- Burn-rate analytics are included and integrate with reporting: Yes.

## Building Your Investor Packet
- Choose the reporting period in your analytics or reporting section.
- Include key exhibits: cash position, operating inflows and outflows, burn summary, and runway overview.
- Add narrative context for material variances and one-time items.

## Workflow Tips
- Lock your dataset before exporting to prevent late changes.
- Attach notes explaining accounting treatments, vendor changes, or hiring plans.
- Export your packet and store a versioned copy for audit consistency.

title: Sky Blue: Understanding Your Free Period
content:
## What Your Free Period Covers
- Monthly maintenance fees are waived for 6 months.
- Your account features continue to function normally during this time.

## When It Begins and Ends
- The free period starts when your account is opened and runs for 6 months.
- You can track remaining time in your billing or plan details screen.

## What Happens After
- When the free period ends, the monthly maintenance fee becomes $25.00.
- No action is required to continue service; billing updates automatically.

## Managing Your Timeline
- Review your billing settings to confirm the free period end date.
- Set internal reminders to prepare for the transition to $25.00.

## Referral Program During Free Period
Even during your free period, you can participate in the referral program once eligible:
- Qualifying deposit: Referred startups must deposit at least $10,000 to trigger the bonus
- Time window: The deposit must be made within 90 days of account opening
- Your eligibility: Referrers must meet the 45-day tenure threshold based on their banking history with Rho-Bank.

title: Spending Alerts and Budget Controls on Hunter Green
content:
## Defaults at a glance
- Real-time spending alerts are enabled by default: Yes.
- The default alert threshold is $100. You will receive an alert when a single transaction meets or exceeds this amount.
- A spending limit buffer of $2,000 allows limited flexibility above your available balance for transactions.

## Customizing alerts
- Adjust your alert threshold above or below $100 to match your typical transaction sizes.
- Enable alerts for card-present, online, or international transactions based on your risk tolerance.
- Set delivery preferences for push or email notifications so you see alerts in time to act.

## Understanding the spending limit buffer
- Transactions within the $2,000 buffer may be approved even when your available balance is temporarily insufficient.
- Use alerts to immediately review and reconcile any buffered transactions so your balance aligns with expected activity.

## Best practices
- Calibrate the threshold near your average invoice or purchase size so meaningful alerts are surfaced.
- If you anticipate larger purchases, temporarily raise the threshold and then reset it after the transaction clears.

title: Troubleshooting: Beige Fee and Balance Issues
content:
## Quick Reference: Fees and Balance Thresholds

| Item | Amount | When it matters |
|---|---|---|
| Monthly maintenance fee | $200.00 | Charged if you do not meet the waiver balance threshold within the billing period |
| Fee waiver balance threshold | $500,000 | Balance required to waive the monthly maintenance fee |
| Minimum balance requirement | $250,000 | Balance required to maintain the enterprise-level treasury account |
| Domestic out-of-network ATM fee | $0.00 | Per transaction at non-Rho ATMs within the U.S. |
| Foreign ATM withdrawal fee | 1% of withdrawal amount, minimum $2.00 | Per withdrawal at international ATMs |

Note: The ATM section below addresses only fees charged by us.

---

## Troubleshooting an Unexpected Monthly Maintenance Fee

If you were charged $200.00 and expected a waiver:

1. Verify the waiver threshold was met
   - Confirm your Beige account balance met or exceeded $500,000 during the relevant billing period.
   - Do not confuse the waiver threshold with the minimum balance requirement; meeting $250,000 alone does not waive the fee.

2. Review fund movements that may have lowered your balance
   - Check liquidity rules, balance aggregation flows, and auto-fund movements that could have moved funds out of the account near period end.
   - Review API/ERP-triggered transfers and scheduled sweeps for the period.
   - Confirm dual authorization did not leave an inbound transfer pending at the time the balance was measured.

3. Reconcile timing
   - Compare timestamps of transfers with the date/time your period closed.
   - If transfers posted after the relevant cut-off, they would not count toward the waiver balance for that period.

4. Prevent recurrence
   - Maintain a buffer above $500,000 to account for intraday or end-of-day movements.
   - Adjust auto-movement rules or transfer windows so your Beige account remains above $500,000 when the balance is measured.
   - Ensure dual approvers are available to approve time-sensitive transfers.

5. Request a review if needed
   - If your records show the Beige account balance met $500,000 for the period and the fee still posted, provide the statement period, balance history, and transfer details for investigation.

---

## Troubleshooting Minimum Balance Issues

If your balance dipped below $250,000 unexpectedly:

- Review automated transfers and aggregation
  - Check whether liquidity tools or auto-fund rules moved funds out of the Beige account temporarily.
  - Confirm any planned replenishment transfers were executed and fully posted.

- Check approval status
  - Verify whether dual authorization delayed a transfer intended to keep the account above $250,000.

- Stabilize operationally
  - Increase the Beige account’s target buffer so routine sweeps and settlement variances do not push the balance below $250,000.
  - Where applicable, shift transfer timing to earlier in the day to reduce posting-risk.

---

## Troubleshooting ATM Fee Discrepancies

Use this section to reconcile Rho-Bank’s ATM fees shown on your statement.

1. Domestic out-of-network ATM transactions
   - Our fee per transaction: $0.00.
   - If you see any domestic out-of-network ATM fee from us other than $0.00, capture the transaction details and request a review.

2. Foreign (international) ATM withdrawals
   - Our fee is 1% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $2.00 per withdrawal.
   - Examples:
     - $400 withdrawal: 1% of $400 = $4.00; fee should be $4.00.
     - $150 withdrawal: 1% of $150 = $1.50, which is below the $2.00 minimum; fee should be $2.00.
   - If the fee you see seems off, confirm the USD amount used for the posted withdrawal and recalculate using 1% with the $2.00 floor.

---

## Common Root Causes and Fixes

- Balance just under waiver threshold
  - Cause: Routine sweeps or late postings left the Beige account slightly below $500,000.
  - Fix: Raise the account’s target buffer and adjust sweep timing so the balance is above $500,000 at measurement.

- Aggregation or auto-fund rules moved funds out unexpectedly
  - Cause: Liquidity rules executed earlier than expected or before the period closed.
  - Fix: Update rule timing or conditions; confirm posting order and dependencies.

- Pending transfers due to dual authorization
  - Cause: A required co-approval wasn’t completed in time.
  - Fix: Set up backup approvers and alerts to ensure time-sensitive movements complete before balance measurement.

- ATM fee mismatch
  - Cause: Misinterpreting which withdrawals are domestic vs. foreign, or not applying the minimum for foreign withdrawals.
  - Fix: Reconcile using $0.00 for domestic and 1% with the $2.00 floor for foreign transactions.

---

## What to Provide When Contacting Support

Prepare the following to expedite resolution:
- Statement period and date the fee posted
- Balance history for the period relative to $500,000 and $250,000
- Transfer logs (including API/ERP-initiated movements), rule configurations, and approval timestamps
- For ATM issues: transaction date, location (domestic or foreign), withdrawal amount in USD, and the fee posted relative to 1% and $2.00

title: Troubleshooting: Hunter Green Fees and Charges
content:
## Identify and Resolve Unexpected Charges

Use the sections below to diagnose why a fee posted, how it’s calculated, and what to do if you believe it’s incorrect.

---

## Monthly Maintenance Fee

You were charged $25.00 if the account did not meet the waiver criteria for the statement period.

Troubleshooting steps:
- Confirm your statement period and check the “Fees & Charges” section for the $25.00 line.
- Verify whether your balance met the waiver threshold of $5,000 for the period.
- If you recently moved funds near the end of the cycle, the timing of deposits/withdrawals may have affected the period-end balance used to determine the waiver.
- If you believe your balance qualified for the waiver but the fee posted, request a review and include your statement period and balance details for the period.

Tips to avoid future charges:
- Keep your balance at or above $5,000 throughout the statement period.
- Set internal alerts to notify you before the cycle ends if your balance is trending below $5,000.

---

## Domestic Wire Transfer Fee

An outgoing domestic wire triggers a fee of $15.00.

Troubleshooting steps:
- Review your transaction history for outgoing domestic wires on the date the fee posted.
- Check whether a team member initiated a wire or if a scheduled/recurring wire executed.
- If you see multiple fees for a single wire, compare timestamps and reference numbers; submit both entries for review if they appear duplicative.

How to avoid:
- Where possible, consider alternative transfer methods for domestic payments that do not incur the $15.00.

---

## ATM Fees

### Domestic out-of-network ATM usage
- Fee: $2.00 per transaction when using non‑Rho ATMs within the U.S.
- You may also see a separate surcharge from the ATM operator; this is set by the ATM owner.

Troubleshooting steps:
- Match the ATM withdrawal date/time and location to the fee posted.
- If you were using an in-network ATM but still see $2.00, provide the ATM address and receipt for review.

### Foreign (international) ATM withdrawals
- Fee: 2.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $4.00.
- In addition, the ATM operator may add its own surcharge.

How the fee is calculated:
- The fee is the greater of:
  - Withdrawal amount × 2.5%
  - $4.00

Examples:
- Withdrawing 80.00 in local currency equivalent: 80.00 × 2.5% = 2.00; minimum $4.00 applies, so the fee is $4.00.
- Withdrawing 400.00 in local currency equivalent: 400.00 × 2.5% = 10.00; since this exceeds $4.00, the fee is 10.00.

If a foreign ATM fee looks incorrect:
- Confirm the final settled withdrawal amount (in USD equivalent) used for the percentage calculation.
- Check for any separate ATM-operator surcharge on your receipt.
- If you see both a foreign ATM fee and a domestic out-of-network fee for the same international withdrawal, share the transaction details for review.

---

## Sustainability Allocations Within Fees

Some fees on Hunter Green include a carbon-offset allocation. If you see a note or line item indicating a contribution tied to a fee:
- This does not increase the total you pay beyond the published fee amounts (e.g., $25.00, $15.00, $2.00).
- If you believe a separate charge was added in error rather than noted as an allocation within an existing fee, share the statement line items for review.

---

## Quick Reference: Fees Discussed Here

| Charge type                          | Amount/Calculation |
|-------------------------------------|--------------------|
| Monthly maintenance fee             | $25.00 |
| Waiver threshold (per period)       | $5,000 |
| Domestic wire (outgoing)            | $15.00 |
| Domestic out-of-network ATM         | $2.00 per transaction |
| Foreign ATM withdrawal              | 2.5% (minimum $4.00) |

---

## Requesting a Fee Review

Provide the following to expedite resolution:
- Statement period and the specific fee(s) in question
- For maintenance fee reviews: balance evidence relative to $5,000 during the period
- For domestic wire fees: wire date, amount, and reference
- For ATM fees: date/time, location, receipt (showing any operator surcharge), and the withdrawal amount used to compute the fee

Submit this information through your usual Rho support channel.

title: Troubleshooting: My World Blue fee wasn't waived
content:
## Check what was charged
- Confirm the amount matches the monthly maintenance charge: $50.00.
- Note the statement period and the posting date of the charge on your statement.

## Why your fee may not have been waived
- Balance threshold not met: Your account balance did not meet or exceed $25,000 during the waiver evaluation period for the statement cycle.
- Timing of deposits/credits: Funds that were pending or posted after the cycle’s cutoff would not count toward meeting $25,000 for that cycle.
- Multi-currency effects: If you hold balances in multiple currencies, the USD-equivalent total at the time of evaluation may have fallen below $25,000 due to exchange-rate movements.
- Intra-period dips: If your balance dropped below $25,000 at any point during the evaluation period, the waiver may not have applied.
- Reversals/adjustments: Chargebacks, refunds, or other adjustments posted before the evaluation could have reduced your qualifying balance.

Note: If you cannot view fees or statements due to your permission level, ask an administrator to verify on your behalf.

## How to self-audit quickly
1. Identify the exact statement cycle in which the fee posted.
2. Review your posted transactions and end-of-day balances for that cycle.
3. If you hold multiple currencies, estimate the USD-equivalent totals for those dates and compare your total to $25,000.
4. Look for any day or cutoff moment when the total may have fallen below $25,000, or when key deposits posted after the cutoff.

## If you believe the waiver should have applied
- Gather:
  - The statement showing the $50.00 charge
  - Your balance history for the cycle
  - Any notes on the timing of deposits and currency holdings
- Contact support and request a review of the waiver for that cycle. Indicate that your balances met or exceeded $25,000 and include your supporting details.
- If a correction is warranted, we will reverse the $50.00 for that cycle.

## Tips to avoid this next cycle
- Maintain a buffer above $25,000 to account for FX fluctuations.
- Ensure deposits post before the cycle cutoff; avoid relying on pending credits.
- Monitor balances near the end of the cycle, especially if holding multiple currencies.

title: Troubleshooting: Sky Blue Eligibility Issues
content:

## When to Contact Support

- Include a screenshot of your plan details showing the expected 6 so support can reconcile your account configuration.

title: Troubleshooting: Unexpected Charges on Cobalt Blue
content:
## Identify the charge
- Review the exact transaction description on your statement or in your transaction history (e.g., “Monthly Maintenance Fee,” “Wire Transfer – Domestic,” “ATM Fee,” “International ATM Withdrawal Fee,” “Excess Transaction Fee”).
- Note the posting date, amount, and any memo or terminal/location details.
- Compare the posting date to your recent account activity (wires sent, ATM withdrawals, transaction volume, month-end).

## Common Cobalt Blue charges that may appear
| Line item on statement | Why it appears | How to confirm | Amount |
|---|---|---|---|
| Monthly Maintenance Fee | Your account did not meet the balance waiver requirement during the statement period. | Check the Fee Summary on your statement and verify whether your balance met $2,500 for the period. | $20.00 |
| Excess Transaction Fee | You exceeded your monthly free transaction limit. | Count your posted transactions for the statement period and compare to your free allowance; the fee line often notes the number of excess items. | $0.25 per excess transaction |
| Wire Transfer Fee – Domestic | You initiated a domestic wire transfer. | Confirm a wire was submitted on or near the posting date in your transfer history. | $20.00 |
| ATM Fee – Out of Network | You used a non‑Rho ATM in the U.S. | Match the fee date to an ATM withdrawal at a non‑Rho terminal. Note: the ATM owner may also assess a separate surcharge. | $2.00 per ATM transaction |
| International ATM Withdrawal Fee | You withdrew cash at a foreign (international) ATM. | Match the fee date/location to an overseas ATM withdrawal. | 2.5% of the withdrawal amount, minimum $4.00 |

Note: Cobalt Blue does not assess overdraft fees. If you see a charge that appears to be an overdraft fee, review the line’s exact description—it may correspond to one of the fees above. If still unclear, request a review.

## Troubleshooting by charge type

### Monthly Maintenance Fee posted unexpectedly
- Verify whether your account met the waiver balance requirement of $2,500 during the statement period.
- Check if the fee posted at or just after the statement cycle date.
- Compare your balance history for the full period; large debits near period‑end can affect waiver eligibility.
- If you believe you qualified for the waiver, request a fee review and include your statement period dates and balance evidence.

### Excess Transaction Fee you didn’t anticipate
- Add up your posted transactions for the statement period and compare to your plan’s free monthly transaction limit.
- Open the fee detail to see how many items were assessed at $0.25 each.
- If the count appears off, provide a list of the transactions you believe were miscounted when requesting a review.

### Wire Transfer Fee – Domestic you didn’t expect
- Confirm whether a domestic wire was sent on the fee date (or one business day prior).
- Distinguish wires from other transfers by the transaction description (“Wire,” routing via Fedwire, etc.).
- If you did not authorize a wire, report immediately for investigation. Otherwise, the standard fee is $20.00.

### ATM fees: domestic vs. international
- Out‑of‑network (domestic) ATM usage: Expect $2.00 per ATM transaction. This is separate from any surcharge by the ATM owner, which can appear as a separate line item.
- Foreign (international) ATM withdrawals: The fee is 2.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $4.00. Example: for a withdrawal, multiply the amount by 2.5%; if that result is less than $4.00, the minimum applies.

## Quick checklist before requesting a review
- Match the fee’s posting date to a specific action (wire, ATM withdrawal, month‑end, spike in transaction volume).
- Capture screenshots of:
  - The fee line item (showing date, amount, and description)
  - Any related transaction (wire confirmation, ATM withdrawal)
  - The relevant statement page (Fee Summary and balance history for the period)
- Briefly explain why the fee appears incorrect (e.g., you met $2,500 during the period, transaction count appears miscalculated, withdrawal was domestic vs. international, etc.).

## Requesting a review or dispute
- Contact us as soon as you notice the charge.
- Include: account name, last 4 digits, statement period, fee date/amount/description, and your supporting screenshots or documents.
- We will investigate and correct any misapplied fees. If additional information is needed (e.g., to verify ATM location or wire authorization), we will follow up.

title: True Blue Account: ATM Fees and Out-of-Network Usage
content:
## Fees at a glance

| Transaction type | Where it applies | Fee |
|---|---|---|
| Out-of-network ATM withdrawal | Domestic (U.S.) non-Rho ATMs | $1.00 per transaction |
| Foreign ATM cash withdrawal | International ATMs | 2% of the withdrawal amount; minimum $3.00 per withdrawal |
| Monthly ATM fee rebates (cap) | Out-of-network ATM usage | Up to $30 per month |

Note: ATM owners may assess their own surcharges on out-of-network transactions. Those surcharges are separate from Rho’s fees.

## Domestic out-of-network ATM usage (U.S.)

- When you use a non-Rho ATM domestically, you are charged $1.00 per transaction.
- The ATM owner may also charge a separate surcharge at the terminal; you can accept or cancel the transaction after the surcharge is disclosed.

Example:
- If you complete 5 out-of-network withdrawals in a month, Rho’s fees would total 5 × $1.00. Any ATM owner surcharges would be in addition to this amount.

## International ATM withdrawals

- For cash withdrawals at foreign (international) ATMs, the fee is 2% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $3.00 per withdrawal.
- The percentage is applied to each withdrawal; if the calculated amount is below $3.00, the minimum applies.

Examples:
- $100 withdrawal: 2% = $2.00, which is below the $3.00 minimum, so the fee is $3.00.
- $200 withdrawal: 2% = $4.00, so the fee is $4.00.

## Monthly ATM fee rebates

- You can receive up to $30 in ATM fee rebates per month for out-of-network ATM usage.
- The cap is applied on a monthly basis; once total eligible ATM fees reach $30 for the month, no additional ATM fee rebates are provided for that month.

Examples:
- If your monthly out-of-network ATM fees total $12, up to $12 may be rebated (within the $30 cap).
- If your monthly out-of-network ATM fees total $36, the maximum rebate is $30.

## Tips to manage costs

- Prefer Rho ATMs to avoid the $1.00 out-of-network fee.
- Abroad, consider fewer, larger withdrawals to reduce the impact of the $3.00 per-withdrawal minimum.
- Track your monthly ATM fees so you know when you are approaching the $30 cap.

title: True Blue Account: Fee Waivers and Balance Requirements
content:
## Waiving your monthly maintenance fee
- You will be charged $75.00 each month unless you meet the waiver threshold.
- The monthly maintenance fee is waived when your balance is at least $50,000 during the statement period.

## Minimum balance requirement vs. fee waiver threshold
- Minimum balance requirement: $25,000 to maintain the account and access premium features.
- Fee waiver threshold: $50,000 to waive the $75.00 monthly maintenance fee.
- Falling below $25,000 may limit access to premium features until your balance is restored.

## Overdrafts and shortfalls
- If your balance goes negative, a per-occurrence overdraft fee of $25.00 may apply.

## How to avoid fees
- Maintain a balance of at least $50,000 to waive the monthly maintenance fee.
- Set internal processes to monitor balances against both $25,000 and $50,000.
- If a shortfall is anticipated, transfer funds promptly to avoid the $25.00 and preserve eligibility for the fee waiver.

## True Blue Referral Program

Refer other high-volume businesses to True Blue and earn significant rewards:

| Benefit | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Referrer bonus | $350 per referral |
| New member bonus | $250 |
| Maximum referrals per year | 15 |
| Qualifying deposit | $50,000 |
| Deposit window | 120 days |
| Account age requirement | 90 days |

### Referral requirements
- The referred business must deposit $50,000 within 120 days of opening their True Blue account.
- Eligibility is based on how long you've been a Rho-Bank checking customer. The account age threshold is 90 days, calculated from when you opened your oldest checking account.
- Referral bonuses are paid after qualification is confirmed and are subject to the annual limit of 15.

title: True Blue Account: Interest and APY Explained
content:

## How interest accrues
- Interest compounds daily, which increases the amount of interest credited over time compared to less frequent compounding.

## Crediting
- Interest is calculated on collected balances and credited according to the account terms.

## Maximizing what you earn
- Keep funds in the account continuously so more of your balance benefits from daily compounding at 2.0%.
- Avoid unnecessary transfers out of the account if you aim to maximize compounding effects.
