title: Why am I being charged a fee on my Lime Green Account?
content:
## Common reasons you may see a fee

You may see one or more of the following fees on your Lime Green Account:

- Monthly maintenance fee if your balance does not meet the waiver threshold
- Fees for sending domestic or international wire transfers
- Fees for using non‑Rho ATMs, including percentage‑based fees for foreign ATM withdrawals

### Quick reference

| Fee type | When it’s charged | Amount |
|---|---|---|
| Monthly maintenance fee | Your balance does not meet the waiver threshold | $25.00 |
| Domestic wire transfer | You send a domestic wire | $10.00 |
| International wire transfer | You send an international wire to a foreign bank | $25.00 |
| Non‑Rho ATM (U.S.) | You withdraw cash at a non‑Rho ATM in the U.S. | $1.00 |
| Foreign ATM withdrawal | You withdraw cash at an international ATM | 1.5% of the withdrawal amount, minimum $2.50 |

## Monthly maintenance fee

- You are charged $25.00 when your account balance does not meet $15,000.
- To avoid this fee, maintain a balance at or above $15,000.

## Wire transfer fees

- Domestic wires: Each outgoing domestic wire you send incurs a fee of $10.00.
- International wires: Each outgoing international wire to a foreign bank incurs a fee of $25.00.

## ATM‑related fees

- Non‑Rho ATMs in the U.S.: You are charged $1.00 per withdrawal when using a non‑Rho ATM.
- Foreign (international) ATMs: You are charged 1.5% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum fee of $2.50.
  - How to estimate: Multiply your withdrawal amount by 1.5% and compare the result to $2.50. The higher number is the fee.

## How to confirm which fee was charged

1. Review your transaction history for a separate fee line item (for example, a description referencing maintenance, wire, or ATM).
2. Match the amount to one of the following:
   - $25.00
   - $10.00
   - $25.00
   - $1.00
   - For foreign ATM withdrawals, the greater of:
     - 1.5% of your withdrawal amount, or
     - $2.50

## Ways to avoid or reduce fees

- Maintain a balance of at least $15,000 to waive $25.00.
- Use Rho‑network ATMs to avoid the $1.00.
- Plan international cash needs to minimize the number of foreign ATM withdrawals subject to 1.5% (minimum $2.50).

## If you believe a fee is incorrect

- Gather details (date, amount, and description of the fee; related transaction, if any).
- Contact support with these details so we can review and correct any errors promptly.

title: Why am I being charged a fee on my Navy Blue Account?
content:
## Common reasons you may see a fee

You may see a fee on your Navy Blue Account when you use specific services that carry charges. Review the scenarios below to identify the likely source.

| Fee or charge | When you’re charged | Amount | How to avoid next time |
|---|---|---:|---|
| Domestic wire transfer | You initiate a domestic wire | $15.00 per transfer | Use ACH for domestic payments when timing allows |
| Same-day ACH | You choose same-day ACH speed at submission | $5.00 per transfer | Select standard ACH speed |
| Out-of-network ATM (U.S.) | You withdraw cash at a non-Rho ATM in the U.S. | $2.50 per transaction | Use Rho ATMs |
| Foreign ATM withdrawal (international) | You withdraw cash at an ATM outside the U.S. | 3% of the withdrawal, minimum $5.00 | Minimize withdrawal frequency and amount combinations that trigger the minimum |

### Foreign ATM fee examples
- If you withdraw $80 abroad: 3% of $80 = $2.40, but the minimum is $5.00, so the fee is $5.00.
- If you withdraw $200 abroad: 3% of $200 = $6.00, which is above the $5.00 minimum, so the fee is $6.00.

## Fees you will not see on Navy Blue
- Monthly maintenance fee: $0.00
- Paper statement fee: $0.00
- Standard ACH transfers do not incur a fee; only same-day ACH carries the $5.00 charge when you select that option.

## How to confirm what fee you were charged
1. Open the transaction details for the item in question and note the payment method (wire, ACH with same-day speed, ATM withdrawal).
2. Compare the method and context to the table above to match the fee and amount (including the percentage/minimum for foreign ATM withdrawals).
3. For ATM transactions, confirm whether the withdrawal occurred in the U.S. (out-of-network fee applies) or abroad (foreign ATM fee applies).

## How to avoid or minimize future fees
- Use standard ACH instead of same-day ACH when delivery speed is not critical.
- Prefer ACH transfers over domestic wires when appropriate.
- Withdraw cash at Rho ATMs in the U.S. to avoid the $2.50 fee.
- When traveling internationally, plan fewer, larger withdrawals to reduce the impact of the 3% fee and the $5.00 minimum. 
- Opt for e-statements; there is no paper statement fee ($0.00). 

## If the charge still looks incorrect
Gather the transaction date, amount, and method, then contact support with those details so we can review the specific fee and correct any discrepancy.

title: Why am I being charged a fee on my True Blue Account?
content:
## Common reasons you’re seeing a fee

Below are the most common fee types that can appear on a True Blue account and why they’re charged.

| Fee type | When you’ll see it | Amount posted |
|---|---|---|
| Monthly Maintenance Fee | Your qualifying balance did not meet the waiver threshold for the applicable evaluation period | $75.00 (waived when balance requirement of $50,000 is met) |
| Domestic Wire Transfer Fee | You sent a domestic wire from your account | $5.00 per outbound domestic wire |
| Out-of-Network ATM Fee | You completed a transaction at a non‑Rho ATM in the U.S. | $1.00 per transaction |
| Foreign ATM Withdrawal Fee | You withdrew cash at an international (non‑U.S.) ATM | 2% of the withdrawal amount; minimum $3.00 |

### Example for foreign ATM fee
- If you withdraw $200 at a foreign ATM, the fee is 2% of $200 ($4). Because $4 exceeds the minimum $3.00, $4 would post.

## How to confirm which fee was charged

1. Check your latest statement or transaction history for the fee line item and posted amount.  
2. Match the posted amount to the fees above:
   - $75.00 typically indicates the Monthly Maintenance Fee.
   - $5.00 typically indicates a Domestic Wire Transfer Fee.
   - $1.00 typically indicates an Out-of-Network ATM Fee.
   - A calculated amount equal to 2% of your ATM withdrawal (or $3.00, whichever is higher) indicates a Foreign ATM Withdrawal Fee.
3. For the Monthly Maintenance Fee, compare your balance during the billing period to the waiver threshold of $50,000.

## How to reduce or avoid future fees

- Maintain at least $50,000 to have the $75.00 waived.  
- Use Rho in‑network ATMs to avoid the $1.00.  
- When abroad, be aware that each cash withdrawal will incur 2% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $3.00.  
- Send domestic wires only when necessary to limit paying $5.00 per wire.

## Need help?

If you believe a fee was posted in error, gather the transaction date, amount, and any related activity (e.g., wire reference or ATM withdrawal details) and contact support for a review.

title: World Blue: Free International ATM Access
content:
## What you pay at foreign ATMs

- Rho foreign ATM withdrawal fee: 0% (minimum $0.00)
- Foreign ATM fee rebates: Up to $100 per calendar month for fees charged by foreign ATM operators on cash withdrawals

You will not be charged a Rho fee for international ATM withdrawals. If the foreign ATM operator adds its own fee, we rebate those fees automatically up to the monthly cap.

### Snapshot of foreign ATM costs
| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Rho foreign ATM withdrawal fee | 0% |
| Rho foreign ATM withdrawal fee minimum | $0.00 |
| Maximum monthly rebate for foreign ATM fees | $100 |

## Monthly rebate rules

- Coverage: Rebates apply to fees assessed by foreign ATM operators on cash withdrawals.
- Cap: We rebate up to a total of $100 in a calendar month. Fees above this amount will not be reimbursed for that month.
- Posting: Rebates are issued automatically after the withdrawal and associated fee post to your account. No enrollment or receipt submission is required.

Tip: Keep your ATM receipt when a foreign ATM displays or charges a fee; it’s useful for reference if you need to follow up.

## How to make a fee-free withdrawal abroad

1. Use your card at an international ATM that accepts it.
2. When prompted, select a withdrawal from checking.
3. If asked about currency choice, choose to be charged in the local currency rather than having the ATM convert the amount.
4. Complete your withdrawal. If the ATM charges a fee, it will typically display it before you confirm.

## Example

- You withdraw the equivalent of $200 at a foreign ATM.
- Rho foreign ATM withdrawal fee: 0% with a $0.00 minimum, so you pay $0 to Rho.
- The ATM operator charges a $5 fee. We automatically rebate $5, counting toward your $100 monthly cap.

title: World Blue: How to Avoid Monthly Fees
content:
## Know your thresholds
- The monthly maintenance fee is $50.00
- You can avoid the fee by maintaining an eligible balance at or above $25,000
- Always keep your total balance at or above $10,000 to maintain account status

## Practical tactics to stay above the waiver threshold
- Maintain a prudent buffer over $25,000 to accommodate FX movements and timing of payments
- Consolidate excess funds into the account ahead of major disbursements so you remain above $25,000 throughout the cycle
- Schedule recurring funding to top up balances before expected outflows
- Monitor balances in real time and set internal alerts that nudge you before you drop below $25,000

## Avoid avoidable charges
- Prevent overdrafts; an overdraft triggers a fee of $30.00 when the account goes negative
- Reconcile pending items and expected settlements so you do not rely on funds that are not yet available toward $25,000

## Verify and follow up
- At the end of each statement period, confirm whether you met $25,000
- If you believe the fee was assessed in error, document your balance history and contact support from within your dashboard

title: World Blue: Traveling Abroad with Your Business Account
content:
## Using Your Account Abroad: Cash, Payments, and Controls

Traveling internationally with World Blue is straightforward. The following guidance covers how to access cash, move money, and manage team permissions while you’re on the road.

### Accessing Cash at Foreign ATMs

- You can withdraw local currency abroad without a foreign ATM fee from us.
- Our foreign ATM withdrawal fee is 0% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $0.00.

ATM fee policy summary:
- Rho foreign ATM fee: 0% (minimum $0.00)
- Rebate of ATM operator surcharges: Up to $100 per month

Best practices when withdrawing:
- Choose to be charged in the local currency and decline “dynamic currency conversion” on the ATM to access low‑margin FX rates.
- Prefer bank‑branded ATMs; avoid independent kiosks in tourist areas when possible.
- Keep ATM receipts until your statement closes in case you need to reference fees.
- If multiple employees are traveling, consider setting withdrawal limits and time‑of‑day windows via role permissions.

### Sending and Receiving Payments While Abroad

- You can send and receive cross‑border payments in $140 countries/regions via SWIFT and, where eligible, SEPA.
- Use SEPA for euro‑area beneficiaries when available; use SWIFT for other destinations and currencies.

To send an international payment on the road:
1. Add or select the beneficiary.
2. Choose the payment network (SEPA where eligible, otherwise SWIFT).
3. Select the funding currency from your multi‑currency balances or convert before sending.
4. Enter amount and review the overview screen for currency and network details.
5. Submit and monitor delivery status in your activity feed.

Receiving payments while abroad:
- Share the correct currency‑specific receiving details from your account profile with overseas payers.
- If a payer requests network preference, provide SEPA details for euro payments within the SEPA area; otherwise provide SWIFT details.

### Depositing Funds While Traveling

- Mobile check deposit is available up to $20,000 per day.
- Tips for smooth deposits while abroad:
  - Use clear, well‑lit images on a flat, dark background.
  - Endorse checks as required and include any instructed phrases in the endorsement.
  - Retain the physical check securely until the deposit is confirmed.

### Managing Team Access and Approvals on the Road

Use tiered permissions to keep control while enabling travel flexibility:
- Create a “Travel” role with only the capabilities your travelers need (e.g., ATM withdrawals, card spend, or initiating—but not approving—international wires).
- Set per‑user spend limits and require approvals for high‑value SWIFT payments.
- Restrict who can add new international beneficiaries; require a second approver before first‑time payments.

### Pre‑Trip Checklist

- Confirm each traveler’s ATM PIN and test a small withdrawal before departure.
- Set or review withdrawal and spend limits appropriate for the trip’s costs.
- Pre‑add frequent international beneficiaries and verify their details.
- Ensure the right approvers will be available across time zones for urgent payments.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: How to Apply and Get Started
content:
## Check Eligibility

- If your business has established credit, a Dun & Bradstreet PAYDEX score of 20 or higher is helpful; it is not required for new businesses.

## What You Get Upon Approval
- Earn 1.0% cash back on eligible purchases.
- Annual fee: $0.00.
- Variable APR on carried balances: 20.49%.
- Your starting credit line will be at least $10,000.

## How to Apply
1. Complete the online application with your legal name, address, and Social Security number.
2. Provide basic business details such as legal business name, EIN (if available), entity type, and ownership information.
3. Consent to a personal credit check to evaluate your application against the minimum of $660.
4. If your business has a PAYDEX score, include it to help underwriting; a minimum of 20 is used when applicable.
5. Review and submit your application.

## After Approval: Get Set Up
- Activate your card as soon as it arrives.
- Add your card to your preferred digital wallet and configure card controls, alerts, and spending categories.
- Update your preferred payment method and schedule.

## Unlock the New Customer Promo
- Spend $10,000 in the first 2 months to receive a $500 statement credit.
- Track your progress in your account. Returns and credits reduce your qualifying spend.
- Plan larger purchases early so they count toward the $10,000 requirement within 2 months.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: Making Payments and Managing Your Balance
content:
## Interest on Carried Balances
- If you do not pay your statement balance in full by the due date, interest accrues at a variable rate of 20.49%.

## Payment Methods and Scheduling
- Set up recurring payments to automatically pay either your statement balance or a chosen fixed amount each cycle.
- You can also make one-time payments from your linked bank account at any time.

## Avoiding Fees and Managing Cash Flow
- Pay at least your minimum due by the due date on your statement to avoid a late fee of $32.50.
- Consider paying more frequently to reduce average daily balance and interest charges when carrying a balance at 20.49%.
- Monitor pending transactions and authorizations so you know what will post before your payment clears.

## If a Payment Is Late
- A late payment fee of $32.50 may be assessed, and interest continues to accrue at 20.49% on unpaid balances.
- Make the past‑due payment as soon as possible to minimize additional interest.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: New Customer Promo Offer Details
content:
## Promotion Window
- This offer is available from 2025-10-01 through 2026-03-31.

## How to Qualify
- Make net new purchases totaling $10,000 within the first 2 months of account opening.
- After qualification, a $500 statement credit will be applied to your account.

## What Counts Toward the Threshold
- Only net purchases count; returns or credits reduce your qualifying total.
- Transactions must post to your account within 2 months to be included.

## Tracking and Fulfillment
- Track your progress toward the $10,000 requirement in your account dashboard.
- The $500 will appear on a future statement after qualification is confirmed.

## Tips for Success
- Plan larger purchases early in the period so they post within 2 months.
- Keep documentation of purchases in case a transaction posts later than expected.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: No Annual Fee - What Does This Mean?
content:
## What "No Annual Fee" Means for You
- You are not charged an annual membership fee; the annual fee is $0.00.
- You maintain full access to the card’s features without paying a yearly cost to keep the account open.

## Practical Implications
- All of your costs are tied to how you use the card, rather than a recurring membership charge.
- You can keep the account open for ongoing business spending without planning for an annual renewal bill.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: Referral Program - Earn Rewards for Referring Businesses
content:
## What You Can Earn
- Receive $75 for each successful referral.
- You can earn up to 7 referral bonuses per calendar year.

## What Makes a Referral Successful
- The referred business must be approved for the card.
- The referred business must complete at least $1,750 in net purchases within 90 days of account opening.

## How to Refer
1. Share your referral link or code with another business.
2. Ensure they apply using your link or enter your code during their application.
3. You will be notified when the referral is approved and has met the $1,750 requirement within 90 days.

## Tracking Your Bonuses
- Monitor referral status, qualifying spend progress, and paid bonuses in your account dashboard.
- If a referred account is closed or reversed, any unpaid referral bonus may be canceled.

## Best Practices
- Encourage referrals to complete their initial purchases early to meet the $1,750 requirement within 90 days.
- Remind referrals to use your link or code so your bonus is tracked correctly.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: Travel Benefits and Purchase Protection
content:
## Using Your Card Internationally
- Purchases made outside the United States or with non‑USD merchants incur a foreign transaction fee of 2.75% on the converted amount.
- Some merchants abroad may process transactions in U.S. dollars; confirm the currency at checkout to understand whether the 2.75% will apply.

## Best Practices While Traveling
- Inform your team of expected travel dates and merchants to reduce the chance of declines.
- Keep contact details up to date so you can quickly verify unusual activity while abroad.

## Purchase Protection and Disputes
- Retain receipts and confirmations for travel and lodging. If goods are not delivered or services are not provided as agreed, you can initiate a dispute.
- Start disputes promptly and provide documentation (invoices, correspondence, cancellation confirmations) to support your claim.
- If a transaction is refunded, any associated rewards will be reversed accordingly.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: Understanding Your 1% Cash Back Rewards
content:
## Earning Cash Back
- You earn 1.0% cash back on eligible purchases charged to your account.
- Rewards are calculated on the net transaction amount after any credits or returns.

## Tracking Rewards
- Your rewards balance updates as purchases post to your account.
- You can monitor earned and available rewards in your account dashboard.

## Redeeming Rewards
- You can redeem once your available rewards balance is at least $37.
- Redemptions are applied to your account, typically as a statement credit.

## Tips to Maximize Rewards
- Consolidate everyday business spending on your card to grow rewards faster.
- Avoid returns or cancellations that reduce your net eligible spend and earned rewards.

title: Business Bronze Rewards Card: When and How Are Cash Back Rewards Paid?
content:
## When Rewards Are Earned
- You earn cash back at the rate of 1.0% on eligible purchases when transactions post to your account.
- If a purchase is returned or credited, the associated rewards are reversed.

## How Rewards Are Paid
- Once your available rewards balance reaches $37, you can request redemption.
- Redemptions are applied to your card account, typically as a statement credit.

## Best Practices
- Schedule periodic redemptions once you meet the $37 threshold.
- Keep receipts and invoices for any disputed charges; adjustments can affect your rewards balance.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Adding Authorized Users with Lounge Access
content:
## Authorized User Cardholder Setup
- You can add up to 37 employee cards to your account.
- Each authorized user receives their own card linked to the same account for simplified expense management.

## Lounge Access for Authorized Users
- Authorized users may access participating lounges subject to the same entry requirements as the primary cardholder.
- Lounge visits used by authorized users count toward the account’s total of 9 complimentary visits.

## How to Add Authorized Users
- From your account, navigate to Card Management and select Add Employee Card.
- Input the authorized user’s legal name and mailing details, then submit.
- Set individualized spending controls and category restrictions if desired.

## Best Practices
- Communicate lounge entry expectations to authorized users, including documentation they must present at entry.
- Monitor remaining complimentary visits so your team plans usage within the 9 allowance.
- Revoke or replace cards promptly when roles change to maintain security and benefit control.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Annual Fee and Premium Benefits Guide
content:
## Annual Fee
- The annual fee for the Business Gold Rewards Card is $200.00. It bills to your primary account each anniversary.

## Premium Lounge Access
- Your account includes 9 complimentary lounge visits per account year across participating networks.

## Travel Credits
- You receive annual travel credits totaling $200.
- Apply credits to eligible travel purchases by paying with your card; credits post automatically after eligibility checks.

## International Purchases
- Foreign transaction fee: 0%. Use your card abroad without added currency conversion fees from this charge.

## Concierge Services
- Concierge service included: Yes.
- Access assistance for travel planning, dining arrangements, and time-sensitive business needs through your account’s concierge channel.

## Managing Costs and Benefits
- Review your statement after the anniversary to confirm the $200.00 charge and the replenishment of 9 visits and $200 credits.
- Set alerts for large travel purchases to verify that credits post as expected.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Earning 2.5% Cash Back on Operations Spending
content:
## How Earnings Work
- Eligible operations purchases earn cash back at 2.5%.
- Purchases that do not qualify as operations spend earn cash back at 1.0%.

## Posting of Rewards
- Cash back is calculated based on the merchant category code assigned by the merchant’s payment processor.
- Rewards typically appear after the transaction posts and clears. Returns or credits reduce earned cash back in the same statement period where possible.

## Ensuring You Get the Operations Rate
- Use merchants that are consistently categorized for operations needs, such as vendors for software, utilities, logistics, or business services.
- For recurring bills, confirm the merchant category remains aligned with operations functions to retain the 2.5% rate.

## Disputes and Reclassification Requests
- If a purchase you believe is operations-related does not earn at 2.5%, contact support with the transaction details and merchant information for review.

## Best Practices
- Centralize routine operational expenses on the card to maximize the 2.5% rate.
- Keep receipts and invoices that clearly reflect the operational nature of the purchase to support any category review.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: How to Access Airport Lounges Worldwide
content:
## Your Lounge Access
- You receive 9 complimentary lounge visits per account year.
- Access is provided across 3 participating lounge networks.

## Getting Started
- Activate your lounge benefit in your account dashboard.
- Review participating lounges and entry requirements before you travel.

## At the Lounge Door
- Present your Business Gold Rewards Card and a valid boarding pass matching the name on the card account.
- Some lounges may request a government-issued ID that matches your boarding pass and card name.

## Tracking Your Visits
- Your remaining complimentary visits update in your account after each lounge check-in.
- If you exhaust the 9 allocation, standard lounge entry fees or restrictions may apply at the lounge’s discretion.

## Troubleshooting Access
- If your card is not recognized, ask the lounge to attempt manual validation and then contact support from your account.
- Carry a digital and physical card where possible to ensure successful scanning at different lounges.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Lounge Access Network and Participating Airports
content:
## Network Coverage
- Your benefit includes access to 3 lounge networks.
- You receive 9 complimentary visits per account year, usable across participating lounges.

## Finding Participating Lounges
- Use the lounge locator in your account to search by airport code or city.
- Filter by amenities relevant to your trip, such as quiet zones or workspace availability.

## Entry Notes
- Present the card, same-day boarding pass, and any identification requested by the lounge.
- Operating hours, capacity controls, and guest policies vary by lounge and are subject to change by the lounge operator.

## Tips for Smooth Entry
- Check lounge capacity status before arriving at the airport when available.
- Keep your account profile name consistent with your travel documents to avoid entry mismatches.
- If a lounge is unavailable, search nearby terminals or partner lounges within the same network.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Maximizing Your Operations Rewards
content:
## Align Spend to Operations Categories
- Route recurring operational expenses to your card to earn 2.5%.
- Confirm vendors’ merchant category codes align with operations-related categories; request a review if a vendor appears misclassified.

## Use Employee Cards Strategically
- Issue up to 37 employee cards to capture decentralized operational purchases under the same rewards structure.
- Set category and merchant-level controls to keep spending aligned with operations.

## Maintain Earning on Non-Operations Purchases
- When operations coding is not applicable, you still earn 1.0%.
- Separate discretionary or non-operational expenses to maintain visibility and simplify audits.

## Optimize Workflows
- Centralize billing for software and utilities under a single account to standardize coding and reduce missed rewards.
- Review statements for returns or credits that may adjust previously earned cash back, and verify corrections.
- Keep documentation for high-value operational purchases in case you need to substantiate category eligibility.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Premium Business Credit Card Overview
content:
## Eligibility
- Minimum personal credit score required: $735
- Minimum business credit score required on the PAYDEX scale: 67
- The card is designed for applicants with excellent personal credit and an established business credit history.

## Rewards Structure
- Operations spending earns cash back at 2.5%.
- All other purchases earn cash back at 1.0%.

## Pricing
- Annual fee: $200.00
- APR for carried balances: 17.99%

## Credit Limits
- Initial credit limits start at $37,500 and may reach up to $225,000, subject to credit approval and ongoing account review.

## Application Tips
- Ensure your business credit profile is up to date to meet the 67 requirement.
- Verify your personal credit report is accurate and at or above $735.
- Prepare recent financial statements to support a credit limit request within the $37,500 to $225,000 range.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Referral Program - Earn Rewards for Referring Businesses
content:
## How the Referral Bonus Works
- Earn $200 for each successful referral who is approved and meets the qualifying spend.
- You can earn up to 8 referral bonuses per calendar year.

## Qualifying Spend Requirement
- The referred business must spend $7,500 within 90 from account opening to qualify your bonus.

## Program Timeline
- The referral program accepts new referrals until 2025-09-15. Referrals submitted after this date are not eligible.

## How to Refer
- Generate your unique referral link from your account’s referral center.
- Share the link directly with businesses that consent to receive it.
- Track application status and eligibility in the referral dashboard.

## Payouts
- Once the referred account is approved and completes $7,500 within 90, your $200 is queued for issuance to your account.

## Rules and Best Practices
- Self-referrals are not permitted.
- Share referral invitations only with contacts who have given permission to receive them.
- If a referral applies without your link, the referral may not be attributed.
- Program terms, eligible industries, and promotional availability may change without notice prior to 2025-09-15.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: Travel Perks and Concierge Services
content:
## Travel Credits
- Your account provides annual credits totaling $200.
- Use your card for eligible travel bookings; credits apply automatically once the charge is identified as eligible.

## Concierge Services
- Concierge included: Yes.
- Request support for itinerary planning, complex multi-stop trips, dining reservations, and urgent changes. Initiate requests from your account’s concierge portal.

## International Advantage
- Foreign transaction fee: 0%. This enables international purchases without added foreign transaction charges from this fee.

## Tips to Maximize Value
- Verify a travel purchase qualifies for credits before booking.

- Keep confirmation emails accessible in case the concierge or credits team requests verification.

title: Business Gold Rewards Card: What Qualifies as Operations Spend?
content:
## Qualifying Categories
Transactions commonly recognized as operations spend and eligible for 2.5% may include:
- Business utilities and telecommunications
- Cloud services and software subscriptions used for day-to-day operations
- Shipping, postage, and logistics providers
- Office supplies and routine maintenance services for business premises
- Professional services that support ongoing operations, such as bookkeeping or payroll administration

## How Classification Works
- Merchant category codes are assigned by the merchant’s payment processor. Your rate depends on how the merchant is coded, not the product or service description alone.

## Non-Qualifying Examples
- Cash-like transactions
- Peer-to-peer money transfers
- Debt repayments
- Transactions categorized as entertainment, personal, or non-business use

## Tips to Maintain Eligibility
- Confirm that key vendors are categorized consistently for business operations.
- Use dedicated vendors for recurring operational needs to reduce miscoding risk.
- If a transaction is misclassified, request a review and provide documentation that demonstrates its operational purpose.

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Business Travel Itinerary Management
content:
## Submitting Itineraries
- Share planned trips with the concierge team to centralize bookings and confirmations.
- Provide traveler profiles and preferences so itineraries reflect policy and comfort standards.

## Updates and Changes
- Send modification requests referencing your booking locator to adjust dates, routes, or room types.
- Ask the team to synchronize changes across flights, hotels, and ground transport.

- Request proactive monitoring on critical segments and alternative routing when disruptions occur.
- Keep digital copies of confirmations for quick reissue if needed.

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Dedicated Account Support and Priority Service
content:

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Earning 4% Cash Back
content:

- Cash equivalents, balance transfers, and fees do not earn cash back.

## Best Practices
- Confirm that your vendors process payments under the appropriate merchant category.
- Keep invoices and receipts for category review if you believe a purchase should qualify for 4.0%.

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: How to Use the Concierge Travel Desk
content:

- Share booking references for modifications or disruptions so the concierge can rebook promptly.

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Referral Program - Earn Rewards for Referring Businesses

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Software and Cloud Services - Qualifying Purchases
content:
## Qualifying Software and Cloud Purchases
- SaaS subscriptions and enterprise software licenses billed by software providers.
- Cloud infrastructure, platform, and storage services billed by cloud vendors.
- Developer tools, analytics platforms, and productivity suites billed under software or cloud services categories.

- When merchants classify transactions as software or cloud services, you earn 4.0%.
- If a purchase does not fall under those categories, it earns 1.5%.

## Common Exclusions
- Hardware, peripherals, or on-premise equipment billed as physical goods.
- Professional services, implementation, or consulting billed under services categories.
- Telecom or internet access charges not categorized as software or cloud.

## Tips for Proper Categorization
- Purchase directly from software or cloud vendors rather than general resellers when possible.
- Confirm merchant category coding for large renewals to ensure the enhanced rate of 4.0%.
- Keep invoices for category review if a charge appears misclassified.

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Travel Planning and Booking Assistance Services
content:

- Coordinate multi-traveler itineraries and meeting blocks with negotiated rates where available.

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Understanding Your Annual Fee and Premium Perks

title: Business Platinum Rewards Card: Which Media and Advertising Expenses Qualify?
content:
## Qualifying Media and Advertising Purchases
- Digital advertising on major online platforms when processed under advertising or media merchant categories.
- Search engine marketing, social media ads, programmatic display, and retargeting buys.
- Streaming audio and video media placements when billed by media networks or ad platforms.
- Direct buys from publishers and networks where the merchant category code reflects advertising or media services.

## What Typically Does Not Qualify
- Creative agency retainers, consulting, or production costs if billed under professional services categories.
- Public relations services, talent fees, or event sponsorships coded outside advertising or media categories.
- Invoices routed through non-media intermediaries that use general business services merchant codes.

## Earning Rate
- Qualifying media and advertising transactions earn 4.0% when the merchant submits the charge under an eligible category.

## Tips to Ensure Proper Categorization
- Request direct billing from the advertising or media platform rather than via agencies.
- Verify how the merchant classifies transactions before completing large media buys.
- Keep documentation for any review in case a charge posts under a non-qualifying category.

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Annual Fee and Fee Waiver Options
content:

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Double Cash Back Promo
content:

New Business Silver Rewards Card customers can earn 2x cash back on all purchases for the first 6 months after opening their account.

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Getting Started and Eligibility Requirements

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: How to Earn 10% Back on Travel and Software Purchases
content:
## Earning rates

- Earn 1.0% cash back on all other purchases

- The merchant must process the transaction under a travel or software-related merchant category

- If a merchant sells multiple categories of goods or services, your charge must be coded under a qualifying category to receive 10.0%

## Best practices
- Use this card for airfare, lodging, car rentals, transit, and other travel booked directly with providers or recognized travel agencies
- Use this card to pay recurring SaaS subscriptions and cloud-based software billed by software providers

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: How to File a Travel Insurance Claim
content:
## Before you begin
- Confirm the trip was charged to your card and that the event is covered under the policy
- Collect documentation such as itineraries, receipts, carrier notices, medical reports (if applicable), and proof of out-of-pocket expenses

## Submit your claim
- Contact the travel insurance claims administrator and request the appropriate claim form for your situation
- Provide a clear description of the event and attach all supporting documents
- Keep copies of everything you submit and monitor communications for any additional information requests

## Claim review and reimbursement
- Approved claims are paid up to $15,000 per trip, consistent with policy limits and exclusions
- If additional details are needed, respond promptly to avoid delays
- Once processed, you will receive confirmation of the outcome and any approved reimbursement method

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Maximizing Rewards on Business Travel and Tech Expenses
content:
## Concentrate spend in high-earning categories
- Route eligible travel and software transactions to this card to earn 10.0%
- Use this card for other purchases when convenient to earn 1.0%

## Leverage credits and minimize fees
- Apply your available annual travel credits of up to 50 toward eligible travel purchases when applicable
- For international trips, consider the foreign transaction fee of 1.5% when deciding which card to use abroad

## Equip your team strategically
- Issue employee cards to distribute purchasing power across your organization, up to 17 cards
- Assign each card to the employee or department most likely to incur travel or software charges to capture 10.0%

## Verify merchant category coding
- Confirm that vendors bill under travel or software-related categories before large purchases
- If a charge appears misclassified, request a review so the correct earning rate can be applied

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: New Customer First Year Fee Waiver
content:
## Offer terms
- New customers who open an account during the promotional window receive a first-year annual fee of 0

- Open your account within the promotional window and meet new customer eligibility criteria

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Purchase Protection Benefits Explained
content:
## What’s covered
- Eligible items purchased with your card may be protected against eligible theft or damage occurring within 90 days of the purchase date
- The maximum reimbursement per covered claim is $7,500, subject to terms, conditions, and exclusions

## How to use the benefit
- Retain your purchase receipt and proof of payment with your card
- If an incident occurs, document the circumstances and preserve any relevant reports or repair estimates
- File your claim with supporting documentation so eligibility and benefit amounts can be assessed

## Helpful tips
- Review your statements to match item details to claim documentation
- For high-value items up to $7,500, store serial numbers and photos to streamline verification
- If a merchant offers a replacement or repair, follow those steps and include any paperwork in your claim submission

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Referral Program - Earn Rewards for Referring Businesses
content:
## What you can earn
- Earn $100 for each successful referral that is approved and meets the qualifying spend requirement
- You can earn up to 10 referral bonuses per calendar year

## How to refer
- Share a referral through the official referral channel so applications are tracked correctly
- Ensure the referred business uses your referral details when applying

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Software and SaaS Spending - Qualifying Categories
content:
## Software categories that typically qualify for 10.0%
- SaaS platforms for productivity, collaboration, videoconferencing, and office suites
- CRM, ERP, accounting, and project management subscriptions
- Cloud infrastructure and developer-focused tools billed as software services
- Cybersecurity, endpoint protection, and identity management subscriptions
- Marketing automation, analytics, and content management platforms

## How to ensure your software purchase qualifies
- Pay the software provider directly or via its official billing portal, ensuring the charge is coded under a software-related merchant category
- Review your statements for merchant category descriptions to confirm software classification
- If a vendor sells both services and physical goods, only the portion coded as software will earn the 10.0% rate

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Understanding Your Included Travel Insurance Coverage
content:
## What your travel insurance can cover
- Coverage is available for eligible trips purchased with your card, up to $15,000 per trip
- Covered events may include certain trip interruptions, cancellations, delays, or baggage issues when they meet policy criteria
- Benefits apply as secondary or primary depending on the policy terms and other available coverage

## Coverage limits and documentation
- The maximum payable amount per trip is $15,000, subject to policy terms, conditions, and exclusions
- Claims require proof of payment with your card and supporting documentation such as itineraries, receipts, confirmations, and official reports when applicable

## Practical guidance
- Keep all travel confirmations and receipts in a single place
- Retain correspondence from carriers regarding delays or cancellations
- Report incidents as soon as possible and follow the claim instructions provided by the administrator

title: Business Silver Rewards Card: Which Travel Purchases Qualify for 10% Cash Back?
content:
## Eligible travel categories for 10.0%
- Airlines and air carriers
- Hotels, motels, and other lodging providers
- Car rental agencies
- Rideshare, limousine, and taxi services
- Passenger rail, intercity buses, and ferry operators
- Parking facilities and services coded as travel-related
- Toll payments processed by travel or transportation entities
- Travel agencies and online travel platforms categorized as travel

## Tips to ensure proper classification
- Book directly with travel providers or reputable travel agencies so the charge carries a travel merchant category
- If a charge appears misclassified on your statement, contact support with your receipt so the merchant category can be reviewed
- Split purchases may post in separate categories; only the portion coded as travel will earn 10.0%

title: Green Rewards Card: Apply for a Sustainable Business Credit Card
content:
## Eligibility
- Personal credit: You should meet a minimum FICO threshold of $690.
- Business credit: Established businesses are evaluated using a PAYDEX threshold of 42 in addition to personal credit history.
- Newer businesses: If you have limited business credit history, your personal profile can support your application.

## Costs and Rates
| Cost or Rate | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Annual fee | $100.00 |
| Purchase APR | 19.99% |
| Foreign transaction fee | 1.0% |

## Credit Limits
- Approved credit lines are assigned within a range from $14,000 to $75,000.
- Your limit is determined by factors such as revenue consistency, existing obligations, and overall credit profile.

## Employee Cards
- You can issue up to 15 employee cards to team members.
- Set category-level controls, customize spending rules, and monitor transactions in real time.

## What You Need to Apply
- Business legal name and structure, tax identification, and operating address
- Beneficial owner and authorized officer details
- Recent business financial information and estimated monthly card spend
- Agreement to a personal guaranty where applicable

## How to Apply
- Complete the application in your online banking portal.
- Provide business and owner details, upload requested documents, and review disclosures.
- Submit your application for credit review. You will be notified when a decision is reached.

## After You Submit
- If approved, activate your card and set controls for any employee cards.
- Review your assigned limit and applicable pricing, including $100.00, 19.99%, and 1.0%.
- Add the card to your digital wallet and configure expense categories for smoother reconciliation.

title: Green Rewards Card: Carbon Offset Programs and Options
content:
## Automatic Offset on Spend
- Your card applies an offset of $0.06 pounds of CO₂ for each dollar spent.
- Offsets appear in your environmental dashboard and in the period report once transactions clear.

## Tree-Planting Option
- A tree is planted for each threshold of spend equal to $3,000.
- Calculation method: Eligible spend divided by $3,000 determines planted trees; partial progress accrues toward the next tree.

## Selecting or Adjusting Programs
- In your sustainability settings, choose whether to allocate all offsets to carbon projects, tree planting, or a blend.
- You can update selections at any time; changes apply to spend that posts after your update.

## Project Transparency
- Review summaries for active projects, including region, project type, and verification standards.
- Access certificates or attestations corresponding to reported offsets for inclusion in your records.

## Best Practices
- Align your chosen programs with your internal sustainability objectives.
- Use the per-dollar rate of $0.06 and the tree threshold of $3,000 to plan targeted initiatives.

title: Green Rewards Card: Carbon Reporting for Business Accounting
content:
## Reporting Cadence
- Carbon and sustainability reports are produced on a monthly schedule for consistent accounting alignment.

## Exporting Reports
- Export a summary for executive reporting and a detailed file for accounting.
- Include the export within your month-end or quarter-end close checklist based on your monthly cycle.

## Reconciling to the General Ledger
- Map category-level emissions and offsets to designated ledger accounts.
- Use transaction-level detail to substantiate journal entries for sustainability initiatives.

## Audit Readiness
- Retain exported reports alongside supporting documentation to evidence your methodology and controls.
- Ensure internal policies describe how you use reported offsets in corporate disclosures.

## Integration Tips
- Align your expense categories to sustainability mappings for predictable reporting.
- Establish a workflow to review and approve the monthly file before posting entries.

title: Green Rewards Card: Earning Rewards on Eco-Friendly Purchases
content:
## Cash Back Earning Rates
| Purchase Type | Cash Back Rate |
| --- | --- |
| Sustainable/eco-friendly merchants | 3.0% |
| Other purchases | 1.0% |

## How Rewards Accrue
- Rewards are tracked per transaction and consolidate at the account level.
- Purchases at qualifying sustainable merchants earn 3.0%, while all other eligible purchases earn 1.0%.

## Eligible Purchases
- Most goods and services purchased from merchants that process through standard networks
- Online and in-person transactions that clear under eligible merchant categories

## Ineligible Purchases
- Cash equivalents, balance transfers, and certain fees do not earn rewards.
- Reversed or refunded transactions forfeit associated rewards in the period the adjustment posts.

## Managing and Viewing Rewards
- Track rewards by merchant category and by cardholder in your rewards dashboard.
- Use category filters to confirm which transactions qualified for 3.0%.

## Tips for Maximizing Earnings
- Set default categories for frequent eco-friendly vendors.
- Encourage team members to use the card at qualifying sustainable merchants whenever applicable.

title: Green Rewards Card: How the Sustainability Tracker Works
content:
## Overview of Data Flow
- Transaction categorization: Card purchases are automatically classified by merchant category and spend type.
- Impact mapping: Eligible categories map to emissions factors to estimate your footprint, then apply offsets.

## Offset Mechanics
- For every dollar you spend, the tracker applies an automatic offset of $0.06 pounds of CO₂.
- Offsets are tallied continuously and displayed in your environmental dashboard.

## Reporting Cadence
- Sustainability and carbon reports are compiled on a monthly basis.
- Each report aggregates categorized spend, estimated emissions, and total offsets applied.

## What You Will See in the Tracker
- Real-time spend segmentation by category and by cardholder
- Estimated emissions generated versus offsets applied using $0.06
- Net impact summaries aligned to your monthly reporting period

## Data Quality and Adjustments
- Returns and refunds are netted against the reporting period in which they post.
- If a merchant reclassifies, category updates flow through to your next monthly report.

## Tips for Accurate Tracking
- Assign clear categories to recurring vendors so future transactions auto-map.
- Encourage consistent receipt tagging to improve activity-level reporting.

title: Green Rewards Card: Referral Program - Earn Rewards for Referring Businesses
content:
## How the Referral Program Works
- Share your referral link with a business contact.
- When a referred business is approved and meets the qualifying spend, you earn 75.

## Earning Limits and Timing
- You can earn up to 7 referral bonuses per calendar year.
- The referred business must spend at least $2,250 within 90 days from account opening to qualify your bonus.
- Bonuses are credited after verification that the qualifying criteria have been met.

## Qualifying Criteria
- The referred business must apply through your unique referral link.
- The application must be approved and the account must remain in good standing through the verification date.
- Qualifying purchases exclude cash equivalents, certain fees, and returned transactions.

## Tracking Your Referrals
- View referral status in your rewards section, including approval state and qualifying spend progress.
- You will be notified when a referral meets $2,250 within 90 days and your 75 is pending.

## Tips for Successful Referrals
- Share how the card’s sustainability features align with common business goals.
- Encourage referrals to complete required steps promptly so their spend can count toward the qualification window.

title: Green Rewards Card: Setting Sustainability Goals for Your Business
content:
## Choose Your Goal Framework
- Offset-based goals: Target a specific level of CO₂ offset using the per-dollar offset rate of $0.06.
- Tree-based goals: Target the number of trees to be planted using the spend threshold of $3,000.

## Building an Offset Goal
- Determine the emissions you want to counterbalance.
- Translate the target into required card spend using $0.06 as your conversion factor.
- Track progress in your dashboard and adjust category strategies as needed.

## Building a Tree-Planting Goal
- Decide on a tree count target for the period.
- Convert that objective into a spend target using $3,000.
- Monitor progress and prioritize categories most aligned with your sustainability strategy.

## Execution Checklist
- Configure category mappings that reflect your operational realities.
- Periodically review results and refine goals based on observed trends.
- Share progress with stakeholders to maintain accountability.

title: Green Rewards Card: Understanding Your Carbon Footprint Report
content:
## Report Structure
- Summary page: High-level view of total categorized spend, estimated emissions, and total offsets applied.
- Category analysis: Emissions estimates by spending category with variance highlights period over period.
- Offset details: A breakdown of offsets accrued using the per-dollar rate of $0.06.
- Net impact: Your emissions minus applied offsets for the reporting window.

## Reporting Cadence
- Reports are delivered on a monthly basis. Each report covers activity that posted in that period.

## How Offsets Are Calculated
- Formula: Eligible spend multiplied by $0.06 yields pounds of CO₂ offset.
- Returns and credits: These reduce eligible spend and therefore reduce calculated offsets in the period they post.

## Reconciling to Your Transactions
- Match the total eligible spend reported to your card ledger for the same monthly period.
- Review category mappings for large vendors to ensure the appropriate emissions factors were applied.

## Interpreting Trends
- Use the category analysis to identify high-impact spend areas.
- Compare net impact across consecutive monthly reports to assess progress.

## Exporting and Sharing
- Export the report for internal review, sustainability disclosures, or stakeholder updates.
- Keep the exported file with your accounting records so that your emissions narrative and financials remain aligned.

title: Green Rewards Card: Viewing Your Monthly Environmental Impact Dashboard
content:
## Accessing the Dashboard
- Open your account and navigate to the Environmental Impact section.
- Choose the reporting view aligned with your monthly cadence.

## What Appears on the Dashboard
- Emissions overview: Estimated emissions by category with visual breakdowns.
- Offsets applied: Running total computed using $0.06.
- Tree progress: A progress indicator that advances with eligible spend against $3,000.
- Net impact: Emissions minus offsets for the selected period.

## Filters and Views
- Filter by merchant category or cardholder to isolate specific drivers of impact.
- Toggle views to compare current activity against previous periods on the same monthly cycle.

## Exports
- Download a summary suitable for stakeholder updates.
- Export detail to support internal analysis and reconciliation.

## Troubleshooting Display Issues
- If a purchase is not reflected, wait for the transaction to clear.
- Confirm that the merchant category aligns with your sustainability mapping.
- Revisit settings to ensure your view matches your monthly preference.

title: Green Rewards Card: Which Merchants Are Considered Sustainable?
content:
## How Merchant Eligibility Is Determined
- Eligibility is based on merchant classification, environmental certifications, and program criteria.
- Merchants aligned with sustainable goods, services, or infrastructure are generally considered.

## Examples of Eligible Merchant Types
- Renewable energy providers and clean energy marketplaces
- Public transit agencies and shared mobility platforms
- Electric vehicle charging networks
- Certified green hotels and eco-certified lodging providers
- Sustainable office supplies and recycled materials vendors
- Waste reduction, recycling, and circular economy services
- Carbon offset and climate project organizations
- Sustainable logistics and low-emissions shipping providers
- Community solar and energy efficiency services

## Non-Qualifying or Mixed-Category Merchants
- General merchandise retailers without clear sustainable classifications
- Marketplace transactions where the end merchant category cannot be verified
- Cash equivalents and funding transactions, which do not qualify

## Verifying Merchant Qualification
- Review the merchant category in your transaction details.
- Check for sustainability badges in your transaction feed where available.

## Rewards Reminder
- Purchases at qualifying sustainable merchants earn 3.0% cash back when processed under eligible categories.

title: Platinum Rewards Card: Elite Business Credit Card Application
content:
## Eligibility and Credit Standards
- Minimum personal credit score required: $765
- Minimum business credit score (PAYDEX) for established businesses: 77
- Carried balance APR: 16.99%

## Credit Limits
- Minimum approved credit limit: $75,000
- Maximum available credit limit: $400,000

## How to Apply
- Prepare business legal details, ownership structure, and verification documents that substantiate established operations and strong credit history.
- Ensure the primary owner or officer meets the minimum personal credit threshold and is prepared to authorize a credit review.
- Provide recent business financials and bank references to support the requested credit limit within the available range.
- Submit the application through your online dashboard or with assistance from a commercial lending specialist; include accurate contact information for follow-up.

## Underwriting Review
- Your application is evaluated against both personal and business credit standards, including $765 and 77.
- Requested limits are aligned with financial capacity and may be adjusted within $75,000 to $400,000.
- If approved, your account will reflect the applicable APR for carried balances of 16.99%.

## Tips for a Smooth Review
- Match your requested limit to demonstrated cash flow and liquidity.
- Provide consistent legal names across all documents and tax records.
- Respond promptly to any verification requests to avoid processing delays.

title: Silver Zoom Card: Adding Fleet Drivers to Your Account
content:

- Open your account management portal and navigate to the user or card management section
- Add a driver by entering their name and contact details, then submit the request for an additional card
- Assign role permissions appropriate to the driver’s responsibilities

- Maintain an active roster and promptly deactivate cards for drivers who no longer need access

title: Silver Zoom Card: Designed for Transportation and Logistics Businesses
content:
## Who this card is built for
- Established transportation and logistics operators that can meet both personal and business credit criteria
- Review focuses on a combination of your personal credit profile at or above $680 and a business credit profile at or above 57

- Receive a recurring fuel price reduction of 10.0% at participating stations
- Earn elevated rewards on sector-specific spend at 3.0%
- Earn a flat rate on all other eligible purchases at 1.0%

- Annual membership is $74.50
- New customer fuel financing promotion: an APR of 0% on fuel purchases for the first year, subject to eligibility and timing rules

## Why this mix matters for logistics
- The combination of 10.0% off at the pump and ongoing rewards helps reduce total cost per mile
- Dual-credit evaluation using $680 and 57 supports approval decisions designed for established operators

title: Silver Zoom Card: Eligibility Requirements for Logistics Businesses
content:
## Credit profile requirements
- Personal credit is evaluated and must be at or above $680 as a secondary approval factor
- Business credit is evaluated and must be at or above 57 on the PAYDEX scale for logistics business approval

- Approved accounts receive a credit line within a range that starts at $22,500 and can extend up to $175,000 based on underwriting

- Meeting the minimum criteria of $680 and 57 does not guarantee approval
- Underwriting may consider business tenure, industry stability, and current financial performance
- Ensure all application details are accurate and complete to avoid delays

title: Silver Zoom Card: Fuel Purchase Reporting and Analytics
content:
## Views that matter
- A fuel summary view highlights total fuel spend and reductions applied at 10.0%
- Category earnings reports display rewards accrued on eligible transportation and logistics expenses at 3.0%

## Key fields to review
- Discount rate field: confirms application of 10.0% to eligible fuel transactions
- Category rate field: confirms earning at 3.0% for qualifying spend
- Merchant and station identifiers: help you confirm in-network activity and compare station-level pricing

## How to analyze
- Filter by merchant category and participating network to isolate transactions with the 10.0% reduction
- Export to your accounting system to reconcile reductions and rewards against route plans
- Use variance checks to spot out-of-network fueling where the expected discount did not apply

## Reporting hygiene
- Tag transactions with vehicle or route identifiers for clearer allocation
- Periodically audit a sample of receipts against reported discounts and rewards

title: Silver Zoom Card: How to Claim Your Triple Cash Back Promo
content:
## Before you start
- Confirm that you are within the eligible claim dates of 2024-01-01 through 2025-12-31
- Ensure your account is open and in good standing

## Claim and activation
- Open your account portal and navigate to promotional offers
- Select the triple cash back offer and confirm your acceptance within the claim window
- Watch for an on‑screen or email confirmation that the offer is active

## After you claim
- Your promotional earning period runs for 9 months from account opening
- During the promo period, your earnings reflect a multiplier of 3
- Monitor your statements to verify promotional earnings are posting correctly

## If you miss the window
- Claims submitted after 2025-12-31 are not eligible for activation
- You can continue earning at your standard rates if the promo is not activated in time

title: Silver Zoom Card: How to Get Fuel Discounts at Participating Stations
content:
## Confirm participation before you fuel
- Check that the station belongs to one of the 10 participating fuel networks
- Look for participation indicators at the pump or register, or verify within your account tools

## Using your card at the pump or inside
- Present your card at payment and ensure the merchant is processing the transaction under a participating fuel category
- Keep the receipt in case you need to reconcile discounts later

## How the discount is applied
- Eligible transactions receive a price reduction or statement adjustment equal to 10.0% of the fuel subtotal when processed by a participating location
- If the reduction does not appear immediately, it may post as an adjustment after the transaction settles

## Tips to ensure consistent savings
- Favor stations within the 10 supported networks on regular routes
- If a location appears out of network, choose a nearby participating station to receive the full 10.0% benefit

title: Silver Zoom Card: Managing Multiple Vehicle Expenses
content:
## Structure your fleet spending
- Issue driver cards to team members who need purchasing authority, up to a total of 60 under your account
- Assign cards to specific vehicles or routes for clearer allocation and reconciliation

## Control daily exposure
- Set a per‑driver daily spending cap at or below $1,250 to match expected fueling needs
- Use category controls to ensure the cap applies primarily to fuel and approved logistics expenses

## International purchases
- For fueling outside your home country, transactions are subject to a foreign transaction fee of 2.5%
- Plan routes and budgets with this fee in mind when operating internationally

## Reconciliation workflow
- Tag purchases by vehicle or route to streamline cost-per-mile analysis
- Review exceptions where spend nears or exceeds the $1,250 threshold and adjust controls if necessary

title: Silver Zoom Card: New Customer Promo - 0% APR on Fuel Purchases
content:
## What the promotion offers
- An introductory APR of 0% on fuel purchases for the first year for new customers who qualify under the promotional window

## Promotional window
- Accounts opened within 2025-09-15 through 2025-12-31 are eligible, subject to approval and program rules

## After the promotional period
- Once the promotional fuel period ends, new fuel purchases and any remaining promotional balances accrue interest at your standard rate of 20.99% when carried

## How to make the most of it
- Channel fuel spend to participating stations during the promotional period to pair the financing benefit with eligible fuel savings
- Continue to make at least the minimum required payments by each statement due date to keep the promotional terms in effect

title: Silver Zoom Card: Referral Program - Earn Rewards for Referring Logistics Businesses
content:
## How the referral program works
- Earn a referral bonus of $150 for each referred logistics business that is approved and meets the qualifying spend requirement
- Bonuses are capped at 10 per calendar year

## Qualifying activity for your referral
- The referred business must complete purchases totaling $7,500 within 90 days of account opening

- Share your referral link or submit the referral through your account portal before the referred business applies
- Ensure the referred business uses your link or is otherwise properly attributed during application
