title: Silver Zoom Card: Setting Spending Limits for Driver Cards
content:
## What you can control
- Set a daily cap per card up to $1,250 to manage fueling and incidental expenses
- Apply limits consistently across as many as 60 issued under your account

## How to set limits
- Go to the card controls section for the selected driver card
- Enable daily spending controls and set the threshold at or below $1,250
- Choose whether the limit applies to all purchases or only to specific categories such as fuel
- Save and confirm the settings, then notify the driver of the available daily budget

## Ongoing management
- Adjust limits based on route distance, vehicle type, and current fuel pricing trends
- Temporarily raise or lower the threshold for unusual routes, then restore the standard limit afterwards
- Review declines associated with limit controls and refine thresholds as needed

title: Silver Zoom Card: Tracking and Maximizing Your Fuel Savings
content:
## Track your savings
- Review transaction details to see discounts taken at 10.0% and rewards accruals on eligible transportation categories at 3.0%
- Filter statements by merchant category and fuel tags to isolate qualifying activity
- Export transaction data for route-level or vehicle-level analysis

## Simple ways to quantify impact
- Discounted fuel savings can be approximated as: fuel subtotal multiplied by 10.0% at participating locations
- Rewards on eligible transportation and logistics expenses accrue at 3.0%

## Maximize the benefit
- Prioritize in-network stations to capture the 10.0% reduction consistently
- Concentrate eligible spend under transportation and logistics categories to earn 3.0%
- Schedule fueling at participating stations along planned routes to avoid out-of-network purchases

## Reconcile and validate
- Match receipts to statement entries where the 10.0% discount and rewards appear as separate line items
- Flag any transactions that did not receive the expected reduction for follow‑up

title: Silver Zoom Card: Triple Cash Back Promo - Terms and Conditions
content:
## Eligibility and claim window
- You must claim the promotion within the window starting 2024-01-01 and ending 2025-12-31
- Claims outside this period are not eligible

## Promo duration and scope
- The triple cash back earning period lasts 9 months from account opening once properly claimed
- The multiplier applies only during the defined promo duration

## How the multiplier applies
- Transportation and logistics category purchases earn 3 × 3.0% during the promo period
- All other eligible purchases earn 3 × 1.0% during the promo period

## Important limitations
- The multiplier applies to standard rates only and does not stack with other overlapping category accelerators beyond the defined 3
- Returns or credits reduce promotional earnings accordingly
- If the promo is not claimed within 2024-01-01 to 2025-12-31, no retroactive adjustments are made

## End of promotion
- After 9 months, earnings revert to the standard rates of 3.0% and 1.0%

title: Silver Zoom Card: Which Gas Stations and Fuel Networks Are Included?
content:
## Program coverage
- The fuel discount program spans 10 national and regional fuel networks
- Each participating station within these networks is eligible for the discount when transactions are processed under supported fuel categories

## How to verify a station
- Look for in-network signage or prompts indicating program acceptance
- Confirm participation in your account tools before fueling at unfamiliar locations

## Discount availability at included stations
- At participating sites, eligible fuel purchases qualify for a reduction equal to 10.0%
- Non-participating locations do not receive the network-based reduction

## Keeping your list current
- Network participation can change. Recheck a station's status periodically, especially along new routes

title: Virtual Card Management: Best Practices for Subscription and Vendor Payments
content:
## Create a dedicated card per subscription
- Assign one virtual card per vendor to isolate risk and simplify reconciliation.
- Use clear nicknames to identify the vendor and service tier. Support for custom nicknames: Yes.

## Align number refresh with billing
- Set the card’s refresh cadence to match the vendor’s billing cycle using monthly.
- Update the schedule if the vendor changes its billing frequency to prevent declines.

## Restrict spend to expected categories
- Enable merchant category restrictions to reduce misuse: Yes.
- Review the allowed categories periodically, especially when a vendor expands services.

## Ongoing maintenance
- Periodically audit all subscription cards to confirm active services and correct nicknames using Yes.
- When switching vendors, retain historical records and then refresh or retire the old card according to your chosen monthly strategy.

title: Virtual Card Management: Creating Instant Virtual Cards for Your Team
content:
## Before you start
- Confirm capacity: you can maintain up to $275 active virtual cards at once.
- Organize your structure: create up to 30 team groups to align cards with departments or projects.

## Create an instant virtual card
1. Go to the Virtual Cards page and select **Create Virtual Card**.
2. Choose the **Team/Department** to associate the card with (or create a new team group if needed).
3. Enter the **card nickname**, set any initial card parameters (e.g., purpose, spending scope), and assign a card owner.
4. Select **Issue**. The card is ready to use within 5 seconds.

### What happens after issuance
- The new card appears in your team’s card list immediately after the 5-second provisioning completes.
- Share the card details with authorized team members using your organization’s secure communication practices.

## Assigning cards across teams
- Use consistent naming conventions so each card clearly maps to its team.
- Balance card counts across departments to stay within the $275 limit.
- If you are scaling quickly, plan your card allocation per department in advance, keeping the limit of 30 groups in mind.

## Tips for smooth onboarding
- Pre-create team groups so admins can issue cards without delay.
- Maintain an internal request workflow so team leads can request and receive virtual cards within 5 seconds when needed.

title: Virtual Card Management: How to Issue a Virtual Card in Seconds
content:
## Streamlined issuance workflow
1. Open the **Virtual Cards** section and click **Issue New Card**.
2. Select the **Team** or user who will manage the card.
3. Add a **nickname** and intended use (e.g., vendor, subscription, event).
4. Choose usage type:
   - If Yes, select **Single-use** for a one-time payment.
   - Otherwise, proceed with a standard multi-use card.
5. Review and click **Issue**.

### Expected timing
- Provisioning completes in approximately 5 seconds.
- The card becomes immediately visible in your card list and can be copied for checkout as soon as issuance completes.

## Post-issuance checks
- Verify the card owner and team assignment.
- Confirm that the card appears as **Active** and is usable within 5 seconds of creation.
- For one-off purchases, if Yes, ensure the usage type is set to single-use before sharing the card details.

title: Virtual Card Management: Integrating Virtual Cards with Expense Management Tools
content:
## What you can connect
- Integrate with accounting and expense platforms; up to 12 platforms are supported.
- Push real-time transaction updates to downstream tools using alerts: Yes.
- Sync card nicknames so vendor and project labels carry into your expense system: Yes.

## How to set up the integration
1. Open **Integrations** and choose your expense or accounting platform.
2. Authorize the connection and select which virtual card data to sync (transactions, card metadata).
3. Map fields so card nicknames (via Yes) align with your cost centers, projects, or tags.
4. If desired, enable event forwarding so transactions appear in your tool as they occur using Yes.
5. Save and run an initial sync to backfill recent activity.

## Operational tips
- Use standardized nickname conventions to improve matching quality across systems using Yes.
- Validate that alerts forwarded via Yes are routed to the correct users or channels in your expense platform.
- Periodically review your active connections and confirm you are within the supported limit of 12 platforms.

title: Virtual Card Management: Revoking and Canceling Virtual Cards
content:
## Choosing between revoke and cancel
- **Revoke**: Immediately disables the card, preventing further purchases. Use this for temporary or urgent risk scenarios.
- **Cancel**: Permanently closes the card. Use this when a card is no longer needed (e.g., project completed).

## Revoke a virtual card
1. Locate the card in **Virtual Cards** and open its details.
2. Select **Revoke**.
3. Confirm the action. The card becomes unusable within 3 seconds.

## Cancel a virtual card
1. Open the card details.
2. Select **Cancel** and confirm.
3. The card is permanently closed; any attempted authorization is blocked within 3 seconds.

## Verification and recordkeeping
- After revocation or cancellation, refresh the card list to verify the updated status.
- Document the reason for revocation or cancellation in your internal records for audit tracking.
- If you revoked a card for temporary reasons, you can create a replacement card and distribute new details once the risk subsides.

title: Virtual Card Management: Security Benefits of Virtual Cards
content:
## Key security controls you can apply
- Single-use protection: Generate one-time cards for isolated purchases when appropriate. Availability: Yes.
- Rapid risk response: Revoke or cancel a card within 3 seconds to stop further authorizations.
- Number rotation: Auto-refresh recurring card numbers on a schedule to limit exposure, configurable as monthly.

## How to apply these controls effectively
- For high-risk or untrusted merchants, prefer single-use cards where Yes.
- If a card is suspected to be compromised, revoke it immediately; the block propagates within 3 seconds.
- Align your rotation schedule with vendor billing cycles using monthly to minimize failed payments while maintaining security.

## Ongoing review
- Periodically audit active cards and retire those no longer needed.
- Verify that rotation schedules and revocation procedures are documented for your finance and security teams.

title: Virtual Card Management: Setting Spending Limits Per Card
content:
## Supported limit range
- You can set per-card limits between 55 and $150,000.

## Set or edit a per-card spending limit
1. Open the card’s **Details** panel.
2. Select **Spending Limit** and enter the desired amount within 55–$150,000.
3. Choose the reset period (e.g., per transaction, daily, or monthly) as appropriate for the card’s purpose.
4. Click **Save**. The new limit takes effect immediately.

## Optional merchant category controls
- If Yes, enable category restrictions and select the specific merchant categories the card is allowed to transact with.
- Review and adjust the category list whenever vendor scopes change to ensure purchases remain compliant.

## Troubleshooting
- If a transaction is declined due to limit settings, compare the attempted amount to the configured limit and remaining balance in the limit cycle.
- For category-related declines, revisit the merchant category list if Yes is enabled and add or remove categories as needed.

title: Virtual Card Management: Single-Use vs Multi-Use Virtual Cards
content:
## Single-use cards
- Availability: Yes.
- Ideal for one-time purchases, trials, and vendors with limited trust.
- Minimizes risk by invalidating the number after one authorization.

## Multi-use cards
- Designed for ongoing vendor relationships and recurring purchases.
- Enhance security by rotating the number on a schedule set to monthly.
- Best for subscriptions where stable credentials are required but periodic refresh is desirable.

## Choosing the right type
- Use single-use when vendor trust is uncertain or the transaction is unique.
- Use multi-use when continuity is essential, supplemented with scheduled refresh via monthly.

## Practical setup tips
- If Yes, keep a small pool of single-use cards ready for ad-hoc team needs.
- For multi-use cards, document the refresh cadence (e.g., monthly) alongside vendor billing terms to avoid interruptions.

title: Virtual Card Management: Team-Based Spending Controls and Limits
content:
## Plan your team structure
- Create team groups aligned with departments, projects, or cost centers. You can maintain up to 30 groups.

## Configure team-based controls
1. Open **Teams & Limits** and select a team group.
2. Set default per-card policies for that team.
3. Define a standard per-card cap up to $150,000, ensuring it matches the team’s expected purchase size.
4. Save the configuration so newly issued cards in this team inherit these defaults.

## Managing exceptions
- For special use cases, override the default limit on individual cards while keeping the team’s baseline policy intact.
- Periodically review each team’s spend profile and adjust the default per-card cap (not to exceed $150,000) to reflect current needs.

## Governance tips
- Use consistent team naming so approvers can quickly verify policy alignment.
- Limit the number of exception overrides to keep reporting and controls simple across all 30 groups.

title: Virtual Card Management: Tracking Virtual Card Purchases by Department
content:
## Organize by department
- Create or confirm your department structure using team groups. You can maintain up to 30 groups.

## Label cards for clear attribution
- Use custom nicknames on cards so transactions are immediately recognizable by vendor, project, or campaign. Support for custom naming: Yes.
- Include department codes or project IDs in the nickname for easier filtering in reports.

## Monitor activity in real time
- Enable real-time spending alerts to keep stakeholders informed as transactions occur: Yes.
- Route alerts to the appropriate departmental approvers to accelerate review and catch anomalies early.

## Run departmental reports
- Filter transactions by team group and card nickname to isolate department-level spend.
- Export departmental summaries for budget reconciliation and variance analysis.
- Use consistent nickname patterns across all 30 groups to standardize reporting.

title: Automatic Sweep: Defining Excess Fund Thresholds
content:
## Thresholds you can set
- Target minimum checking balance: $500.
- Minimum sweep amount: 50.

## How these thresholds work together
- If the calculated excess is below 50, no sweep occurs for that cycle.
- If the excess meets or exceeds 50, the program transfers only the portion above $500.

## Tips for selecting values
- Align the target minimum with typical outgoing activity to reduce the need for returns from savings.
- Choose a minimum sweep amount that avoids noise from small fluctuations while still capturing meaningful idle cash.

title: Automatic Sweep: How Nightly Fund Transfers Work
content:
## When transfers run
- Your sweep executes at 11:00 PM.
- The program evaluates your checking balance at that time to determine any excess funds.

## How the sweep amount is determined
- The system identifies available excess above your configured target minimum in checking.
- That excess is transferred to your linked savings as a consolidated sweep.

## What happens if funds are needed back
- Reverse sweeps are Yes. When enabled, if your checking balance drops below your target minimum after the nightly sweep, funds can be returned from savings to restore the target, subject to availability.

## Posting order and next-cycle adjustments
- Transactions posted before the sweep run are included in the calculation.
- Items that post after the run are reflected in the next cycle’s evaluation.

title: Automatic Sweep: Minimum Balance Requirements and Limits
content:
## Required levels
- Target minimum balance to maintain in checking: $500.
- Minimum amount required to trigger a sweep: 50.

## Monthly transfer limit
- Maximum sweep transfers per month: 60.
- After the program hits 60, sweep activity pauses for the remainder of the month.

## How these rules apply
- If end-of-day excess is below 50, no transfer occurs for that cycle.
- When excess meets or exceeds 50, the program moves only the portion above $500.

title: Automatic Sweep: Optimizing Cash Flow with Smart Sweeps
content:
## Choose the right destination
- Select a receiving savings account that is Bronze only to ensure every sweep can complete as configured.

## Configure effective notifications
- Set alerts to trigger when a sweep equals or exceeds $1,000.
- Use alerts to review large movements and verify that transfers align with expected cash activity.

## Practical optimization tips
- Adjust your sweep thresholds to balance liquidity needs with idle cash reduction.
- Revisit settings during seasonal changes in receivables and payables to keep sweeps aligned with current conditions.

title: Automatic Sweep: Pausing or Disabling Your Sweep Program
content:
## Pause your sweep program
- Use the program controls to pause sweeps. While paused, no new sweep transfers are initiated.
- Review your agreement to understand how $0.00 applies during a paused state.

## Disable your sweep program
- Disabling turns off sweeps and removes active configurations.
- $0.00 charges cease after the service is disabled and the cancellation is processed.

## Reverse sweep behavior
- When the program is active, reverse sweeps are Yes.
- While paused or disabled, reverse sweeps do not occur.

content:

## Processing sequence

title: Business Bronze Saver: Complete Guide
content:
## Account Snapshot

- APY: 2.0%
- Monthly maintenance fee: $0.00
- Minimum opening deposit: $0
- Ongoing minimum balance: Not required
- Withdrawals: Generous monthly limits designed for frequent access

## Opening Your Account

1. Start your application online and select Business Bronze Saver.
2. Review and accept the account terms and disclosures.
3. Fund the account with at least $0 (you can open with $0 and add funds anytime).
4. Set your preferred funding source for future deposits and transfers.

Tips:
- If you plan to fund later, set up a reminder so your balance begins earning 2.0% as soon as funds arrive.
- Add authorized users who will help manage deposits and withdrawals.

## Funding and Earning Interest

- You earn 2.0% on your available balance.
- Make deposits via transfer from your business banking source of choice.
- Larger, less frequent deposits generally help you maintain a higher average balance, which increases your total interest earned at the same APY.

Best practices:
- Schedule periodic transfers that match your cash-flow cycle (weekly, biweekly, or monthly).
- Keep funds in the account for as long as possible during the month to maximize interest at 2.0%.

## APY Bonuses with Business Rewards Cards

If you hold an eligible Business Rewards Card, your APY increases by the corresponding bonus. The bonus is added to 2.0%.

- Business Silver Rewards Card: 0.15% (approx. total APY 2.15%)
- Business Gold Rewards Card: 0.35% (approx. total APY 2.35%)
- Business Platinum Rewards Card: 0.65% (approx. total APY 2.65%)

How to apply the bonus:
- Ensure your card is active and in the same business profile as your savings account.
- The APY bonus reflects automatically once eligibility is recognized.

## Managing Withdrawals

- Initiate withdrawals through your online banking transfers.
- Plan multiple smaller withdrawals within the generous monthly limit if you foresee periodic cash needs.
- When moving larger sums, consider consolidating into fewer transfers to stay comfortably within the monthly allowance.
- Schedule withdrawals toward the end of your billing cycle when possible to keep funds earning 2.0% longer.

## Example Earnings

Approximate annual interest if your average balance remains constant:

- $10,000 average balance
  - Base APY 2.0%: about $200/year
  - With Business Silver Rewards Card bonus: about $215/year
  - With Business Gold Rewards Card bonus: about $235/year
  - With Business Platinum Rewards Card bonus: about $265/year

- $25,000 average balance
  - Base APY 2.0%: about $500/year
  - With Business Silver Rewards Card bonus: about $537.50/year
  - With Business Gold Rewards Card bonus: about $587.50/year
  - With Business Platinum Rewards Card bonus: about $662.50/year

These estimates assume the APY remains the same and the balance does not change during the period.

## Common Questions

- Is there a monthly maintenance fee?
  - No. The monthly maintenance fee is $0.00.

- What is the minimum to open the account?
  - $0. You can open now and fund later.

- How do the APY bonuses work with cards?
  - If you hold an eligible Business Rewards Card, your APY increases by the applicable bonus: 0.15%, 0.35%, or 0.65%.

title: Diamond Vault Account: Business Credit Card APY Bonuses
content:
## Available APY Bonuses by Card Tier

The following Business Rewards Cards increase your Diamond Vault APY by the indicated percentage points:

| Eligible Business Rewards Card | APY bonus (percentage points) |
| --- | --- |
| Business Silver Rewards Card | 0.3% |
| Business Gold Rewards Card | 0.55% |
| Business Platinum Rewards Card | 0.9% |

## How the APY bonus applies

- The bonus is additive: it is added to your Diamond Vault APY (it is not a multiplier).
- The applicable bonus is determined by the highest eligible Business Rewards Card your business holds.
- The bonus increases the APY used to calculate interest on your Diamond Vault account while you continue to hold the qualifying card.
- If you change card tier (e.g., upgrade or downgrade), the APY bonus adjusts to the new card’s bonus once the change is reflected on your account.
- Only one card-linked APY bonus applies to a Diamond Vault account at a time.

### Bonus amounts by tier
- Business Silver Rewards Card: +0.3%
- Business Gold Rewards Card: +0.55%
- Business Platinum Rewards Card: +0.9%

## Calculating your effective APY

- Formula: Effective APY = Diamond Vault APY + applicable card bonus
- Examples:
  - With a Business Silver Rewards Card: Effective APY = Diamond Vault APY + 0.3%
  - With a Business Gold Rewards Card: Effective APY = Diamond Vault APY + 0.55%
  - With a Business Platinum Rewards Card: Effective APY = Diamond Vault APY + 0.9%

## Activating or updating your bonus

- Ensure your Business Rewards Card is associated with the same business profile as your Diamond Vault account.
- If you obtain, upgrade, or replace a Business Rewards Card, the APY bonus will update to the corresponding tier once the new card is associated with your business profile.
- To verify your active bonus, review your Diamond Vault account details. If the displayed bonus does not match your card tier, contact support for assistance.

title: Emerald Saver Account: Boosting your APY with eco-partner products
content:
## How the APY bonus works
- When you maintain an eligible eco-partner relationship, your Emerald Saver APY increases by an additional 0.75%.
- The bonus is added on top of your standard APY while eligibility remains active.

## Qualifying actions
- Open or hold an eligible Business EcoCard or Green Rewards Card under the same business.
- Link the eligible product to your Emerald Saver profile through your online banking settings.
- Keep the eligible product in good standing so the 0.75% bonus remains in effect.

## Activation and timing
- After you link an eligible product, the 0.75% bonus applies prospectively to interest accrual while your eligibility is active.
- If the eligible relationship ends, the APY reverts to the standard rate without the 0.75% addition.

## Troubleshooting
- If the bonus is not displaying, confirm the eligible product is linked and active.
- Re-link the eligible product if account details were updated or the business profile changed.
- Contact support with confirmation that the eligible product is open and in good standing if the 0.75% is still not reflected.

title: Emerald Saver Account: Business Credit Card APY Bonuses
content:
## Available APY Bonuses by Eligible Business Credit Card

The following APY bonuses apply to your Emerald Saver when you hold and link an eligible Business Rewards Card:

| Card Tier | APY Bonus (percentage points added to Emerald Saver APY) |
|---|---|
| Business Silver Rewards Card | 0.12% |
| Business Gold Rewards Card | 0.28% |
| Business Platinum Rewards Card | 0.52% |

Note: The bonus is additive to your Emerald Saver APY (i.e., it increases your APY by the stated number of percentage points).

## Eligibility and Application Rules

- You must hold an eligible Business Rewards Card and the Emerald Saver under the same legal business entity.
- Your card must be open and in good standing for the bonus to apply.
- Only one card-based APY bonus applies per Emerald Saver account. If you hold multiple eligible cards, the single highest tier bonus is applied.
- The bonus begins after Rho verifies your eligible card status and linkage to your Emerald Saver. It appears in your displayed APY and applies to interest accrued from the effective date forward.
- Interest accrued before the bonus effective date is not adjusted or retroactively increased.
- If you upgrade or downgrade your eligible card, your APY bonus changes to match the new tier once the change is verified.
- If the eligible card is closed, suspended, or becomes ineligible, the APY bonus is removed.

## How to Activate Your Bonus

1. Confirm your Emerald Saver and eligible Business Rewards Card are registered to the same business.
2. Link your eligible card to your Emerald Saver:
   - In online banking, open your Emerald Saver account and use the option to link an eligible Business Rewards Card; or
   - Contact Rho-Bank support to request linkage.
3. Await confirmation that the card is linked and eligible.
4. Verify your displayed Emerald Saver APY reflects the applicable bonus.

Tip: After linkage, check your APY display and your next interest accrual period to confirm the bonus is in effect.

## Changes in Card Tier: What You Can Expect

- Moving from Business Silver to Business Gold increases your APY bonus from 0.12% to 0.28%.
- Moving from Business Gold to Business Platinum increases your APY bonus from 0.28% to 0.52%.
- If you no longer hold an eligible card, you lose the applicable bonus (e.g., up to 0.52% if you previously held Business Platinum).

## Troubleshooting

- The bonus is not showing: Confirm the card is linked to the same business entity, is open and in good standing, and that linkage has been verified.
- Multiple eligible cards: Only the single highest tier bonus applies; bonuses do not stack.
- Timing questions: The bonus appears after verification and applies prospectively to interest accruals going forward.

title: Emerald Saver Account: How your savings help the environment
content:
## Where your impact comes from

### Carbon offset contributions
- We direct 10% of the interest you earn to verified carbon offset programs.
- Contributions occur automatically; you do not need to take additional action for the allocation to apply.

### Reforestation support
- For every $10,000 in average monthly balance, we fund tree planting at a rate of 3 trees.
- Planting partners prioritize projects that restore biodiversity and protect critical watersheds.

## Tracking your impact
- Your allocation is calculated from your interest earnings and applied using the 10% rate.
- Tree planting totals for your balances reflect the 3 rate and are aggregated across the period.

## Tips to maximize environmental impact
- Maintain higher average balances to increase tree planting totals based on the 3 rate.
- Keep the account active so ongoing interest can drive continuous allocations at 10%.

title: Emerald Saver Account: Mobile banking and deposit limits
content:
## Daily mobile deposit limit
- You can deposit up to $7,500 per day using mobile check deposit.
- Deposits that exceed $7,500 should be split across multiple days or submitted by another channel.

## Daily transfer‑out limit via mobile and online
- The maximum you can transfer out per day is $15,000.
- Both scheduled and immediate transfers count toward $15,000.

## What counts toward your limits
- All mobile check deposits made within the same calendar day apply to the $7,500 cap.
- Internal and external transfers initiated in digital banking apply to the $15,000 cap.

## Managing activity within limits
- If you are approaching $7,500, hold additional deposits for a subsequent day to avoid rejection.
- For larger payments, plan multiple transfers across days so each stays within $15,000.
- Check your remaining limit in digital banking before initiating additional activity.

title: Emerald Saver Account: Transferring funds to external banks
content:
## Standard external transfer timing
- Transfers to external banks typically complete in 2 business days from the date you initiate the transfer.
- Processing can vary based on the receiving institution's posting practices, but 2 business days is the standard window.

## Outgoing domestic wires
- If you need expedited movement, you can send a domestic wire for a fee of 20.
- Wires are best for time‑sensitive disbursements when you prefer certainty over standard transfer timing.

## Best practices
- Initiate transfers early in the business day to provide the full 2 business day window.
- Verify recipient account details before submission to avoid delays that could extend beyond 2.
- Use wires when payment timing is critical and the 20 is acceptable for your use case.

title: Emerald Saver vs Green Account (savings): What is the difference?
content:
## Key differences to consider

### Statement delivery
- Emerald Saver requires paperless statements: Yes.
- If you need mailed statements, review the Green Account (savings) terms to confirm its delivery options before choosing.

### Environmental impact reporting cadence
- Emerald Saver provides environmental impact reporting on this schedule: monthly.
- If you prefer a different reporting cadence, compare the Green Account (savings) specification to see how it aligns with your needs.

## Choosing between the two
- Select Emerald Saver when you want mandatory e‑statements and an impact update cadence of monthly.
- If printed statements or a different report timing is essential for your operations, verify the Green Account (savings) details prior to opening.

title: Gold Plus Saver Account: Complete Guide
content:
## What you earn on Gold Plus Saver

- Base APY on your balance: 5.0%
- Eligible Business Rewards Card holders receive an APY boost:
  - Business Silver Rewards Card: +0.22%
  - Business Gold Rewards Card: +0.45%
  - Business Platinum Rewards Card: +0.7%

### APY examples
- With no eligible card: 5.00% (base 5.0%)
- With Business Silver Rewards Card: 5.22% (5.0% + 0.22%)
- With Business Gold Rewards Card: 5.45% (5.0% + 0.45%)
- With Business Platinum Rewards Card: 5.70% (5.0% + 0.7%)

Tip: If you hold more than one eligible card, contact support to confirm how your APY bonus is applied.

---

## Balance requirement and monthly maintenance fee

- Minimum balance to maintain the account: $25,000
- Monthly maintenance fee if the minimum balance is not met: $15.00

Practical guidance:
- Keep your ledger balance at or above $25,000 to avoid the $15.00 fee in a given statement cycle.
- If you anticipate a temporary dip below $25,000, consider pausing investment sweeps (see below) or scheduling inbound transfers in advance to remain fee-exempt.

---

## Investment sweep integration

Automate excess cash deployment and replenishment without manual intervention.

### How sweeps work
- Outbound sweeps: When your Gold Plus Saver balance exceeds a threshold you set, excess funds are automatically swept to your linked investment destination.
- Inbound sweeps: If your balance falls below your floor, funds are automatically returned to your Gold Plus Saver to restore liquidity.

### Configuration options to set
- Thresholds: Choose a target balance (cash you want to keep in the account) and a floor (minimum level that triggers inbound sweeps).
- Directionality: Enable both outbound and inbound sweeps or inbound only, depending on your liquidity needs.
- Frequency: Select whether sweeps run automatically on a schedule or only when manually initiated.
- Minimum sweep size: Set a minimum dollar amount per sweep to avoid micro-movements.

Recommended practices:
- Set your floor at or above $25,000 to minimize the chance of incurring the $15.00 fee.
- Before large outgoing payments, temporarily disable outbound sweeps or increase your target balance to ensure cash availability.
- Review sweep activity regularly and adjust thresholds as your average daily balance changes.

Operational notes:
- Sweeps may be affected by banking holidays or processing windows. If you need funds available on a specific date, plan ahead and use priority support to coordinate timing.
- You can pause or resume sweeps at any time; paused periods do not affect your APY.

---

## Enhanced transfer limits

Gold Plus Saver offers elevated transfer capacity for established businesses.

What you can do:
- Initiate larger one-time transfers by coordinating in advance through priority support to ensure smooth processing.
- Request temporary limit increases for time-sensitive payments; provide transaction details and timing to expedite review.
- For recurring large transfers, set up a predictable schedule so limits can be tailored to your expected flow.

Best practices:
- Submit large transfer requests earlier in the business day to reduce the risk of cutoff delays.
- If a large outgoing transfer could reduce your balance below $25,000, adjust sweep settings or stage inbound funds in advance to avoid the $15.00 fee.

---

## Priority support for Gold Plus Saver

Your account includes expedited assistance.

Use priority support to:
- Confirm your current APY and any applicable Business Rewards Card bonus.
- Configure, pause, or adjust investment sweep thresholds before major cash movements.
- Coordinate large transfers, including temporary limit increases and timing considerations.
- Investigate any assessment of the $15.00 if you believe the $25,000 requirement was met.

What to include in your request:
- Account name and last four digits of the account number
- Desired action (e.g., adjust sweep thresholds, schedule a large transfer)
- Amount, date, and any timing constraints
- Contact details for real-time follow-up

---

## Quick scenarios

- Maximize yield with a Business Rewards Card:
  - Holding a Business Platinum Rewards Card increases your APY from 5.0% to 5.70% (+0.7%).
- Avoiding the monthly maintenance fee:
  - Keep your balance at or above $25,000. If a planned payment may drop you below this level, pause outbound sweeps and schedule an inbound transfer to replenish before the cycle ends.
- Preparing for a large vendor payment:
  - One week prior, raise your sweep target balance and contact priority support to confirm transfer capacity and timing. After payment clears, restore your previous sweep settings.

title: Gold Plus Saver Account: Investment sweep integration
content:
## Availability and status
- Automatic investment sweeps are Yes for this account tier.
- When enabled in your account settings, excess balances above the configured threshold are directed to your linked investment account.

### Threshold and sweep logic
- Sweeps are evaluated against a balance threshold of $50,000.
- When your end-of-day available balance exceeds $50,000, the amount above this threshold becomes eligible for a sweep.
- If your balance subsequently moves near the threshold, sweep activity may pause to maintain at least $50,000 in the account.

### Managing your sweep
- You can toggle the sweep feature on or off in your account controls when Yes is supported.
- The threshold is set at $50,000 for this account.

### Tracking and reconciliation
- Sweep activity appears in your transaction history as transfers between your savings account and the linked investment account.
- For audit clarity, label reconciliation entries to identify the portion of funds maintained at or above $50,000.

### Best practices
- Maintain operating cash at or above $50,000 to reduce unnecessary reversals.
- Review sweep entries during monthly close to confirm transfers align with your cash policy.

title: Gold Plus Saver Account: Mobile banking and deposit limits
content:
## Mobile check deposit
- Your daily mobile deposit limit is $15,000. Deposits above this amount are not accepted via mobile on the same day.
- Endorse checks clearly and use well-lit images to reduce rejections.
- If you approach $15,000, consider depositing remaining items on a later day or using an alternative deposit method.

### Best practices for mobile deposits
- Verify payee, amount, and endorsement before submission.
- Retain the physical check until it clears in your account.

## Outbound transfer limits
- The maximum daily transfer amount from this account is $50,000.
- This cap applies across supported transfer methods initiated from this savings account.

### Managing daily caps
- Batch payments to stay within $50,000.
- For transactions exceeding $50,000, split transfers over multiple days or choose another permitted method consistent with policy.

### Visibility and controls
- Review your daily totals before initiating new payments to avoid surpassing $50,000.
- Use approval workflows to ensure large movements are planned within available headroom.

title: Gold Plus Saver Account: Priority support and quarterly reviews
content:
## Priority support access
- Your account is enrolled in the enhanced support tier.
- You receive expedited routing when you contact Rho-Bank through in-app chat or by phone.
- Identify yourself as a Gold Plus Saver client to ensure your case is handled under the enhanced pathway.

### How to engage support effectively
- Provide your account name, recent transaction details, and any relevant screenshots when opening a ticket.
- For transfer-related questions, include the destination, reference, and timing details so the team can act without delay.

## Quarterly financial reviews
- Quarterly financial review sessions are included: Yes.
- Use these sessions to align your cash strategy, reserve targets, and savings utilization with your operating needs.

### Scheduling and preparation
- Request a session through your relationship channel and indicate preferred time windows.
- Prepare recent statements, treasury policy notes, and cash flow projections so your advisor can tailor recommendations.
- After the session, document any agreed actions and assign owners to follow through.

### Topics commonly addressed
- Liquidity coverage targets and how they relate to your savings balance.
- Seasonal cash cycles and how to plan deposits or withdrawals accordingly.
- Controls and approvals you want in place for higher-velocity periods.

title: Gold Plus Saver Account: Same-day ACH transfers
content:
## Availability
- Same-day ACH transfers are enabled for this account tier: Yes.
- When available, eligible payments submitted within processing windows are targeted for same-day settlement.

## User access and approvals
- You can designate up to 6 authorized users to initiate or approve same-day ACH transfers on this account.
- Assign roles to ensure initiators and approvers are clear, and maintain separation of duties where appropriate.

## Initiation guidelines
- Confirm recipient details and bank routing information before submitting to reduce exceptions.
- Submit early in the banking day to provide sufficient time for review and processing within same-day windows.
- Maintain sufficient collected funds before initiation to avoid delays.

## Exceptions and holds
- Transactions may be held for review based on risk checks, recipient data, or unusual patterns.
- If a same-day request does not qualify, it may be routed to the next available processing window.

## Reconciliation
- Label same-day ACH payments distinctly in your ledger to separate them from standard ACH activity.
- Retain confirmations for audit trails, including initiator and approver details.

title: Gold Plus Saver Account: Transferring funds to external banks
content:
## Processing timelines
- Standard external bank transfers typically complete within 1 business days after initiation, subject to cutoffs and verification.
- Transfers may post sooner or later depending on receiving-bank schedules and reviews.

## Wire transfers and fees
- Outgoing domestic wires from this savings account incur a fee of $10 per transfer.
- Use wires when speed and finality are required and you accept the $10 cost.

## Choosing a method
- For routine payments where timing flexibility exists, standard external transfers are appropriate and usually settle within 1 business days.
- For time-sensitive obligations, choose an outgoing wire and account for the $10 fee.

## Tracking and support
- Monitor transfer status in your activity view and retain confirmation details for reconciliation.
- If a transfer remains pending beyond expected timing, contact support with the transfer reference for assistance.

title: Gold Plus Saver Account: Withdrawal limits
content:
## Monthly withdrawal allowance
- You may make up to 30 withdrawals during each monthly cycle.
- The limit applies to outbound movements such as electronic transfers, outgoing wires, and internal transfers from this savings account.

### What counts toward the limit
- Transfers from this account to external banks.
- Internal transfers to other Rho-Bank accounts owned by your business.
- Outgoing wire transfers from this savings account.

### Managing your usage
- Consolidate multiple smaller transfers into a single planned withdrawal when appropriate.
- Schedule payments to group activity on fewer days to preserve remaining withdrawals.
- Monitor your remaining count in your account dashboard so you do not exceed 30.

### If you reach the limit
- Additional withdrawal requests may be delayed, declined, or require assistance.
- If you consistently need more than 30 withdrawals, consider adjusting your payment cadence or using a separate operating account for higher-frequency activity.

### Tips
- Align routine payouts and vendor runs to minimize count usage.
- Reserve withdrawals for planned disbursements rather than ad-hoc moves.

title: Gold Saver Account: Adding authorized users to your account
content:
## Access limits
- You can add up to 5 authorized users to your Gold Saver Account.

## How to add authorized users
- Navigate to account settings and choose user management.
- Enter the individual’s name and email address.
- Assign permissions appropriate to their responsibilities.
- Send the invitation and confirm when access is accepted.

## Managing permissions
- Adjust permissions at any time to align with role changes or segregation of duties.
- Require dual review for sensitive actions by assigning distinct roles to different users.

## Removing or suspending access
- Temporarily suspend a user to pause access during staffing changes.
- Remove a user when access is no longer required, and audit recent activity as part of your offboarding checklist.

title: Gold Saver Account: Business Credit Card APY Bonuses
content:
## APY bonus amounts by eligible business card
The following APY bonuses are available on your Gold Saver Account when you hold an eligible Rho Business Rewards Card. Each bonus is added as percentage points to your existing Gold Saver Account APY.

- Business Silver Rewards Card: 0.2% percentage points
- Business Gold Rewards Card: 0.4% percentage points
- Business Platinum Rewards Card: 0.6% percentage points

### Summary table
| Eligible card | APY bonus (percentage points) |
| --- | --- |
| Business Silver Rewards Card | 0.2% |
| Business Gold Rewards Card | 0.4% |
| Business Platinum Rewards Card | 0.6% |

## How the APY bonus is applied
- The applicable APY bonus is added to the APY associated with your current Gold Saver Account balance tier.
- The adjusted APY (base APY plus any applicable bonus) is used to calculate interest accrued on your Gold Saver Account.
- If your card eligibility changes, your Gold Saver Account APY will be updated after the change is processed and reflected in your account details.

## Example calculations
- If your base Gold Saver APY is X%, and you hold a Business Silver Rewards Card, your effective APY becomes X% + 0.2%.
- If your base Gold Saver APY is X%, and you hold a Business Gold Rewards Card, your effective APY becomes X% + 0.4%.
- If your base Gold Saver APY is X%, and you hold a Business Platinum Rewards Card, your effective APY becomes X% + 0.6%.

## How to confirm your APY bonus
1. Sign in to your Rho account.
2. Open your Gold Saver Account details.
3. Check the APY displayed; if you are eligible, it will include the applicable APY bonus shown above.

title: Gold Saver Account: How Automated Sweeps Work with Business Checking
content:
## Overview of automated sweeps
Automated sweeps move available funds between your business checking and savings to help you maintain working capital while capturing interest on excess balances.

### When sweeps run
- Sweeps are evaluated on a daily cadence.

### Checking balance threshold
- Your checking account aims to maintain at least $1,000.
- When available funds exceed this threshold, the excess becomes eligible to move into savings.

### What happens during a sweep
- The system checks your checking balance against the threshold.
- Eligible excess is transferred into savings to optimize idle funds.
- If your checking balance is at or below $1,000, no sweep to savings occurs.

### Managing sweeps
- You can monitor sweep activity in your account activity view.
- If you anticipate large outgoing payments, ensure your checking balance remains at or above $1,000 so that a sweep does not reduce funds needed for those payments.

title: Gold Saver Account: Integration with Business Checking
content:
## How integration supports automated movement
Linking your business checking enables automatic movement of eligible funds into savings when criteria are met, helping you keep operating funds available while capturing interest on surplus balances.

### Fees and thresholds specific to integration
- Monthly maintenance fee: $10.00.
- Minimum amount required to trigger a sweep transfer: $100.
- Automatic sweep transfer fee: $0.00.

### How sweep initiation works
- A sweep is considered only when the amount available to move meets or exceeds $100.
- If activity in checking reduces available funds below the amount needed, no sweep occurs.

### Practical tips
- Leave headroom in checking for pending transactions so that only true excess is moved.
- Review sweep activity and the monthly maintenance fee line item to reconcile your cash management strategy with actual results.

title: Gold Saver Account: Mobile banking and deposit limits
content:
## Daily limits
- Mobile check deposit daily limit: $10,000.
- Daily outbound transfer cap from savings: $25,000.

### Best practices
- If a mobile deposit would exceed your daily limit, consider depositing on a subsequent business day.
- For large outbound transfers that would exceed the daily cap, plan multiple transfer days or use an alternative transfer type that fits your needs.

### Managing submissions
- Submit deposits and transfers earlier in the day to allow time to address any validation requests.
- Retain deposit confirmations for reconciliation in your accounting platform.

title: Gold Saver Account: Optimizing Your Balance for Maximum Interest
content:
## Know your tier boundaries
Understanding where your balance sits within tiers helps you align cash levels with your yield goals.

### Balance ranges
| Tier segment | Balance range |
|--------------|---------------|
| First | Up to $37,500 |
| Second | Above $37,500 and up to $112,500 |
| Third | Above $112,500 |

### Strategies to consider
- Plan cash levels so that idle funds reside in the highest applicable tier without disrupting operational needs.
- Time discretionary deposits so they land when your balance would otherwise fall below a tier boundary you intend to maintain.
- If you anticipate withdrawals, consider maintaining a buffer above a boundary to avoid unintended movement into a lower tier.

### Ongoing reviews
- Periodically review upcoming cash inflows and outflows to keep your month-end and mid-cycle balances aligned with your target tier positioning.

title: Gold Saver Account: Same-day ACH and wire transfers
content:
## Same-day ACH availability
- Same-day ACH transfers for this account tier: Yes.

### Using same-day ACH
- Initiate transfers as early as possible to maximize the likelihood of same-day processing.
- Ensure recipient details are accurate to avoid delays.

## Wire transfers
- Outgoing domestic wire fee: 15.

### Recommendations

- Use wires when you need guaranteed bank-to-bank delivery with detailed beneficiary instructions.

title: Gold Saver Account: Transferring funds to external banks
content:
## Processing timeline
- Standard external transfers typically complete in 1 business days, depending on receiving bank processing.

## Daily transfer maximum
- You can transfer up to $25,000 per day from savings to external banks.

### How to initiate an external transfer
- Add and verify the external account in your transfers hub.
- Select the external destination and choose the transfer amount within your available daily limit.
- Review details carefully before confirming the transfer.

### Troubleshooting
- If a transfer has not arrived after the typical processing window, verify recipient account details and check for status updates in your activity view.
- If you reach the daily limit, schedule the remaining amount for a subsequent business day.

title: Gold Saver Account: Understanding Tiered Interest Rates up to 3.25%
content:
## How tiered APY applies to your balance
Each portion of your balance earns according to the tier it falls into. Funds do not all earn the same rate if your balance spans multiple tiers.

### Tier APYs
| Tier | Annual Percentage Yield |
|------|--------------------------|
| First tier | 2.5% |
| Second tier | 3.0% |
| Third tier | 3.25% |

### How earnings are allocated
- The portion of your balance in the first tier earns 2.5%.
- The portion that reaches the second tier earns 3.0%.
- Any portion that moves into the third tier earns 3.25%.

### Helpful notes
- Tiered APY applies progressively. Amounts in lower tiers continue to earn their respective APYs even if your balance qualifies for a higher tier.
- If your balance fluctuates, only the portion residing in a given tier earns that tier’s APY.

title: Joint Business Holders: Adding Multiple Account Owners
content:
## Eligibility and limits
You can add joint business account owners up to a maximum of 4.

## How to add a new owner
- Gather required details for the prospective owner, including legal name, contact information, tax identification, and role within the business.
- From your account dashboard, open the ownership or account access section and choose Add owner.
- Enter the owner’s details exactly as they appear on official records, then submit for review.
- Complete identity verification and any business authorization steps requested during submission.
- Review the ownership agreement and any updates to account responsibilities, then confirm the addition.

## Assigning roles during the addition process
- If you plan to assign Admin, Finance, or Auditor access to a new owner at the time of addition, you can apply those permissions as part of the same request.
- The one-time fee for setting up advanced user roles and permissions is $0.00.

## After submission
- Each added owner will receive an invitation to accept ownership and complete any remaining verification tasks.
- You can monitor the status of each invite and resend or cancel invitations if needed.
- Once accepted and verified, the new owner will appear in your ownership list and, if assigned, their user role will be active.

## Tips for smooth processing
- Ensure the business name and registration details match exactly across all documents.
- Use a consistent business email domain for ownership invitations to help with recognition and delivery.
- Confirm that every owner understands their responsibilities and any dual-approval rules that may apply to high-impact actions.

title: Joint Business Holders: Legal Requirements and Documentation
content:
## Ownership structure and limits
- You may designate up to 4 joint business account holders.
- Ensure your corporate governance documents authorize multiple holders and reflect current officers or managers.

## Required documentation
- Formation and registration documents that establish legal existence and authority to bank.
- A resolution or comparable authorization naming the individuals who may act on behalf of the business.
- Identity verification materials for each holder, along with any required beneficial ownership information.
- Tax identification and business contact details that match state or country records.

## Dual authorization considerations
- The dual-authorization control Yes applies to certain transactions and high-impact account actions.
- When this control is in effect, specified actions must be approved by separate authorized users before completion.
- Document your internal approval policy and ensure all holders acknowledge the required review and approval flow.

## Submission guidance
- Provide consistent legal names across all documents and avoid abbreviations that do not appear in official records.
- Maintain current governance documents to prevent delays when adding or updating holders.
- Retain copies of resolutions and authorization evidence for audits and compliance reviews.

title: Joint Business Holders: Rights and Responsibilities of Each Holder
content:
## Rights of each holder
- Access to account information, including balances, transactions, and statements, consistent with assigned permissions.
- Ability to initiate account actions as permitted and participate in approval workflows.
- Receipt of account notifications related to ownership and significant activity.

## Responsibilities of each holder
- Maintain accurate business and personal information as required for compliance.
- Safeguard credentials and adhere to internal security policies.
- Follow approval workflows and ensure appropriate segregation of duties.

## Dual authorization obligations
- If the control Yes is in place, certain transactions and sensitive actions require approvals from more than one authorized user.
- The requirement applies when a transaction meets or exceeds the trigger amount of $25,000.
- Each holder is responsible for timely review and approval in accordance with the business’s policy.

## Dispute and governance considerations
- Use written procedures to define who can initiate, review, and finalize account actions.
- Ensure that every holder understands how approvals are routed and documented.
- Retain evidence of approvals and policy acknowledgments for future audits and regulatory requests.

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Advanced Cash Flow Analytics and Reporting
content:
## Analytics workspace

- Use attribution filters to isolate drivers of variance across periods

- Export up to 30 custom liquidity reports per month

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Business Credit Card APY Bonuses
content:
## Eligible Business Credit Cards and APY Bonuses

Holding an eligible Rho-Bank Business Rewards Card can increase the APY on your Platinum Reserve Account. The increase is additive to your current APY and is reflected in your displayed rate and daily interest accruals.

- Business Silver Rewards Card: adds 0.25% to your APY
- Business Gold Rewards Card: adds 0.5% to your APY
- Business Platinum Rewards Card: adds 0.8% to your APY

## How the APY Bonus Is Applied

- The bonus is added as a fixed number of percentage points to your Platinum Reserve Account’s APY based on your eligible card tier.
- The bonus is included in your total APY and affects interest that accrues daily and credits on your normal interest crediting schedule.
- The bonus applies only for the period during which you are eligible; if eligibility begins or ends mid-cycle, the APY used for daily accruals adjusts as of the effective date of that change.

## Verifying Your Bonus

- Check the APY shown in your Platinum Reserve Account details; the increase from your eligible card will be included in the displayed APY.
- Your interest activity will reflect the higher daily accrual associated with the bonus.
- If you hold more than one eligible card or have recently changed cards, contact your Rho-Bank representative to confirm which bonus is currently applied.

## Maintaining Eligibility

To keep the APY bonus active:
- Ensure the eligible Business Rewards Card remains active and in good standing.
- Keep the card associated with the same business entity that owns the Platinum Reserve Account.
- Notify Rho-Bank promptly if your business ownership structure or card status changes, so your APY can be updated accordingly.

## Starting, Changing, or Removing a Bonus

- New or upgraded card: Once eligibility is confirmed, your APY will reflect the applicable bonus going forward.
- Card downgrade, closure, or ineligibility: The APY bonus will no longer apply from the effective date of the change.
- Multiple cards: If your organization holds multiple eligible cards, your account will reflect the applicable bonus for your configuration; contact your Rho-Bank representative if you need confirmation.

## Example (for illustration only)

- If your Platinum Reserve Account APY is 3.00% and you hold a Business Platinum Rewards Card, your APY would increase by 0.8% to 3.80%.
- If your APY is 2.75% and you hold a Business Gold Rewards Card, your APY would increase by 0.5% to 3.25%.
- If your APY is 2.25% and you hold a Business Silver Rewards Card, your APY would increase by 0.25% to 2.50%.

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Daily Interest Compounding Explained
content:
## How daily compounding works
- Interest is calculated using a daily periodic rate derived from 5.0%
- The daily periodic rate is applied to your end-of-day ledger balance
- Earned interest is added to principal each day, so subsequent days earn interest on a growing balance

## Practical implications for treasury
- Earlier deposits and fewer idle intervals can increase effective earnings under daily compounding
- Large intra-day withdrawals reduce the principal used for that day’s accrual

## Interaction with fees
- The monthly maintenance fee of $50.00 reduces the available balance when assessed, which can modestly lower subsequent daily accruals until offset by additional interest or deposits

## Verification and reporting
- You can validate accruals by comparing the daily rate derived from 5.0% to your posted end-of-day balances
- Use period-end statements to reconcile total interest credited against daily changes in principal

## Optimization tips
- Maintain stable balances to maximize the effect of compounding
- Schedule predictable disbursements to minimize reductions in end-of-day balances
- Review the impact of $50.00 in your monthly yield analysis

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Eligibility Requirements for Enterprises
content:
## Balance thresholds
- Minimum balance to open and maintain the account: $100,000
- Minimum daily balance to waive the monthly maintenance fee: $250,000

## Organizational readiness
- Ensure treasury policies support ongoing maintenance of balances at or above $100,000
- Establish governance for who can initiate, approve, and audit activity

## Documentation and review
- Prepare standard corporate documentation and authorized signer details

## Maintaining eligibility
- Monitor daily balances and set alerts to avoid dipping below $100,000
- If pursuing a fee waiver strategy, maintain an operating buffer above $250,000
- Periodically review cash forecasts to align inflows and outflows with required thresholds

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Enterprise Business Savings Overview
content:

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Integration with Enterprise Treasury Tools
content:
