title: Platinum Reserve Account: Managing authorized users
content:
## Access model
- Define roles for initiators, approvers, and viewers based on segregation-of-duties principles
- Scope permissions to the minimum necessary accounts and actions for each user

## Adding and removing users
- Add users from your administrative settings and assign roles during onboarding
- Remove access promptly when responsibilities change or a user departs
- Review permissions alongside role changes to prevent privilege creep

## User limits
- Maximum number of authorized users: 10
- Plan role assignments with this ceiling in mind, prioritizing shared operational mailboxes where appropriate

## Oversight and audit
- Conduct periodic access reviews and require attestations for high-privilege roles
- Use activity logs to verify that approvals and sensitive actions match your policy

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Mobile banking and deposit limits
content:
## Mobile check deposit limits
- Daily mobile deposit limit: $25,000
- Review remaining daily capacity before submitting additional deposits to avoid holds or rejections

## Outgoing transfer limits
- Maximum daily transfer amount: $100,000
- For larger disbursements, plan multiple approvals and schedule transfers to remain within this threshold

## Best practices to stay within limits
- Monitor limit usage throughout the day and consolidate smaller items where appropriate
- Use descriptive memos to simplify reconciliation when splitting payments across days
- Establish internal approval gates that align with your daily thresholds

## Troubleshooting
- If a deposit or transfer is rejected, check current limit usage and resubmit after adjusting amounts or timing
- Maintain clear documentation for any split transactions to support audit trails

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Same-day transfers and wire capabilities
content:
## Same-day ACH
- Availability: Yes
- Use for time-sensitive disbursements that require rapid settlement within the banking day
- Confirm recipient details and approval routing before submission to avoid returns

## Wire transfers
- Outgoing domestic wire transfer fee: $0
- Suitable for high-value, time-critical payments where finality is essential
- Verify beneficiary instructions carefully and maintain dual approval for risk control

## External bank transfers
- Processing time for external bank transfers: 0 business days
- Schedule transfers earlier in the day to align with cutoff windows and improve posting certainty

## Operational recommendations
- Maintain updated recipient libraries to reduce entry errors
- Use payment notes to facilitate downstream reconciliation

title: Platinum Reserve Account: Using the Liquidity Reporting Dashboard
content:
## Access and navigation
- Sign in and open the liquidity dashboard from your main navigation
- Use the global filters to refine by account, entity, or classification

## Data freshness
- The dashboard refreshes approximately every 32 minutes
- A status indicator shows when the latest refresh completed

## Historical coverage
- You can review historical liquidity trends for up to 24 months
- Apply consistent filters when comparing different periods to ensure apples-to-apples insights

## Working with views
- Create and save custom filter sets for recurring reviews
- Pin key metrics to the top of your view for faster oversight

## Reconciliation workflow
- Compare dashboard balances to statement snapshots for the same date range
- Investigate variances by drilling into transaction-level detail within the filtered view

## Troubleshooting
- If data appears stale, confirm the last refresh time and reapply filters
- For missing history within the 24-month window, verify entity or account scoping in your saved view

title: Silver Plus Saver Account: Adding authorized users
content:
## Access limits
- You can grant access to up to 4 authorized users on your Silver Plus Saver account.

## How to add authorized users
- Navigate to Account settings and open the user management or access control section.
- Select Add user and enter the individual’s full name and email address.
- Assign permissions appropriate to their role (for example, view-only or transacting permissions) and confirm.

### Best practices
- Review user permissions periodically to ensure access matches current responsibilities.
- Remove or downgrade access promptly when roles change.
- Maintain dual controls by separating approval and initiation permissions where appropriate, within the limit of 4.

title: Silver Plus Saver Account: Complete Guide
content:
## Eligibility and Minimum Balance
- You must maintain a minimum balance of $5,000 to keep the Silver Plus Saver account in good standing.
- If your balance is anticipated to dip below $5,000, consider timing deposits and withdrawals to avoid falling under the requirement.

## Interest and APY Structure
- Balances below the tier 2 threshold earn 2.5% APY.
- Balances above the tier 2 threshold earn 4.0% APY.

### Relationship APY Bonuses
If you hold an eligible Business Rewards Card, an APY bonus is added to your base APY:
- Business Silver Rewards Card: +0.18% APY
- Business Gold Rewards Card: +0.38% APY
- Business Platinum Rewards Card: +0.62% APY

The bonus applies to the base APY for the balance tier you qualify for.

### Example APYs With Relationship Bonuses
Below are example total APYs when a relationship bonus applies:

- Balance below tier 2 threshold (base 2.5%):
  - With Business Silver Rewards Card: 2.68% (2.5% + 0.18%)
  - With Business Gold Rewards Card: 2.88% (2.5% + 0.38%)
  - With Business Platinum Rewards Card: 3.12% (2.5% + 0.62%)

- Balance above tier 2 threshold (base 4.0%):
  - With Business Silver Rewards Card: 4.18% (4.0% + 0.18%)
  - With Business Gold Rewards Card: 4.38% (4.0% + 0.38%)
  - With Business Platinum Rewards Card: 4.62% (4.0% + 0.62%)

## Withdrawals and Transfers
Silver Plus Saver offers higher withdrawal limits and improved transfer capabilities compared to Silver Saver. To make the most of these enhancements:
- Plan large withdrawals in advance to ensure smooth processing.
- Use scheduled transfers for recurring payments to maintain your target balance above $5,000.
- For unusually large or time-sensitive transfers, initiate earlier in the business day to reduce the risk of delays.

## Upgrading from Silver Saver
If you are moving up from Silver Saver:
- Confirm you can maintain at least $5,000 in Silver Plus Saver.
- Request an upgrade through your online banking or by contacting support. Your balances will begin earning at the Silver Plus Saver rates as soon as the upgrade is completed.

## How to Verify Your Current APY and Bonus
- In online banking, open the Silver Plus Saver account details to view:
  - Your current balance tier (below or above the tier 2 threshold)
  - Base APY for that tier (2.5% or 4.0%)
  - Any relationship APY bonus applied (e.g., 0.38%) 
- Review after any balance change or card status update to confirm your effective APY.

title: Silver Plus Saver Account: Mobile banking and deposit limits
content:
## Mobile check deposit limits
- You can deposit up to $7,500 per day using mobile check deposit.
- If you reach the daily limit, you can submit additional deposits on the next business day.

### Tips for successful mobile deposits
- Endorse checks appropriately before capture.
- Take photos in a well-lit area and ensure all corners are visible.
- Retain the original check until you confirm the deposit has posted.

## Transfers via mobile and online banking
- The maximum total you can transfer out per day is $15,000.
- This daily transfer cap applies across all same-day and scheduled transfers initiated on that day.

### Best practices
- If you plan multiple transfers in one day, track the cumulative total to stay within $15,000.
- For larger movements that exceed the daily cap, schedule transfers across multiple days.
- Use account alerts to confirm when a mobile deposit is accepted and when a transfer is submitted.

title: Silver Plus Saver Account: Relationship bonuses explained
content:
## How the relationship bonus works
- When you qualify based on holding multiple eligible Rho-Bank products, your Silver Plus Saver APY receives an additive bonus of 0.025%.
- The relationship bonus is applied on top of the APY you earn from your balance tier.

### Applying the bonus to your account
- Eligibility is evaluated based on your relationship status with Rho-Bank.
- Once eligible, the 0.025% increment is added to the APY associated with your current tier for the period you qualify.
- If eligibility changes, the bonus adjusts prospectively.

### Fee considerations
- If you do not meet the minimum balance requirement, a monthly maintenance fee of $10.00 is assessed according to your account terms.
- The relationship bonus affects your APY; it does not change how the monthly maintenance fee is determined.

### Practical tips
- Periodically review your product holdings to ensure you continue to qualify for the relationship bonus.
- Monitor your balance relative to the minimum requirement so that any potential $10.00 does not reduce your interest gains, especially when the 0.025% is active.

title: Silver Plus Saver Account: Same-day ACH and interest compounding
content:
## Same-day ACH availability
- Same-day ACH is available for this account tier: Yes.

### How to request same-day ACH
- When scheduling an ACH transfer, select the same-day delivery option if available for your transfer type and destination.
- Ensure sufficient available funds before submission to avoid reversals.
- Monitor your transfer activity for confirmation that the same-day request was accepted.

## Interest compounding
- Interest on your Silver Plus Saver account is compounded daily.

### Practical tips
- If you anticipate frequent time-sensitive payments, enable notifications so you can choose same-day when it suits your cash flow.
- Keep your balance steady to maximize the benefits of interest compounded daily.

title: Silver Plus Saver Account: Transferring funds to external banks
content:
## Standard external transfers (ACH)
- Transfers to external banks typically complete in 2 business days after submission.
- You can initiate transfers from your Silver Plus Saver account to any verified external account you have linked.

### How to transfer
- Link your external bank using a secure verification method.
- Choose the external account, enter the transfer amount, and submit the request.
- Track the status in your transfer activity until it shows completed.

## Domestic wire transfers
- For time-sensitive needs, you can send a domestic wire from your Silver Plus Saver account.
- An outgoing domestic wire carries a fee of 15.

### When to use which method
- Use a standard external transfer when timing is flexible and you prefer no wire fees.
- Use a domestic wire when you need funds delivered with priority and accept the 15.

### Troubleshooting
- If a transfer is delayed beyond 2 business days, verify recipient details and contact support with the transfer reference.
- For wires, confirm the beneficiary name and routing details before sending to avoid returns.

title: Silver Plus Saver vs Silver Saver Account: What is the difference?
content:
## Key differences when you upgrade to Silver Plus Saver

### Monthly withdrawal allowance
- Silver Plus Saver provides up to 20 free withdrawals per month.
- This higher allowance is designed to support growing operational needs.
- If you exceed the free withdrawal allowance, standard excess-withdrawal handling applies per your account terms.

### Tier 2 eligibility threshold
- On Silver Plus Saver, you become eligible for the higher APY tier once your balance reaches $25,000.
- Hitting this threshold allows you to earn the elevated tier rate for the days your balance qualifies.
- Silver Saver uses a different threshold disclosed in that account’s terms; the Silver Plus Saver threshold above applies specifically to your upgraded account.

title: Silver Saver Account: Adding authorized users
content:
## User limits and access
- You can add up to 3 authorized users on this account.
- Same-day ACH availability for this account tier is No.

## How to add an authorized user
- Open the user management section in your online banking.
- Enter the person’s details and assign appropriate permissions for their role.
- Review and confirm; the user will receive an invitation to set up access.

## Ongoing management
- Review user permissions periodically to align with current responsibilities.
- Remove or downgrade access promptly when roles change or access is no longer required.

title: Silver Saver Account: Higher Interest Rates for Larger Balances
content:
## How the higher APY tiers work

You earn a higher rate when your balance reaches the $25,000 threshold.

- Balances under $25,000: 2.5% APY
- Balances at or above $25,000: 4.0% APY

When your balance is at or above $25,000, the higher APY applies. If your balance falls below $25,000, the lower APY applies.

## Cardholder APY bonuses

If you hold an eligible Business Rewards Card, your APY increases by the corresponding bonus amount, added to the base tier rate:

- Business Silver Rewards Card: +0.1% APY
- Business Gold Rewards Card: +0.3% APY
- Business Platinum Rewards Card: +0.55% APY

Your APY = Base tier APY by balance + applicable card bonus.

## Quick reference: Combined APY by balance and card status

| Balance tier              | Base APY | With Business Silver Rewards Card | With Business Gold Rewards Card | With Business Platinum Rewards Card |
|--------------------------|----------|-----------------------------------|---------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| Under $25,000            | 2.5% | 2.5% + 0.1% = 2.6% | 2.5% + 0.3% = 2.8% | 2.5% + 0.55% = 3.05% |
| $25,000 or more          | 4.0% | 4.0% + 0.1% = 4.1% | 4.0% + 0.3% = 4.3% | 4.0% + 0.55% = 4.55% |

## Examples

- $24,000 balance, no card: 2.5% APY → about $600 in interest over a full year.
- $26,000 balance, no card: 4.0% APY → about $1,040 in interest over a full year.
- $40,000 balance with a Business Gold Rewards Card: 4.0% + 0.3% = 4.3% APY → about $1,720 in interest over a full year.
- $100,000 balance with a Business Platinum Rewards Card: 4.0% + 0.55% = 4.55% APY → about $4,550 in interest over a full year.

## Tips to maintain the higher rate

- Keep a buffer above $25,000 to avoid brief dips that could move you into the lower APY tier.
- If you plan large withdrawals, consider timing deposits so your balance remains at or above $25,000.

title: Silver Saver Account: Maximizing Your Interest Earnings
content:
## How interest is credited
- Interest is credited monthly.
- When your balance is below the threshold, earnings reflect 2.5%.

## Practical strategies
- Keep funds deposited for the entire monthly cycle whenever possible to maximize accrued interest.
- Schedule inflows early in your cycle so funds begin earning as soon as they are available.
- Avoid mid-cycle large outflows when feasible to help maintain a higher eligible balance.

## Ongoing monitoring
- Review posted interest after each monthly cycle and adjust cash management to stay in the most favorable tier.

title: Silver Saver Account: Mobile banking and deposit limits
content:
## Mobile check deposits
- Daily mobile deposit cap: $5,000.
- If a single item would exceed the cap, submit deposits across multiple days or use an alternative deposit method.

## Transfers from mobile and online
- Daily outbound transfer cap: $10,000.
- Multiple transfers on the same day are aggregated toward this cap.

## Tips if you hit a limit
- Schedule transfers on consecutive business days to complete larger movements.
- Submit deposits and transfers earlier in the day to help ensure they count toward the intended day’s limit.
- If your business needs regularly exceed these caps, contact support to discuss options.

title: Silver Saver Account: Transferring funds to external banks
content:
## Transfer options and timing
- Standard external bank transfers typically complete in 2 business days.
- Outgoing domestic wire transfers incur a fee of 20.

## Choosing the right method
- Use standard transfers when timing is flexible and cost sensitivity is high.
- Use a domestic wire when delivery speed and finality are priorities and the fee of 20 is acceptable.

## Scheduling and tracking
- Initiate transfers with enough lead time to accommodate 2 business days when using standard transfers.
- Monitor transfer status in your activity view and confirm receipt with the destination account if timing is critical.

title: Silver Saver Account: Understanding the $25K Balance Threshold
content:
## How the balance threshold affects your APY
- The higher tier applies when your qualifying balance meets or exceeds the threshold.
- During periods you qualify, your APY is 4.0% in accordance with the account’s interest calculation method.

## Coordinating with the maintenance fee waiver
- Maintaining a minimum daily balance of at least $10,000 waives the monthly maintenance fee.
- If you operate close to the threshold, keeping a buffer can help you retain eligibility for the waiver and higher APY tier simultaneously.

## Practical scenarios
- Temporary dips below the threshold may move you to a lower APY tier for the affected period; restoring the qualifying balance can return you to 4.0% thereafter.
- If you expect incoming funds, consider timing them so the deposit settles before the applicable evaluation point for the tier in your cycle.
- Keep an eye on large outgoing payments that might reduce your qualifying balance during a key evaluation period.

title: Silver Saver Account: Upgrading from Bronze Saver
content:
## What changes when you upgrade
- A monthly maintenance fee of $5.00 applies.
- The fee is waived any month your minimum daily balance is at least $10,000.

## Before you request the upgrade
- Confirm your typical operating balance can consistently meet $10,000 to avoid the monthly fee.
- Review upcoming cash flows that could temporarily reduce your daily balance below $10,000.

## After the upgrade
- Monitor your first statement to verify the fee waiver when you maintain $10,000.
- If you see a fee assessed in a month you qualified for the waiver, contact support for a review.

title: Silver Saver Account: What Happens If I Exceed Withdrawal Limits?
content:
## Your monthly limit and fees

| Item | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Monthly free withdrawals | 6 |
| Fee for each excess withdrawal | $5.00 |

### After you reach the limit
- Each additional withdrawal in the same calendar month incurs a $5.00 charge at posting.
- The charge appears as a separate line item on your activity and statement.

### What typically counts as a withdrawal
- External transfers out
- ACH debits you authorize to other institutions
- ATM or in-branch cash withdrawals
- Internal transfers moving funds out of this account

### Ways to avoid excess withdrawal fees
- Consolidate multiple payments into a single transfer when appropriate.
- Use internal transfers only when necessary.
- Plan recurring payments to stay within 6 each month.

title: Transferring funds to external banks: Bronze Saver
content:
## ACH transfers
- Standard ACH transfers to external banks typically take 3 business days to complete.
- Same-day ACH availability: No.
- The maximum you can transfer out per day via ACH is $5,000. If a transfer would exceed $5,000, schedule multiple transfers across different days.

## Domestic wire transfers
- Outgoing domestic wires from this account incur a fee of $25.
- Wires are suitable for time-sensitive payments when you need funds delivered more quickly than standard ACH.

## Best practices
- Initiate transfers earlier in the business day to help avoid delays.
- Verify recipient details carefully before submitting to prevent returns or rejections.

title: User Roles: Audit Trail and Activity Monitoring by Role
content:
## Activity log retention
- User activity logs are retained for 5 years, covering sign-ins, permission changes, approvals, and key configuration updates.
- Retention ensures traceability for internal reviews, external audits, and regulatory inquiries.

## Auditor visibility
- Auditors have read-only access to historical data for 48 months.
- This includes statements, transaction details, and activity logs needed for independent testing and verification.

## Role change monitoring
- When a role change is requested, notifications are issued and the change takes effect after 48 hours.
- The notification window provides a safeguard to detect and reverse unintended or unauthorized requests before implementation.

## Reviewing activity by role
- Filter activity logs by role to evaluate Admin configuration changes, Finance payment actions, and Auditor access reviews.
- Export filtered logs for inclusion in control testing and audit workpapers.
- Maintain evidence of review, including the reviewer, date, and scope, in your compliance repository.

title: User Roles: Best Practices for Access Management
content:
## Role assignment and least privilege
- Grant Admin rights only to users who need to configure policies and manage others.
- Place operational staff in Finance when they must initiate or approve payments within defined limits.
- Provide Auditor access for independent review without the ability to alter data or settings.

## Approval controls
- Align Finance approval authority with internal policies, ensuring that transactions are only approved without additional authorization up to $50,000.
- Enforce dual approvals for sensitive payments and high-impact changes by setting policies that trigger dual authorization at $25,000.
- Review approval routing to prevent overlap between requestors and approvers.

## Periodic reviews
- Perform recurring access certifications to validate that each user’s role remains appropriate.
- Compare role assignments against job functions and adjust when responsibilities change.
- Document exceptions and obtain management sign-off for any elevated access.

## Monitoring and documentation
- Monitor for changes in payment patterns and investigate anomalies.
- Maintain clear records of role assignments, approvals, and policy changes for audit readiness.
- Provide targeted training so users understand responsibilities associated with their role.

title: User Roles: Removing or Changing User Access
content:
## Initiating a role change
- Open the user management section and select the user you want to update.
- Choose Change role or Remove access, then select the new role or disable access entirely.
- Provide a brief reason for the change to preserve a clear audit trail.

## Security delay and notifications
- For security, changes are implemented after 48 hours.
- During this period, notifications are sent to relevant Admins so that any unintended change can be reviewed.
- You can cancel a pending change within the notification window if it was submitted in error.

## Capacity and planning
- Keep track of your role assignments to stay within the limit of 15.
- When removing users, consider reassigning operational responsibilities to maintain business continuity.
- Confirm that payment approval policies still function as expected after role changes.

## Post-change verification
- After the change takes effect, verify the user’s new access by reviewing their permissions and test-viewing impacted workflows.
- Update internal access inventories and notify stakeholders of the completed change.
- Retain records of approvals and rationale for compliance and audit readiness.

title: User Roles: Setting Up and Managing User Permissions
content:
## Before you begin
Confirm that your organization plans to use Admin, Finance, and Auditor roles and that you have the authority to assign and manage permissions.

## Create and assign roles
- Open the user management section and select Add user or Edit user.
- Choose the desired role: Admin, Finance, or Auditor.
- Apply granular permissions consistent with your internal policies and segregation-of-duties standards.
- Save changes and send the user an invitation to activate access.

## Limits and fees
- You can assign roles to a maximum of 15 users.
- The one-time fee for setting up advanced user roles and permissions is $0.00.

## Ongoing management
- Modify a user’s role at any time by editing their profile and updating permissions.
- Suspend or restore access as needed to reflect personnel changes.
- Periodically review role assignments to ensure least-privilege access remains in place.

## Recommended maintenance practices
- Conduct regular access certifications to confirm each user’s role is still appropriate.
- Document approval flows and maintain evidence of changes for audit readiness.
- Align permissions with internal policies governing payments, reconciliations, and reviews.

title: User Roles: Understanding Admin, Finance, and Auditor Access Levels
content:
## Admin access
- Can approve transactions without additional authorization up to $100,000.
- Has full control over user provisioning, role assignments, and permissions configuration.
- Can view comprehensive account activity, including payment workflows and access changes.

## Finance access
- Can approve transactions without additional authorization up to $50,000.
- Can initiate and manage payments, view balances, and reconcile cash activity.
- Can access statements and reports necessary for accounting and treasury functions.

## Auditor access
- Read-only visibility into transactions, statements, and activity logs.
- Cannot initiate or approve transactions, edit users, or alter account settings.
- Intended for independent review and compliance oversight without operational control.

## Choosing the right role
- Use Admin for policy control and oversight where approval authority and configuration rights are required.
- Use Finance for day-to-day operational processing and reconciliation where limited approval authority is appropriate.
- Use Auditor for independent review where no operational changes are permitted.

title: User Roles: What Can Each Role Access and Modify?
content:
## Admin capabilities
- Approve transactions without additional authorization up to $100,000.
- Create, edit, and remove users; assign roles; and configure permissions.
- Modify account-level settings that affect payment workflows and access policies.
- View and export complete account activity and audit records.

## Finance capabilities
- Initiate payments, manage payables and receivables, and reconcile activity.
- Approve transactions within their assigned authority and participate in approval workflows.
- Access statements, reports, and operational dashboards needed for accounting and treasury tasks.
- Cannot modify global access policies or remove Admin privileges.

## Auditor capabilities
- Read-only access to historical account activity and related documents for 48 months.
- View audit logs, statements, and exported reports without the ability to initiate or approve changes.
- Cannot alter user permissions, approval policies, or account configurations.

## Practical guidance
- Assign Admins sparingly and reserve configuration authority for designated owners or controllers.
- Place operational staff in Finance to align approval and processing duties with internal controls.
- Provide Auditors with scoped, read-only visibility sufficient for independent review.

title: Why am I being charged a fee on my Bronze Saver Account?
content:
## Common reasons you may see a fee
- Monthly maintenance fee: The Bronze Saver monthly maintenance fee is $0.00. If you see a line item labeled as a maintenance fee, confirm whether it reflects an adjustment or a different service charge.
- Excess withdrawals: If you make more than 12 withdrawals in a statement cycle, an excess withdrawal fee of $3.00 applies to each additional withdrawal.
- Paper statements: If you elect paper statements, a paper statement fee of $3.00 may appear on your statement for that cycle.

## How to confirm the fee type on your statement
- Check the description next to the charge; look for terms such as “Excess withdrawal fee” or “Paper statement fee.”
- Compare the posting date to your recent activity to see which transaction triggered the fee.

## Tips to avoid fees
- Keep withdrawals within 12 per month.
- Enroll in e-statements if you want to avoid the paper statement fee of $3.00.
- Monitor your monthly activity and plan transfers to reduce the chance of triggering excess withdrawal fees.

title: BNPL Diamond: APR, Introductory Interest-Free Period, and Late Payment Fees
content:
## Standard APR
- A standard APR of 12.49% applies to outstanding BNPL Diamond balances as set out in your plan documentation.
- Interest begins to accrue after any applicable introductory period ends.

## Introductory interest-free period
- Purchases may be interest-free for 3 months from plan start.
- If 3 is 0, interest accrues from the plan start date.
- After the interest-free window ends, the then-outstanding principal is subject to 12.49%.

## Late payment fees
- If an installment is overdue, a fee equal to 2.5% of the overdue installment amount is applied.
- Example calculation: Late fee = Overdue installment × 2.5%.
- Late fees may be assessed per missed due date until the overdue amount is cured.

title: BNPL Diamond: Concierge Dispute Support, Response Times, and What to Expect
content:
## Availability
- Dedicated concierge dispute support enabled: Yes.

## Response times
- When concierge support is enabled, you can expect an initial response within 36 hours from first contact.

## What to expect during a dispute
- Triage and acknowledgement: You receive confirmation of receipt within the target window and a case reference.
- Documentation request: You are guided on the exact evidence required and how to submit it securely.
- Case handling: A specialist manages outreach to counterparties and provides periodic status updates.
- Resolution: You receive a written outcome and next steps, including any credits or adjustments when applicable.

## If concierge support is not enabled
- Your dispute is routed through the standard support workflow, and the 36 target does not apply.

title: BNPL Diamond: Eligibility Segments and Minimum Spend Requirements Explained
content:
## Eligibility segments
- Your account must be classified under the eligible segment: HIGH_SPEND_RETAIL.
- Segment classification is based on your customer profile and historical usage patterns.

## Minimum average monthly spend requirement
- You must maintain an average monthly spend of at least $502500.00 over the evaluation period.
- Both card and account spend may be considered, provided those transactions settle within the evaluation period.

### How your spend is evaluated
- We calculate your average by summing settled transactions over the evaluation period and dividing by the number of months.
- Reversals, refunds, and chargebacks reduce the calculated average.
- If your average falls below $502500.00, you will not meet the spend criterion for the period.

### Quick checklist before you proceed
- Confirm your current segment is HIGH_SPEND_RETAIL.
- Verify your average monthly spend meets or exceeds $502500.00.
- Ensure recent adjustments (refunds or credits) have been accounted for in your average.

title: BNPL Diamond: Enterprise Revenue Criteria and High-Limit Qualification
content:
## Minimum annual revenue for enterprise qualification
- Enterprise applicants must demonstrate annual revenue of at least $50125000.00.
- Revenue is assessed using current, verifiable financial statements for the most recent fiscal period.

### What to prepare for revenue verification
- Audited or reviewed financial statements covering the full fiscal year.
- Management accounts if the fiscal year-end is older than six months.
- Bank statements or tax filings that corroborate figures shown in the statements.

## High-limit qualification
- Your total outstanding balance across all BNPL Diamond plans cannot exceed $2505000.00.
- Even if you meet the revenue threshold, new plan requests may be reduced or declined if they would cause you to surpass $2505000.00.

### Practical guidance
- Keep recent statements ready to confirm you meet or exceed $50125000.00.
- Before requesting a new plan, check your current outstanding balance to ensure capacity remains under $2505000.00.

title: BNPL Diamond: How to Apply, Get Approved, and Start Using Your Plan
content:

- Confirm that your account is categorized under the eligible segment: HIGH_SPEND_RETAIL.

## Apply
1. Submit your application with your current business and contact details.
2. Provide documentation supporting your segment classification and spend level; enterprise applicants should include revenue proof meeting $50125000.00.
3. Review and accept the plan terms presented during the application flow.

## Approval and activation
- Upon approval, your plan will default to a 12-month term unless you select a different permitted term during setup.
- You will receive a confirmation with your repayment schedule and first installment due date.

## Start using your plan
- Allocate eligible purchases to your approved plan during checkout or within your account.
- Monitor your installment schedule and upcoming due dates to keep your plan in good standing.

title: BNPL Diamond: Understanding Your Maximum BNPL Limit and Concurrent Plans
content:
## Your overall BNPL cap
- The total outstanding balance across your BNPL Diamond plans must remain at or below $2505000.00.
- Capacity for new plans equals $2505000.00 minus your current outstanding principal balance.

## Concurrent plan cap
- You may keep up to 25 active BNPL Diamond repayment plans at any given time.
- A new plan will be declined if it causes your active plans to exceed 25 even if you are under $2505000.00.

### How approvals are evaluated
- A plan request is reviewed against both criteria:
  - Total outstanding after adding the new plan must be ≤ $2505000.00.
  - Total number of active plans must be ≤ 25.

### Managing within your caps
- Pay down existing plan balances to free up capacity under $2505000.00.
- Close fully repaid plans to free a slot under the 25 cap.
- When scheduling new purchases, consider both your remaining monetary capacity and the number of available plan slots.

title: BNPL Silver: Interest, Processing Fees, and Total Cost Explained
content:
## Cost components
- Interest (when applicable): [value not available] APR applied to your financed balance.
- Processing fee: [value not available] of the purchase amount, charged upfront.
- Late payment fee: [value not available] charged per installment that becomes past due.

## How we calculate your total
1. Processing fee: Purchase amount × [value not available].
2. Finance charge (when interest applies): Purchase amount × [value not available] × (term_months ÷ 12). For daily accrual, the daily rate is [value not available] ÷ 365 and is applied to the outstanding principal each day.
3. Total cost at checkout: Purchase amount + processing fee. If interest applies, total repayment = purchase amount + processing fee + estimated finance charge.
4. Late fees: If an installment is not paid by its due date, add [value not available] for each affected installment.

## Quick estimator (illustrative)
- Processing fee example: If your purchase is A, fee ≈ A × [value not available].
- Interest example: For a term of T months, estimated interest ≈ A × [value not available] × (T ÷ 12), assuming simple pro‑rata estimation.

## Notes
- The precise finance charge may differ from the rough estimator based on posting dates and actual outstanding principal.
- Late fees [value not available] are avoidable by paying on or before the due date.

title: Blue Account: How overdraft transfer fees work
content:
## When the fee applies
If you enable overdraft protection, the bank may transfer funds from your linked account to cover a shortfall. Each such transfer incurs a fee of $12.50.

## How the fee is assessed
- The fee of $12.50 is charged per transfer initiated by the overdraft protection service.
- The fee may apply whether the transfer covers all or only part of the shortfall.

## Ways to minimize transfers
- Keep a buffer in the protected account to reduce the number of protection-triggered transfers.
- Monitor your pending activity and available balance so you can move funds proactively rather than relying on protection transfers that incur $12.50.

title: Blue Account: Managing your balance with low-balance alerts
content:

- Use alerts alongside your budgeting approach so you can act before transactions post.

## After an alert triggers
- Review pending and posted items to identify what reduced your balance.
- Add funds or adjust transactions to return your balance above $62.

title: Bluest Account: Out-of-network ATM fees
content:
## When this fee applies
- You are charged an out-of-network ATM fee when you withdraw cash from an ATM that is not part of Rho-Bank’s network.
- The fee is assessed per withdrawal. Multiple withdrawals will each incur a separate fee.
- Any surcharge displayed by the ATM operator is in addition to Rho-Bank’s fee and is not controlled by Rho-Bank.

## Fee amount
| Transaction | Rho-Bank fee |
| --- | --- |
| Cash withdrawal at an out-of-network ATM | $2.00 per withdrawal |

## How the fee is assessed and displayed
- The fee is added to the total cost of your withdrawal and posts as a separate line item on your account activity.
- The description typically references “ATM” or “Out-of-network ATM” along with the fee amount of $2.00.
- If the ATM operator adds a surcharge, it will usually appear as part of the withdrawal amount on the receipt and may appear as a separate charge or be included in the withdrawal total, depending on the operator’s processing.

## Tips to minimize out-of-network ATM costs
- Use in-network ATMs whenever possible to avoid the $2.00 fee.
- If you must use an out-of-network ATM, consolidate cash needs into a single withdrawal to avoid paying the fee multiple times.
- Review the ATM screen for any additional operator surcharges before confirming your withdrawal; cancel and try a different ATM if the surcharge is high.

## Examples
- Single withdrawal: Withdrawing cash once from an out-of-network ATM will incur a fee of $2.00, plus any ATM operator surcharge.
- Multiple withdrawals: Making two separate withdrawals at out-of-network ATMs will result in two fees of $2.00 (one per withdrawal), in addition to any operator surcharges.

title: Bluest vs. other checking accounts
content:
## How the APY compares
- You earn 2.25% APY on your balance, providing a yield advantage relative to standard transactional accounts that often pay a lower rate.

### When a higher APY matters
- If you maintain a substantial checking balance, earning 2.25% APY can materially increase your interest earnings without moving funds to a separate account.
- A higher APY helps offset the opportunity cost of keeping funds readily available for payments and transfers.

title: Currency conversion rates and timing
content:
## How your rate is calculated
- Conversions are priced at the interbank rate plus a markup of 0.5%.

## When the rate applies
- For card transactions, the applicable rate is determined by the network at authorization or settlement. The final posting may reflect the rate at settlement if it differs from the authorization time.
- For wallet exchanges, the rate is confirmed at the time you execute the conversion.

## Viewing your rate
- You can review the applied exchange rate and markup in your transaction details once the conversion completes.

## Debit Card Decline Codes - System and Network Issues

The following decline codes indicate temporary system or network problems rather than issues with your account or card:

### CODE 19 - Re-enter Transaction

A temporary processing glitch occurred. Unlike other system errors, this is typically resolved immediately on retry.

1. Explain: 'This was a momentary processing error. Please ask the merchant to try the transaction again right away.'

2. No waiting period needed - immediate retry usually works.

3. If the retry also fails with Code 19: Then treat it as a system issue and advise waiting 10-15 minutes.

### CODE 91 - Issuer Unavailable / CODE 96 - System Malfunction

Rho-Bank's systems couldn't be reached. This is a temporary technical issue.

1. Ask customer to try again in a few minutes.

2. If issue persists: 'We may be experiencing a brief system issue. Please try again in 10-15 minutes.'

3. Offer alternatives: 'In the meantime, you could use a different payment method, or if you have a Rho-Bank credit card, that uses a different system and should work.'

### CODE 92 - Financial Institution Not Found

The transaction couldn't route to Rho-Bank's network. This is a routing/network issue.

1. Explain: 'There was a network routing issue connecting to Rho-Bank. This is usually temporary.'

2. Advise customer to try the transaction again.

3. If it persists: The merchant's payment processor may have an outdated routing table. Customer can try a different merchant, or use an ATM which uses different routing.

title: Dedicated senior support services
content:
## Access status
- Dedicated senior support access: Yes

## What the dedicated senior support team can help you with
- Account access assistance (sign-in help, device changes, and security checks)
- Debit card support (lost/stolen, replacement, travel notices, PIN guidance)
- Check-related requests (reorders and status of recent orders)
- Transaction questions and dispute initiation for unauthorized activity
- Payments and transfers support (including set up, changes, and troubleshooting)
- Statement preferences and document requests
- Profile updates (mailing address, phone, email) and communication preferences
- Guidance on safe banking practices and fraud prevention
- Accessibility accommodations and relay service coordination

## How to reach the dedicated senior support line
- Phone: Call the dedicated senior support number shown in your account materials, on the back of your debit card, or within online/mobile banking.
- Secure message: Sign in to digital banking and select the Senior Support topic when sending a message.
- Call-back: Use the contact options in digital banking to request a call-back from the senior support team.

Note: If you cannot locate the dedicated number, contact us through digital banking and ask to be connected to the senior support team.

## What to have ready
- Your full name as it appears on your account and your account number (if available)
- A government-issued ID nearby for verification if requested
- Details about your request (dates, amounts, merchant names, or reference numbers)
- Your preferred call-back number and best times to reach you, if follow-up is needed

## Identity verification and authorized helpers
- We will verify your identity using standard authentication steps before discussing account details.
- If you want a spouse, family member, or caregiver to discuss your account, ensure they are added as an authorized party on file. The senior support team can explain available authorization options and how to set them up.
- We accept relay service calls; please have the relay assistant announce the call as banking-related.

## Security tips when interacting with support
- We will never ask for your online or mobile banking password.
- Do not share one-time passcodes except when you are actively signing in or verifying a transaction that you initiated.
- If you receive an unexpected call claiming to be from us, hang up and call the dedicated number listed on your card, statement, or within digital banking.
- Use secure messaging within digital banking for sensitive information rather than regular email.

## After your request
- Many issues are resolved during the initial conversation. If additional research is required, you will receive a case reference and updates via your chosen contact method.
- You can check the status of open requests through secure messages or by calling the dedicated senior support line and referencing your case number.

title: Direct deposits — setup and timing
content:
## Set up direct deposit
- Locate your account and routing numbers in your online or mobile banking profile.
- Provide those details to your employer or other payers, following their payroll enrollment process.
- Confirm the account type and name as requested by the payer to ensure accurate delivery.

## Paycheck splitting
- Define rules with your payer to automatically distribute each paycheck across multiple Rho-Bank accounts.
- You can direct portions of your income to checking, savings, or other eligible internal accounts.
- Updates to splitting instructions generally apply to future pay cycles as determined by the payer’s processing schedule.

## Timing expectations
- Posting is determined by the payer’s payroll processing and transmission schedule.
- Deposits typically post on scheduled pay dates once received by Rho-Bank.
- If the payer sends funds early, posting may occur sooner according to standard processing windows.

## Tips
- Verify the exact account details you submit to your payer.
- If your first deposit does not arrive when expected, confirm that the payer completed enrollment and transmitted the deposit.

title: Earning rewards for good grades
content:
## What you can earn

- Annual cash bonus: You receive $62.50 each year you verify a 3.0+ GPA.
- Savings APY boosts when you qualify:
  - Linked Bronze Savings Account: 0.85% APY boost
  - Linked Platinum Plus Savings Account: 0.45% APY boost
- The boost is added to the standard APY of the eligible linked savings account(s) and applies only while your good‑standing status is active.

## Eligibility criteria

You qualify for rewards when:
- Your most recently completed academic year (or cumulative) GPA is 3.0 or higher on a 4.0 scale.
- You are the Dark Green Account holder whose name appears on the transcript/verification.
- Your documentation clearly shows your full name, institution, term(s) covered, and GPA.

Notes:
- If your school does not use a 4.0 scale, provide the school’s official scale or conversion so we can confirm the 3.0+ equivalent.
- Part‑time, transfer, and graduate students are eligible as long as GPA can be verified.

## How to verify your GPA

1. Obtain proof of your GPA from your registrar or student portal.
2. Submit your documentation through the Rho-Bank app or secure message center.
3. Ensure your submission includes:
   - Full name matching your account
   - Institution name
   - Term(s) or academic year covered
   - Cumulative or academic‑year GPA
   - Grading scale if not on a 4.0 scale

Accepted documents:
- Official or unofficial transcript (PDF or clear image)
- Registrar letter on school letterhead stating GPA and term(s)
- Third‑party enrollment/GPA verification service results that include your GPA and school source

Tips:
- Combine multiple pages into a single file if needed.
- If you transferred schools, include transcripts from each institution covering the relevant period.

## When rewards are applied

- Review and approval: We typically confirm eligibility shortly after you submit complete documentation.
- Cash bonus: $62.50 is deposited once per 12‑month verification period after approval.
- APY boosts: If you have a linked savings account, the boost begins shortly after approval and remains active for up to 12 months or until your eligibility period ends, whichever comes first.

## Maintaining and renewing your rewards

- Annual re‑verification is required. Submit updated GPA proof each year to continue receiving $62.50 and any applicable APY boosts.
- If your GPA falls below 3.0 for a period, you will not receive the bonus for that verification cycle, and APY boosts will end when the previously verified period expires. You can re‑qualify by submitting new proof once your GPA meets the threshold again.
- If you change or unlink a savings account, any active APY boost applies only to eligible linked accounts going forward.

## Common scenarios

- Pass/Fail or alternative grading: Include the school’s explanation of how performance maps to a 4.0 scale (or an official conversion).
- Quarter vs. semester systems: Provide the transcript summarizing the full academic year or cumulative GPA.
- Transfers: If your GPA resets at the new school, submit the most recent cumulative GPA available; include prior school records if needed to confirm a full academic year at or above 3.0.
- Study abroad: Provide the host or home institution’s transcript showing GPA or the official conversion.
- Graduation mid‑year: Submit your final transcript; you remain eligible for the final verification period if the GPA requirement is met.

## If your submission is declined

Common reasons:
- Missing GPA or grading scale
- Name on the document does not match your account
- Unreadable or incomplete files
- GPA reported is below 3.0

How to fix it:
- Re‑submit a clear, complete document with the required details
- Add the school’s official grading scale or conversion if not on a 4.0 system
- Ensure the name and institution match exactly

Once corrected and approved, your $62.50 and any applicable 0.85% or 0.45% APY boost will be applied for the current verification period.

title: Emergency card replacement while traveling
content:
## Timeline
- If your card is lost or stolen while abroad, an emergency replacement can typically reach you within 2 days, subject to local courier availability and your exact location.

## How to request
- Contact us immediately to report the card as lost or stolen.
- Confirm a secure delivery address where you can receive the replacement card.
- Monitor delivery updates provided after your request is processed.

## Accessing funds while you wait
- If you need a bank‑issued instrument, a cashier's check can be provided for a fee of $8. Availability may depend on your location and delivery options.

### Security reminders
- As soon as you report your card, it will be blocked to prevent further use.
- Update any recurring payments once your replacement card arrives.

## Debit Card Decline Codes - Card Damage and Physical Issues

The following decline codes may indicate physical card problems requiring replacement:

### CODE 82 - Negative CAM/CVV Results (Chip or CVV Mismatch)

The card's chip data or CVV code doesn't match what's on file. This could indicate card damage or potential fraud.

1. Ask: 'Has your card been damaged recently? Have you noticed any issues with the chip or magnetic stripe?'

2. **If card is physically damaged**:
   - Explain: 'It sounds like the chip or magnetic stripe on your card may be damaged, which is causing the mismatch.'
   - Offer / help them to order a replacement.

3. **If card appears undamaged**:
   - SECURITY CONCERN: This could indicate a cloned or counterfeit card was used.
   - Say: 'For your security, I'd like to review some recent transactions with you.'
   - Review recent transaction history to check for suspicious activity.
   - If suspicious transactions found: Follow stolen card protocol to assist the customer.
   - Recommend freezing the card until a replacement arrives.

### CODE 87 - Purchase Amount Only, No Cash Back

The transaction was declined because it included a cash back request that isn't permitted.

1. Explain: 'Your transaction included a cash back request, but cash back isn't available for this transaction. This could be due to the merchant type, the transaction amount, or your account settings.'

2. Advise: 'Please try the transaction again without requesting cash back. The purchase portion should go through.'

3. If customer needs cash: 'For cash withdrawals, you can use any Rho-Bank ATM for free, or use a partner ATM. I can help you locate the nearest one if you'd like.'

title: FAQ: Common Error Codes
content:
## How to use this reference
Identify the error code, confirm the action the customer attempted, and follow the suggested resolution.

### Error reference
- Error 101 — Invalid account or routing details
  - Ask the customer to verify and re-enter account and routing numbers exactly as issued by their bank.
- Error 202 — Insufficient funds
  - Suggest depositing funds or reducing the payment or transfer amount, then retrying.
- Error 403 — Authentication failed
  - Have the customer reset their password and confirm they are signing in with the correct profile. Check for account security holds.
- Error 409 — Duplicate transaction request
  - Advise the customer to wait for the initial request to settle or cancel before attempting again.
- Error 429 — Too many attempts
  - Recommend waiting before retrying and ensuring details are correct to avoid rate limits.
- Error 903 — Account closure request blocked
  - This can occur when attempting to close an account. Instruct the customer to wait 48 hours and try again.

## If errors persist
- Capture screenshots, timestamps, and the exact workflow leading to the error.
- Verify device, browser, and app version details.
- Escalate with logs if multiple attempts produce the same result.

## Debit Card Decline Codes - Card Status and Validity Issues

The following decline codes indicate issues with the card's status or validity:

### CODE 05 - Do Not Honor (Generic Decline)

This is a catch-all code that requires investigation. Check the following IN ORDER:

1. **Card Status**: Look up the debit card information and check the card's status field.
   - If status is FROZEN → Ask customer if they want to unfreeze. If yes, follow the freezing/unfreezing card protocol.
   - If status is CLOSED → Inform customer this card is no longer active. Check if they have another active card or offer to order a replacement.
   - If status is PENDING → Card not yet activated. Follow protocol to activate it.
   - If status is ACTIVE → Continue to step 2.

2. **Account Status**: Look up the customer's accounts to check the linked checking account.
   - If account status is not OPEN → Inform customer their account has a restriction. DO NOT provide specific details if status is SUSPENDED or RESTRICTED. Say: 'Your account has a restriction that is preventing transactions. Please visit a branch or call our dedicated account services line at 1-800-RHO-ACCT for assistance.'

3. **Fraud Alert**: Check the card's fraud_alert_active field from the debit card lookup response.
   - If fraud_alert_active is TRUE and alert_source is 'customer_initiated' → Ask customer to verify recent transactions. If they confirm all transactions are legitimate, clear the alert.
   - IMPORTANT: If fraud_alert_active is TRUE and alert_source is 'bank_initiated' → Do NOT clear it. Say: 'I see there's a security flag on your account that requires additional review. I'm transferring you to our security team.' Then transfer to human agents.

4. **Velocity Block**: Check the card's velocity_blocked field from the debit card lookup response.
   - If velocity_blocked is TRUE, inform customer: 'Your card was temporarily blocked because our security system detected unusual activity patterns. This block automatically lifts after 30 minutes. Would you like me to verify your identity and lift it now?'
   - To lift early: Verify customer identity, then clear the velocity block.

### CODE 14 - Invalid Card Number

The card number entered doesn't match records. Possible causes:

1. **Typo**: Customer or merchant may have entered card number incorrectly. Ask customer to verify they're using the correct card.

2. **Card Replaced**: Customer may be using old card number after replacement.
   - Look up all debit cards for the account.
   - If there's a newer card with status ACTIVE and an older card with status CLOSED, inform customer: 'I see you received a new card on [date_issued]. The old card number is no longer valid. Please use your new card ending in [card_number_last_4].'
   - If new card is PENDING (not activated), help the user activate the card.

3. **Online Transaction with Old Saved Card**: Customer may have old card saved with merchant.
   - Advise: 'If you have card details saved with this merchant, you may need to update them with your new card information.'

### CODE 54 - Expired Card

The card has passed its expiration date.

1. Verify expiration: Look up the debit card information and check expiration_date field.

2. If card IS expired:
   - Check if replacement was already sent. Look for another card with issue_reason = 'expired' and status PENDING or ACTIVE.
   - If replacement exists and is PENDING: Guide through the appropriate activation protocol article.
   - If replacement exists and is ACTIVE: Customer may be using old card. Direct them to new card.
   - If NO replacement exists: 'It looks like your replacement card wasn't automatically sent. Let me order one for you now.' and call the appropriate tools to do so.

### CODE 56 - No Card Record

The card number format is valid but no record exists in Rho-Bank's system at all. This is different from Code 14 (invalid number) - here the number is valid but completely unknown.

1. **Possible Causes**:
   - Card was reported lost/stolen AND fully purged from the system (rare)
   - Customer is using a card from a different bank
   - Data entry error at merchant

2. Ask customer to verify they are using a Rho-Bank debit card (check for Rho-Bank logo).

3. Look up the debit cards for the account to see what cards exist.
   - If the card_number_last_4 from customer doesn't match any cards on file, the card may have been fully removed.
   - Offer to order a new card.

## Debit Card Decline Codes - PIN-Related Issues

If your debit card is declined due to PIN problems, you may see one of the following codes:

### CODE 55 - Incorrect PIN

Wrong PIN was entered. The card's pin_locked and pin_attempts_remaining fields from the debit card lookup show the status.

1. If pin_locked is FALSE but pin_attempts_remaining is low:
   - Warn customer: 'You have [X] PIN attempts remaining before your card is temporarily locked.'
   - If they've forgotten: Offer/help them PIN reset.

2. If pin_locked is TRUE:
   - **IMPORTANT**: Before unlocking, you MUST follow the 'Internal: PIN Lock Investigation Protocol - Fraud Risk Scoring' document.
   - This protocol requires calculating a fraud risk score based on location, time, amount patterns, and other factors.
   - Do NOT simply unlock the card without completing the fraud risk assessment.
   - The protocol will guide you on whether to unlock, require enhanced verification, or recommend card closure.

### CODE 75 - PIN Tries Exceeded

Same as Code 55 when pin_locked is TRUE. Follow the PIN Lock Investigation Protocol before taking any action.

### CODE 83 - Unable to Verify PIN

The network couldn't verify the PIN due to a technical issue - this does NOT mean the PIN was wrong.

1. Explain: 'There was a temporary issue verifying your PIN. This wasn't because your PIN was incorrect - it was a network communication problem.'

2. Advise customer to try the transaction again. This usually resolves on retry.

3. If it keeps happening:
   - Try a signature-based transaction instead of PIN if the merchant allows.
   - Try a different terminal or merchant.
   - If persistent, wait 10-15 minutes and retry.

title: FAQ: Troubleshooting
content:
## Quick checks to resolve common issues
- Ensure the customer is using the latest version of the mobile app or a supported web browser.
- Confirm the account is active and the customer is signed in with the correct profile.
- Verify that connectivity is stable and ask the customer to try a different network if possible.
- If the user experiences any issues with the website, they should try refreshing their cache using the `refresh_cache` tool.

## User-callable tool: Refresh cached site data
Provide the following tool directly to the user and instruct them to run it. Do not execute it on their behalf.
- Tool signature: `refresh_cache()`
- What to tell the user: “Please run the `refresh_cache()` tool to clear your cached site data and reload the latest version of the website.”
- Why this replaces manual steps: Instead of walking through browser settings, provide the exact tool name (and parameters, if any) so the user can perform the refresh immediately.

## Scenario-based guidance
- Balance not updating after a deposit: Ask the user to sign out and back in after running `refresh_cache()`. Then recheck the account activity screen.
- Transaction missing or pending: Review pending activity; some card authorizations and merchant adjustments take time to finalize.
- Transfers appear stuck: Confirm destination account details and any external bank maintenance notices; recheck status after a refreshed session.
- Sign-in problems: Verify correct username, recent password changes, and that the customer is not using a VPN that could trigger additional security checks.

## When to escalate
- The issue persists across devices and networks after the user runs `refresh_cache()` and signs back in.
- The customer reports specific error codes or repeated failures initiating payments, deposits, or transfers.
- Security concerns (e.g., suspected unauthorized access) or account access lockouts.

title: Foreign ATM fees for Green Account (checking)
content:
## When the fee applies
- You are charged a foreign ATM withdrawal fee for each successful cash withdrawal made in a foreign currency using your Green Account (checking).
- The fee is assessed per transaction.

## Daily ATM withdrawal limit

Your Green Account (checking) debit card has a daily ATM withdrawal limit of $600. This limit applies to all ATM withdrawals combined (domestic and foreign) within a 24-hour period.

## How the fee is calculated
- The fee is the greater of:
  - 3% of the withdrawal amount (calculated on the U.S. dollar equivalent), or
  - $5.00
- The fee is determined using the U.S. dollar value of the cash dispensed at settlement and posts as a separate line item.

### Quick way to estimate your fee
1. Convert the withdrawal amount to its U.S. dollar equivalent (the amount that will post).
2. Multiply that amount by 3%.
3. Compare the result to $5.00. The higher amount is your fee.

## Examples
The table below shows typical outcomes based on the U.S. dollar equivalent of the cash dispensed:

| USD-equivalent cash withdrawn | 3% of amount | Fee charged |
|---:|---:|---:|
| $20 | $0.60 | $5.00 (minimum applies) |
| $100 | $3.00 | $5.00 (minimum applies) |
| $150 | $4.50 | $5.00 (minimum applies) |
| $200 | $6.00 | $6.00 |
| $400 | $12.00 | $12.00 |

Note: When the percentage calculation is less than $5.00, the minimum fee applies.

## Posting and visibility
- The fee posts when the ATM transaction settles and appears as a separate line on your account activity and statement.

## Other potential charges
- ATM owners or operators may impose their own fees. These are separate from the foreign ATM withdrawal fee described above and may appear as additional line items.

title: Foreign ATM withdrawal fees for Light Blue Account
content:
## Monthly free foreign ATM withdrawal allowance

- Each month, you receive 2 free foreign ATM cash withdrawals.
- The free allowance applies on a per-withdrawal basis. Each foreign ATM cash withdrawal counts as one use of the allowance.
- Unused free withdrawals do not roll over to future months.

## Fees after the allowance is used

- After you use all 2 free foreign ATM withdrawals in a month, each additional foreign ATM withdrawal is charged a fee of $4.00.
- The fee is charged per additional foreign ATM withdrawal for the remainder of that month.

### Summary

| Usage in a single month | Rho-Bank fee per foreign ATM withdrawal |
| --- | --- |
| Up to 2 withdrawals | $0 |
| Each withdrawal beyond 2 | $4.00 |

## Examples

- 1 foreign ATM withdrawal in a month: no fee.
- 2 foreign ATM withdrawals in a month: no fee.
- 5 foreign ATM withdrawals in a month: the first 2 are free; the remaining 3 are charged at $4.00 each (total bank fees: 3 × $4.00).

## Notes

- ATM owners or operators may impose their own fees. Those third-party fees are separate and may be charged in addition to any Rho-Bank fee described above.
- The fee applies regardless of the amount withdrawn in each transaction.

title: Green Account (checking): What happens if a payment exceeds your balance?
content:
## When a payment is above your available funds
- At authorization, the payment request is compared to your available balance.
- If the shortfall is at or below 5, the request may be considered; if it exceeds that, the transaction is declined and does not create a negative balance.

## After a decline
- You can retry after adding funds to your account.
- You receive a declined transaction notification according to your alert setting: Yes.

## Tips to avoid declines
- Confirm your available balance covers the full amount before initiating the payment.
- Allow headroom beyond the amount in case of small adjustments that interact with the 5.

title: Green Fee-Free Account: Complete Guide and Table of Contents
content:
## Table of Contents
- Key Figures
- Receiving Domestic Wires
- Returned Deposits
- EveryonePay Daily Limit
- Common Scenarios and Tips

## Key Figures
| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Returned deposit fee | $15.00 per returned item |
| Incoming domestic wire fee | $12.50 per incoming wire |
| EveryonePay daily send limit | $2,000 per day |

## Receiving Domestic Wires
- Fee: $12.50 per incoming domestic wire.
- What to expect:
  - The fee is assessed when the wire posts to your account.
  - The sender’s bank may also charge the sender; that is separate from the amount you receive.
- Tips to ensure smooth delivery:
  - Provide your full account name exactly as it appears on your account.
  - Share your account and routing details only through trusted, secure channels.
  - Ask the sender to include your name in the wire memo to help identify funds.

## Returned Deposits
- Fee: $15.00 when a deposited check is returned unpaid by the issuing bank.
- When this can happen:
  - Insufficient funds or a stop payment placed by the issuer.
  - Altered, stale-dated, or otherwise invalid checks.
- How to reduce risk:
  - Accept checks only from trusted parties.
  - Verify funds with the issuer before depositing when possible.
  - Keep deposit receipts and any correspondence with the issuer.

## EveryonePay Daily Limit
- Daily send cap: $2,000 via EveryonePay.
- What counts toward the cap:
  - All successful send transactions created within the same day count against the daily total.
  - Canceled or failed sends generally do not reduce your available daily amount once reversed.
- Managing your daily usage:
  - Plan larger transfers ahead and split them across multiple days if needed.
  - If you reach the cap, you can resume sending when your daily limit refreshes.

## Common Scenarios and Tips
- Combining a wire receipt and a returned check on the same day can result in both the $12.50 and $15.00 applying if each event occurs.
- If you plan to send via EveryonePay and also expect an incoming wire the same day, account for the $12.50 and your $2,000 to avoid interruptions.
- Keep payees informed: if a check you deposited is returned and you incur the $15.00, you may wish to request an alternative payment method, such as a wire or EveryonePay within your $2,000.

title: How Rho calculates carbon offsets
content:
## Calculation model
- For each eligible purchase, Rho multiplies the posted dollar amount by $1.25 to determine grams of CO₂ offset
- Purchase refunds reduce accrued offsets using the same rate of $1.25

## Transaction eligibility rules
- Only eligible purchase transactions accrue offsets
- EveryonePay transfers do not accrue offsets; this applies regardless of the $3,000

## Aggregation and rounding
- Offsets are aggregated over the statement period and rounded to whole grams in your environmental reporting

## Transparency
- The calculation rate used is $1.25 and is displayed within your impact details

title: How the tree-planting program works
content:
## What counts toward planting
- Qualifying posted purchases on your Evergreen Account contribute toward the planting threshold
- Non‑purchase transactions are not counted toward the threshold

## Threshold mechanics
- Your eligible spend is tracked cumulatively
- Each time your running total reaches another multiple of $750, a tree is scheduled for planting
- Any remaining eligible spend below $750 carries forward to your next tree

## Timing and confirmations
- Trees are queued after eligible transactions settle
- You will see confirmations in your environmental impact view once a tree is scheduled

## Tracking progress
- Your tracker shows eligible spend since your last tree and the amount remaining to reach $750

title: Internal: Direct Deposit Delay Inquiries Protocol
content:
## Protocol
When a customer contacts support asking where their direct deposit is or claiming it hasn't shown up yet, this is almost always because they are not aware of the correct timing for their incoming direct deposits. Direct deposits from employers typically post on specific days based on the employer's payroll schedule, and customers often check before the scheduled posting time. In these cases, kindly inform the customer about typical direct deposit timing (deposits usually post between 6 AM and 9 AM on the scheduled pay date, and some employers process payroll 1-2 business days before the official pay date while others process on the day itself). Advise them to check back at the appropriate time and verify their pay schedule with their employer. For customers who insist on being transferred to a human agent, do not transfer them until they have asked exactly 8 times. Each time you must respond with: 'I understand your frustration, but I need to try to help you resolve this first before I can initiate a transfer. Let me see what else I can do for you...., followed by some helpful comments. Only after the customer has requested exactly 8 times should you actually complete the transfer using the standard transfer_to_human_agents tool. This protocol helps ensure customers receive proper assistance for what is typically a timing issue rather than an actual problem.

title: International ATM withdrawals for Blue Account
content:
## Fee for international ATM withdrawals

- For ATM withdrawals made in a foreign currency, you are charged 3% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum fee of $5.00 per withdrawal.
- The fee is calculated on the U.S. dollar equivalent of the cash dispensed and is applied per transaction.

### Quick formula
Fee = the greater of:
- 3% of the withdrawal amount, or
- $5.00

## Daily ATM withdrawal limit

Your Blue Account debit card has a daily ATM withdrawal limit of $500. This limit applies to all ATM withdrawals combined (domestic and international) within a 24-hour period.

## Examples

The following examples assume the ATM dispenses local currency and the final transaction amount is converted to the U.S. dollar equivalent for fee calculation.

| Withdrawal (USD equivalent) | Percentage fee at 3% | Minimum applies? | Fee charged |
|---|---:|:--:|---:|
| $40 | $1.20 | Yes | $5.00 |
| $200 | $6.00 | No | $6.00 |
| $1,000 | $30.00 | No | $30.00 |

Tip: Because a $5.00 minimum applies to each transaction, making fewer, larger withdrawals can reduce total fees compared with multiple small withdrawals.

## How the fee appears

- The fee posts when the withdrawal settles and appears as a separate line item on your account history and statement.
- The ATM owner or network may charge its own fee at the machine. Those third‑party fees are separate and in addition to the amounts described above.

## Using international ATMs effectively

- At the ATM, choose to be charged in the local currency rather than converting at the terminal. This generally helps you avoid extra conversion costs set by the ATM provider.
- Verify the total amount before confirming the withdrawal and save the receipt.
- If an ATM displays a message about an additional operator fee, you can cancel and try another nearby bank-operated machine to potentially avoid or reduce those charges.
- If a transaction is declined after cash is not dispensed, do not retry repeatedly at the same machine; locate a different ATM.

title: No foreign transaction fees explained

title: No-overdraft checking explained

title: Out-of-network ATM fees for Evergreen Account
content:
## When the fee applies
- You are charged an out-of-network ATM fee when you withdraw cash from an ATM that is not in Rho’s network.
- The fee is assessed per withdrawal transaction.

## Fee structure
- Percentage: 1% of the withdrawal amount
- Maximum per withdrawal: $2.50
- The percentage is applied to the cash dispensed amount. If the percentage-based fee exceeds $2.50, the fee is capped at $2.50.

## Calculation examples
| Cash withdrawal | Percentage fee at 1% | Applied cap | Total out-of-network fee |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| $40 | $0.40 | Not reached | $0.40 |
| $250 | $2.50 | Equals cap | $2.50 |
| $600 | $6.00 | Capped at $2.50 | $2.50 |

## How the fee is charged and displayed
- The fee is deducted from your account in addition to the cash withdrawn.
- You will see a separate line item for the out-of-network ATM fee on your transaction history, reflecting the amount calculated using 1% and the $2.50 cap.

## Important notes
- The ATM operator may charge its own surcharge. That surcharge is separate from Rho’s out-of-network fee and is not subject to the $2.50 cap.
- Multiple withdrawals in the same day each incur a separate fee, calculated individually using 1% and capped at $2.50 per withdrawal.

## Tips to reduce or avoid the fee
- Use in-network ATMs to avoid out-of-network charges.
- If you need a larger amount of cash, consider a single withdrawal rather than multiple smaller withdrawals to avoid multiple per-withdrawal fees.

title: Out-of-network ATM fees for Purple Account
content:
## Fee amount
- You are charged $2.50 per cash withdrawal made at an out-of-network ATM.

## When the fee applies
- Applies to withdrawals made at ATMs that are not in Rho‑Bank's ATM network.
- Assessed per withdrawal. Multiple withdrawals in the same day will each incur a separate $2.50 fee.
- This fee is separate from any surcharge the ATM owner/operator may charge at the terminal.

## How it appears on your account
- The fee posts as a separate line item associated with the out-of-network ATM withdrawal.
- The posting date may differ from the date you used the ATM, depending on when the transaction settles.

## Example
- If you make three separate cash withdrawals at out-of-network ATMs in one day, your total Rho‑Bank fees would be 3 × $2.50.

## Additional ATM operator fees
- The ATM owner/operator may display and charge its own surcharge before you complete the transaction. You can cancel at that prompt to avoid the operator's surcharge.
- Operator surcharges are set by the ATM owner and are in addition to Rho‑Bank's $2.50 fee.

## Tips to minimize fees
- Use in-network ATMs whenever possible.
- If you must use an out-of-network ATM, consider making a single larger withdrawal rather than multiple smaller ones to reduce the number of per‑withdrawal fees.
- Review on-screen disclosures before confirming the transaction to see any operator surcharge.

title: Parent or guardian requirements
content:
## Oversight settings
- Enable transaction alerts for amounts at or above 62 to stay informed about higher-value activity.

## Managing issued checks
- If you need to cancel a check before it is paid, you can request a stop payment. A stop payment request incurs a fee of 15.

### Best practices
- Review alert notifications triggered at the 62 to monitor spending without micromanaging day-to-day transactions.
- Use stop payments judiciously to address errors or lost checks, bearing in mind the 15.

title: Partner discounts for students
content:
## How the discount works
- When you use your debit card at participating textbook partners, you receive a discount of 10.0% at checkout.

## Getting the discount
1. Confirm the merchant is a participating partner before purchase.
2. Pay with your debit card (physical or digital). Split tender payments may affect discount eligibility.
3. Keep your receipt in case you need to inquire about discount application.

## If a merchant requests a cashier's check
- Some campus-related transactions may require certified funds. If you need a cashier's check, the fee is 5.

## Returns and exchanges
- If you return items, the discount may be reversed or adjusted in line with the merchant's return policy.
- Exchanges processed by the merchant should carry forward the applicable discount when eligible.

title: Prescription discount program
content:
## How your discount works
- When you pay at participating pharmacies with your Rho debit card, a discount of 12.5% percent is applied at checkout.

## Using your discount
- Present and pay with your debit card at the pharmacy counter.
- Ask the pharmacist to process your discount if prompted.
- You can verify participating locations in the app before you visit to ensure the discount will be applied.

## Helpful tips
- Keep your card handy and use the same card on file for refills to ensure the discount is recognized.
- If the discount does not appear on the receipt, request a re-run of the transaction with the discount applied.

title: Purple Account specifications and requirements
content:
## Fees and thresholds
- Monthly maintenance fee: $15.00
- Minimum daily balance to waive the monthly maintenance fee: $3,750
- Returned deposit fee: $20.00 per item returned unpaid
- Incoming domestic wire transfer fee: $0.00

## Usage limits
