title: Silver Rewards Card: Understanding Your Included Travel Insurance Coverage
content:
## Coverage Overview
- Per-trip coverage limit: up to $15,000
- Covered incident types: up to 5 categories (for example, trip cancellation or interruption, delays, lost or delayed baggage, or similar covered events)

## Key Eligibility Conditions
- Charge the covered travel fare in full to your card for coverage to apply
- Coverage applies per trip and is subject to policy terms, exclusions, and documentation requirements

## What Is Typically Covered
- Non-refundable, prepaid travel expenses when a covered event occurs
- Essential items during eligible baggage delay
- Reasonable additional costs due to covered delays

## What Is Typically Not Covered
- Known events or foreseen issues at the time of booking
- Non-covered reasons for cancellation or changes
- Losses without required documentation (e.g., carrier delay notices, receipts)

## How Benefits Are Applied
- Reimbursements are capped per trip at $15,000 and depend on the specific covered incident type among the up to 5 categories
- Benefits may be secondary to other applicable coverage you hold

## Proof You Should Keep
- Original itineraries and tickets
- Receipts for additional expenses
- Official delay/cancellation notices or property irregularity reports (for baggage issues)

title: Silver Rewards Card: Which Travel Purchases Qualify for 4% Cash Back?
content:
## Travel Purchases That Typically Qualify
You earn 4.0% back when the transaction is categorized as travel. Examples include:
- Airlines and airfare booked directly with carriers
- Hotels, resorts, and motels
- Car rentals and car-sharing agencies
- Passenger rail (intercity trains), ferries, and long-distance bus operators
- Online travel agencies and tour operators when they are the merchant of record
- Rideshare and taxis when processed under a travel category

## Situations That May Not Qualify
- In-flight or onboard purchases coded as retail or food rather than travel
- Vacation rentals or home-sharing platforms coded under real estate or “miscellaneous” categories
- Parking garages, tolls, or local transit if the merchant is not classified as travel
- Purchases made through third-party payment processors that mask the travel category

## Tips to Ensure Eligibility
- Book directly with travel providers or well-known travel agencies that act as merchant of record
- Review your statement after posting; if an eligible trip did not earn at the travel rate, contact support with receipts for a category review

title: Submitting a Cash Back Dispute (Internal)
content:
## When to use this
Use this process when a customer believes there is a discrepancy between the cash back they received for a particular transaction and the cash back they should have received. This process applies to all credit card transactions.

## How to submit the dispute
Instead of a step-by-step workflow, instruct the customer to use the tool directly:

- Tool to provide to the user: `submit_cash_back_dispute_0589(user_id: str, transaction_id: str)`
- Tell the customer to run the tool with their own user_id and the specific transaction_id for the purchase in question.

## Agent notes
- Confirm the customer has the correct transaction_id before they submit.
- Do not collect sensitive card details; the tool uses the identifiers provided by the user.
- Advise the customer that supporting context (e.g., category or promotion expectations) may be requested later during review, but the submission itself is initiated with the tool above.

title: Think You're Missing Cash Back? Here's How to Get Help
content:
At Rho-Bank, we want to make sure you receive every dollar of cash back that you've earned. We understand that occasionally there may be discrepancies between the cash back you expected to receive on a purchase and what actually appeared on your account. If you've reviewed your rewards and something doesn't seem right, we're here to help investigate and make it right.

## When to Reach Out

You might want to contact us about your cash back if:

- A purchase that should have earned bonus cash back only received the standard rate
- You made a purchase in a bonus category but didn't receive the higher reward rate
- The cash back amount seems lower than what you calculated based on your card's rates
- A qualifying purchase doesn't show any rewards at all
- You believe a merchant was miscategorized and you should have earned a different rate

## How We Can Help

Our customer service team has the tools to investigate cash back discrepancies and review your transaction history. When you contact us, we'll look into the specific transaction, verify the merchant category, check the applicable reward rates for your card, and determine if an adjustment is needed. If we find that you were indeed shortchanged on your rewards, we'll make sure the correct amount is applied to your account.

## What You'll Need

To help us investigate quickly, it's helpful to have the following information ready when you contact us:

- The date of the transaction in question
- The merchant name and purchase amount
- The cash back amount you received versus what you expected
- Any receipts or documentation you have from the purchase

## Contact Us

If you think you're missing cash back or have any questions about how your rewards are calculated, please reach out to our customer service team. You can call us at 1-800-RHO-BANK, chat with us through the Rho-Bank mobile app, or visit rhobank.com/help. We're committed to ensuring you get the full value of your Rho-Bank credit card rewards.

title: Understanding Your Credit Card Rewards Categories
content:
One of the great benefits of your Rho-Bank credit card is the ability to earn rewards on your everyday purchases. To help you maximize your earnings, it's important to understand how reward categories work and which types of purchases qualify for bonus rewards on your specific card.

## How Reward Categories Work

Every Rho-Bank credit card earns rewards on all purchases, but certain categories of spending may earn you bonus rewards at a higher rate. Your card has a base reward rate that applies to all purchases, plus bonus categories where you can earn even more. The specific bonus categories and rates vary depending on which Rho-Bank credit card you have.

## Available Reward Categories

Rho-Bank recognizes the following spending categories for rewards purposes:

- **Travel**: Flights, hotels, car rentals, cruises, and other travel-related purchases
- **Dining**: Restaurants, cafes, bars, and food delivery services
- **Groceries**: Supermarkets, grocery stores, and specialty food shops
- **Entertainment**: Movies, concerts, sporting events, and streaming services
- **Shopping**: Retail stores, department stores, and online marketplaces
- **Utilities**: Electric, gas, water, internet, and phone bills
- **Transportation**: Rideshare services, public transit, parking, and tolls
- **Software**: Software subscriptions, licenses, and cloud services
- **Media**: Advertising spend and digital media purchases
- **Green/Sustainable**: Eco-friendly merchants and sustainable products
- **Operations**: Business operational expenses

## Maximizing Your Rewards

To get the most out of your rewards, we recommend using your Rho-Bank credit card for purchases in your card's bonus categories whenever possible. You can find your specific card's bonus categories and reward rates by logging into your account on the Rho-Bank mobile app or website, or by reviewing your card's terms and conditions.

If you have any questions about which category a particular merchant falls into, or how to maximize your rewards earning potential, our customer service team is always happy to help. Give us a call at 1-800-RHO-BANK or chat with us through the mobile app.

title: Virtual Card Management: Best Practices for Virtual Card Security
content:
## Core safeguards
- Turn on real-time transaction alerts (Yes) so you can validate unexpected charges immediately.
- Restrict merchant categories where appropriate (Yes) to reduce exposure to off-policy or high-risk spend.
- Use one-time cards for untrusted or short-term vendors (Yes) to minimize stored-card risk.
- Revoke at the first sign of compromise; immediate revocation (Yes) disables the card within 3 seconds.

## Routine hygiene
- Rotate cards used with third parties during vendor offboarding or scope changes.
- Avoid sharing card details across multiple users; provision separate cards instead.
- Store card data only in secured systems with access controls and audit logs.

## Rapid response playbook
1. Revoke the affected card (effective within 3 seconds).
2. Review alerts and recent transactions (Yes) to identify unauthorized activity.
3. If necessary, recreate access with a one-time card for immediate needs (Yes) while you investigate.
4. Tighten merchant category settings (Yes) before reissuing a replacement.

title: Virtual Card Management: Creating One-Time Use Cards for Security
content:
## Availability
- One-time (single-use) virtual cards must be enabled: Yes

## Create a one-time use card
1. Go to Cards > Virtual and select Create new card.
2. Choose the one-time use option (availability: Yes).
3. Confirm and generate the card details.
4. Use the card for a single transaction and do not store it for future use.

## Usage notes
- After one successful authorization, the card is no longer usable for additional payments.
- If a transaction fails, you may need to generate a new one-time card and retry.
- Share the card details only with the intended payee and avoid saving them in vendor profiles.

title: Virtual Card Management: Getting Started with Virtual Cards
content:
## Quick setup checklist
- Confirm instant issuance availability: Yes
- Expected issuance time: 5 seconds
- Maximum active virtual cards per account: 60
- Support for recurring-use cards: Yes
- Annual fee for this feature: $25.00

## Create your first virtual card
1. Go to Cards in your dashboard and select Virtual.
2. Choose Create new card.
3. Select the intended usage profile. If you need a card that can be reused for ongoing payments, choose the recurring option (availability: Yes).
4. Review and confirm. Your card details will be issued in 5 seconds if instant issuance is enabled (Yes).
5. Securely copy the card details only where needed.

## Capacity and costs
- You can keep up to 60 active virtual cards per account. Archive or cancel cards you no longer need before creating new ones if you hit this limit.
- The annual fee for virtual card management is $25.00. Plan issuance volume and usage to align with your cost expectations.

## Timing expectations
- If instant issuance is enabled (Yes), newly created virtual cards appear within 5 seconds.
- Leave the creation screen open until the card number and security details display.

### Recurring-use cards
- When enabled (Yes), select the recurring option at creation to support repeated transactions with the same vendor.

## Troubleshooting your first issuance
- If the card is not visible after 5 seconds, refresh the card list and verify that instant issuance is enabled (Yes).
- If you are approaching the maximum of 60 active cards, archive or cancel unused cards and retry.

title: Virtual Card Management: How to Instantly Issue a Virtual Card
content:
## Prerequisites
- Instant issuance must be enabled: Yes

## Steps to issue instantly
1. Navigate to Cards > Virtual.
2. Select Create new card.
3. Complete the required fields and confirm.
4. Wait for the card details to appear. With instant issuance (Yes), this takes about 5 seconds.

## Timing and verification
- Typical issuance time: 5 seconds from submission.
- Confirm that the card number, expiry, and security code are visible before leaving the screen.

## Common issues
- If the card does not display after 5 seconds, refresh the card list.
- Verify that instant issuance is enabled (Yes). Re-submit the request if needed.

title: Virtual Card Management: Managing Multiple Virtual Cards for Your Team
content:
## Plan your card inventory
- You can maintain up to 60 active virtual cards per account. Periodically archive or cancel unused cards to stay within this limit.

## Label cards clearly
- Use custom nicknames (Yes) to identify owner, purpose, or vendor (e.g., 'Marketing—Ad Platform A'). This makes reviews and audits faster.

## Apply spending guardrails
- If team caps are available (Yes), set appropriate limits for groups handling higher volumes.
- Configure per-card limits between $55 and $30,000 based on expected purchase size and frequency.

## Use recurring-use cards for ongoing vendors
- For subscriptions or ongoing billing, create recurring-use cards when supported (Yes) so recurring charges remain authorized while other spend is constrained.

## Lifecycle management
- Review card usage monthly. Rename cards (using Yes) to reflect changes in ownership or purpose.
- Reassign or cancel cards linked to completed projects to free capacity toward the 60 limit.

title: Virtual Card Management: Revoking and Canceling Virtual Cards
content:
## Choose the right action
- Revoke to immediately disable a card and block new authorizations.
- Cancel to permanently close a card you no longer intend to use.

## Revoke a virtual card
1. Open Cards > Virtual and select the target card.
2. Choose Revoke.
3. Confirm the action. Revocation is instant (Yes) and takes effect within 3 seconds.
4. Verify that new payment attempts are declined.

## Cancel a virtual card
1. Open the card details.
2. Select Cancel card and confirm.
3. The cancellation completes within 3 seconds. Ensure the card is removed from any saved payment profiles.

## Post-action checks
- Attempting a small authorization should decline within 3 seconds after revoke/cancel.
- Update internal records to reflect the status change.

title: Virtual Card Management: Setting Up Team Spending Caps and Limits
content:
## Enable spending controls
- Verify that team spending caps are available: Yes

## Set a per-card spending limit
1. Go to Cards > Virtual and open the target card.
2. Select Set limit.
3. Enter an amount between $55 and $30,000.
4. Choose the reset period (for example, per transaction, daily, or monthly) and save.

## Apply team-wide caps
1. Navigate to Team settings > Spending controls.
2. If supported (Yes), add a cap that applies across selected team members or card groups.
3. Define the cap amount and reset period, then save.

## Restrict merchant categories
- If available (Yes), select allowed or blocked merchant categories for a card or team policy to prevent out-of-policy spend.

## Review and adjust
- Start with conservative per-card limits (near $55) and increase as needed.
- Periodically compare limits against actual spend and adjust within the allowed range up to $30,000.

title: Virtual Card Management: Tracking Virtual Card Transactions and Spending
content:
## Real-time visibility
- Enable and rely on real-time spending alerts (Yes) to monitor approvals and declines as they occur.

## Organize with nicknames
- Assign consistent, descriptive labels to each card (Yes) so transactions group logically in reports and searches.
- Use a naming convention such as 'Team—Vendor—Purpose' to simplify filtering and reconciliation.

## Monitor recurring spend
- For ongoing payments, use recurring-use cards when available (Yes). This makes it easier to identify subscription charges and compare month over month.

## Monthly reconciliation workflow
1. Filter transactions by card nickname (Yes) to separate spending streams.
2. Review alert history (Yes) for any anomalies that require follow-up.
3. For recurring-use cards (Yes), compare current charges against expected schedules and amounts.
4. Export the filtered view and attach supporting documentation where needed.

title: What Happens When You Order a Replacement Card
content:
## What changes immediately
- Your current card is cancelled for security and cannot be used for new purchases.
- A new card is created with a different card number and CVV.

## What stays the same
- Your account number remains unchanged, so recurring payments linked to your account continue without interruption.

## What you need to do next
- Update any saved card details with merchants after you receive the new card.
- If the replacement is due to suspected fraud, review recent transactions in the Rho-Bank app or website and report any unauthorized charges.

## Notifications
- You receive an email when your replacement order is placed and another email when the card ships.
- Track your email for updates and follow any activation instructions included with your new card.

title: What is Provisional Credit?
content:
When you file a dispute for a credit card transaction, you may hear the term "provisional credit" mentioned. We want to help you understand what this means and how it might apply to your situation.

## Understanding Provisional Credit

Provisional credit is a temporary credit that may be applied to your account while we investigate a disputed transaction. Think of it as Rho-Bank giving you the benefit of the doubt—we temporarily restore the disputed amount to your account so you're not out of pocket while we work to resolve the issue. This allows you to continue using your available credit without being impacted by charges that may ultimately be reversed.

## How It Works

If you're eligible for provisional credit and you file a dispute, the disputed amount will be temporarily credited back to your account, usually within a few business days of the dispute being filed. This credit remains on your account while our team investigates the dispute, which can take anywhere from a few days to several weeks depending on the complexity of the case.

## After the Investigation

Once the investigation is complete, one of two things will happen:

- **If the dispute is resolved in your favor**: The provisional credit becomes permanent, and the matter is closed. You won't owe anything for that transaction.
- **If the dispute is not resolved in your favor**: The provisional credit will be reversed, and the original charge will reappear on your account. You'll be responsible for paying the amount as part of your regular statement.

## Eligibility

Not all disputes automatically qualify for provisional credit. Eligibility depends on several factors, including your account history, the type of dispute, the amount in question, and other criteria. When you file a dispute with our customer service team, they will let you know whether provisional credit applies to your situation.

## Questions?

If you have questions about provisional credit or want to check on the status of a dispute you've filed, please contact our customer service team at 1-800-RHO-BANK or through the Rho-Bank mobile app. We're here to help guide you through the process and keep you informed every step of the way.

title: Why was your credit card replacement request rejected
content:
## There is already a pending replacement request
- You cannot submit another replacement while an existing request is still being processed. Wait until the current replacement is delivered or cancelled before submitting a new request.

## You exceeded the replacement limit for your card tier
- Replacement requests are limited within a 60-day period to help protect your account.
- Limits by tier:
  - Entry-tier: up to 2 replacements per period.
  - Mid-tier: up to 3 replacements per period.
  - Premium-tier and above: up to 4 replacements per period.

## Need more help?
- If you believe you have a legitimate need for an additional replacement beyond these limits, contact customer support to request a manual review of your case.

title: Adding and verifying contacts
content:
## Add contacts
- Go to Everyone Pay and select Add Contact.
- Enter a username, email, or mobile number and confirm the contact’s display details.
- You can save up to $550 contacts, so consider adding frequent recipients for faster checkout.

## Verify contacts
- Contact verification before the first transaction: Yes.
- To verify a contact, open their profile and follow the prompts to confirm identity details.
- Verified contacts display a clear indicator so you can send with greater confidence.

## Managing your contact list
- Edit a contact to update names, labels, or notes.
- Remove contacts you no longer use to keep your list organized.
- Use search and filters to quickly locate a contact when sending.

title: Blocking and unblocking users
content:
## Block a user
- Open the user’s contact card from your contacts or a transaction.
- Select More options and choose Block user.
- Confirm to prevent the user from sending you requests or payments and to stop you from sending to them.

## Unblock a user
- Find the blocked contact in your contacts or privacy settings.
- Select Unblock and confirm to restore messaging and payment capabilities.

## What blocking does
- Blocks payment requests from the user.
- Prevents you from sending payments to the user while blocked.
- Hides their activity from your suggestions and search.

## Tips
- Use blocking when you no longer wish to interact with a user.
- If a user is abusive or fraudulent, block and report the contact from the same menu.

title: Blocking and unblocking users
content:
## Blocking a user
- Navigate to the user profile you want to block.
- Select the option to block and confirm your choice.
- If blocking fails, verify you have not reached the maximum of $275.

## Unblocking a user
- Go to your block list and choose the user you wish to unblock.
- Confirm unblocking to restore normal interactions.
- After unblocking, if you decide to block the same user again, you must wait 12 hours.

## Tips for managing your block list
- Periodically review your block list to ensure you stay within the limit of $275.
- If you need to block a new user but are at capacity, consider unblocking users you no longer need to restrict and note the 12 hours re-block window for any recently unblocked users.

title: Can blocked users still send me payment requests?
content:
## Answer
- Blocking prevents blocked users from sending payment requests: Yes.

## What to do next
- If you want to ensure no requests are received, confirm the user is on your block list and that the block is active.
- If requests appear to come from a different profile, block that profile as well and report any suspicious activity.

title: Canceling a payment
content:
## When cancellation is possible
- You can cancel a payment within 17 minutes of submission if it has not been claimed or posted.

## How to cancel
- Open Activity and select the pending payment.
- Choose Cancel and confirm.
- You will see a cancellation confirmation in your activity once complete.

## If you cannot cancel
- The payment may already be claimed, posted, or past the 17 minute window.
- If a payment cannot be canceled, consider requesting a return from the recipient or initiating a dispute when appropriate.

## Tips
- Double-check recipient details on the confirmation screen to reduce the need for cancellations.
- For time-sensitive reversals, act promptly within the available window.

title: Common security questions for Everyone Pay
content:
## Do I need to verify contacts before sending?
- Contact verification before the first transaction: Yes. Verification helps ensure you are paying the intended person.

## How do I reduce the risk of sending to the wrong person?
- Send only to saved contacts you recognize.
- Review the final confirmation screen before submitting.
- Look for a verified indicator on the contact profile when available.

## How does Rho handle suspicious activity?
- We use risk checks and monitoring to identify unusual patterns.
- Transactions may be paused for review if something looks abnormal.

## What should I do if I suspect fraud?
- Cancel the payment if it is still pending and eligible for cancellation.
- Report the issue from the transaction details and include all relevant information.
- Consider blocking the user to prevent further contact.

title: Common security questions for Everyone Pay
content:
## Do blocks prevent payment requests?
- Blocking prevents blocked users from sending payment requests: Yes.

## Are blocked users notified?
- Blocked users are notified they have been blocked: Yes.

## What should I do if I still receive activity from a blocked user?
- Confirm the user is on your block list and that the profile matches exactly.
- If the activity appears to come from a different profile, block that profile as well.
- If the behavior persists, contact support and include screenshots of the activity and the profile you blocked.

title: Disputing a payment
content:
## When to file a dispute
- You believe the payment was unauthorized.
- The amount or recipient does not match what you intended.
- You encounter suspected fraud or a scam.

## How to start a dispute
- Open the transaction from your activity and select Report a problem.
- Choose the reason that best describes the issue.
- Provide a clear description and attach any supporting evidence such as messages or receipts.
- Submit the dispute and monitor status from the transaction thread.

## What to include
- Why the payment is incorrect or unauthorized.
- Any communications with the recipient.
- Screenshots or documents that support your claim.

## What happens next
- We review your submission and may contact you for additional details.
- If the payment is eligible, we will pursue recovery with the counterparty.
- You will receive updates in-app and by email as the case progresses.

## Tips
- Act quickly once you notice an issue.
- Keep all relevant correspondence until the case is resolved.

title: Editing or canceling a scheduled payment
content:
## Edit options

- You can change the amount, next occurrence date, or cadence for future payments.
- Editing is locked within 13 hours of the upcoming occurrence. Changes made after that window take effect on later occurrences.

## How to edit

- Open the scheduled payment.
- Update the fields you need to change.
- Save your changes. If you are within 13 hours of the next occurrence, you will need to wait until it processes to see the updates apply.

## Canceling a scheduled payment

- Open the schedule and select Cancel.
- Confirm the cancellation to stop future occurrences.
- If the next occurrence is very near, it may still process even after you cancel. Editing remains locked within 13 hours, and cancellation may not affect an occurrence that has already begun processing.

## After you edit or cancel

- Review your schedule list to confirm the new details or the canceled status.
- If you edited near the 13-hour window, verify which occurrence the change affected.

title: Everyone Pay: Getting Started Guide
content:
## Access Everyone Pay
- Open the app or web dashboard and navigate to Payments.
- Select Everyone Pay to view your contacts and recent activity.

## Add your first contacts
- Use the Add Contact action and enter a name, username, email, or mobile number.
- You can save up to $550 contacts.
- Organize contacts with labels and notes to speed up future payments.

## Verify contacts before you send
- Contact verification before the first transaction: Yes.
- To verify, open the contact card and follow the prompts to confirm the recipient’s details.
- Look for a verified indicator on the contact card before sending.

## Configure your preferences
- Enable notifications to stay informed about requests, payments, and contact updates.
- Review your default funding account and any payment preferences in Settings.

## Safety tips
- Verify the recipient details on the confirmation screen every time.
- Use trusted contact information saved to your address book rather than retyping.
- If something looks off, pause and confirm with the recipient using a separate channel.

title: FAQ: Everyone Pay
content:
## Limits and fees
- What are my individual sending limits?
  - Your daily limit is $1,500 and your monthly limit is $12,500.
- How much does an instant transfer cost?
  - Instant transfers include a fee of $0.75 dollars, shown before you confirm.

## Cancellations
- How long do I have to cancel a payment?
  - You can cancel eligible payments within 17 minutes of submission.

## Contacts
- How many contacts can I save?
  - You can save up to $550 contacts in your address book.

## Tips
- Use standard delivery if timing is flexible and you prefer to avoid instant fees.
- Verify recipients before sending to reduce the risk of misdirected payments.
- If a payment fails or is declined, review your remaining daily and monthly capacity and confirm recipient details.

title: How do I increase my Everyone Pay sending limit?
content:
## Request a higher limit

- Open account settings and choose the option to request a limit increase.
- Describe your typical payment volumes, purpose, and the limit you are seeking.
- Allow up to 4 days for review and processing.

### Temporary boosts
- If you need a short-term increase for a specific payment or event, request a temporary boost.
- Temporary boosts can be approved up to 30.0% of your current limit, subject to review.

### Automatic increases
- Automatic increases based on account history: Yes.
  - When True, your limit may grow over time with positive usage and account standing.
  - When False, you will need to submit requests for any changes.

### Tips for faster approval
- Ensure your profile information and documentation are current.
- Provide clear business or personal context for expected payment flows.
- Request only the capacity you reasonably need.

title: How do I remove someone from a split payment?
content:
## Before you remove a participant
- Confirm whether the participant has already paid. If so, plan how you will reallocate or refund before removing them.
- Check your current participant count to understand how removal affects your group relative to the 30 cap.

### Remove a participant
- Open the split details and locate the participant you want to remove.
- Select the option to remove them and confirm your choice.
- Review remaining allocations so the total is still accurate after removal.

### After removal
- If the participant had an outstanding request, confirm it has been canceled.
- Reallocate the removed share among remaining members and verify that the total remains consistent.
- If you need to add a replacement, you can invite another participant as long as the total count stays at or below 30.

### Tips
- Communicate the change to the group so everyone understands any updated allocations.
- Keep a brief note in the split description to document why the participant was removed.

title: How do I stop a recurring payment?
content:
## Cancel the schedule

- Open the scheduled payment and choose Cancel.
- Confirm the cancellation to stop future occurrences.

## Timing considerations

- If you cancel close to the scheduled time, the next occurrence may still process if it has already begun. Editing is locked within 13 hours, and cancellation requests made very close to processing time may not stop the imminent occurrence.

## Verify the outcome

- Check your list of schedules to ensure the status is canceled.
- Monitor your activity around the scheduled date to confirm whether the imminent occurrence processed, then confirm that no further occurrences are set.

## Restarting later

- When you are ready to resume, create a new schedule with your preferred amount and timing.

title: How do I unblock someone I previously blocked?
content:
## Unblock steps
- Open your block list from your settings or privacy controls.
- Select the user you want to unblock and confirm.

## After unblocking
- If you change your mind, you must wait 12 hours before you can block the same user again.
- Review your interactions with the unblocked user to ensure they align with your preferences.

## Tips
- Keep a note of when you unblocked someone if you plan to re-block, so you can track the 12 hours window.
- If you cannot find the user in your block list, confirm you are viewing the correct account or profile.

title: How QR transfers work
content:
## Transfer flow
- A QR code is generated to represent a payment request and remains valid for 17.
- The payer scans the QR using the in-app scanner.
- The app validates the request details and prepares the payment.
- If Yes applies, you will be prompted to review and approve before funds move.
- Once approved (or automatically processed if no approval is required), the transfer is initiated.

### QR code lifecycle
- Created: The code is generated and starts its validity window of 17.
- Scanned: The payer’s app reads the code and loads the payment details.
- Confirmed: If Yes is in effect, the payer approves on the confirmation screen.
- Completed: Funds move after the app submits the payment.

### When a transfer won’t proceed
- The QR is older than 17 and must be regenerated.
- You close or dismiss the flow during the review stage when Yes applies.
- Connectivity issues interrupt the process before submission.

### Practical tips
- Scan promptly to stay within the 17 window.
- If prompted, use the Yes review moment to verify details before sending.

title: How to send and receive money
content:
## Sending money
- Open Everyone Pay and choose a contact from your list or add a new one.
- Enter the amount and an optional note visible to the recipient.
- Choose delivery speed:
  - Standard: completes within 12 hours and does not include the instant transfer fee.
  - Instant: delivers right away and includes a fee of $0.75 dollars or a percentage fee of 0.75% percent, as shown before you confirm.
- Review the confirmation screen and submit.

## Receiving money
- When someone sends you funds, you receive a notification and a new entry in your activity.
- For standard transfers, funds settle within 12 hours after the sender submits.
- For instant transfers, funds are delivered immediately and appear in your activity right away.

## Tips for smooth transfers
- Verify the contact details on the confirmation screen before submitting.
- If you do not recognize a request or payment, decline it and contact support from the transaction details screen.
- Keep your contact list current to avoid sending to outdated details.

title: Managing group balances
content:
## View and interpret statuses
- Open the split details to see each member’s owed amount and whether it has been paid.
- Use the activity timeline to confirm when reminders were sent and whether messages align with the 4 interval.

### Reminder cadence
- Automatic reminders are issued every 4 until the due date.
- If a member indicates they plan to pay soon, you can allow the schedule to continue rather than sending additional manual nudges.

### Due dates and follow-up
- Payments are due within 16 from when the split was created.
- As the due date nears, consider a direct message to any outstanding members to avoid last-minute issues.
- If the due date passes and balances remain, decide whether to continue reminders, adjust shares, or close and recreate the split as appropriate.

### Practical tips
- Reconcile partial payments promptly so remaining balances reflect accurate amounts.
- If a member claims they paid, check their status in the split details and the timing against the 4 schedule and 16 window.

title: Payment processing times
content:
## Standard transfers
- Settle within 12 hours after submission.
- Appear as pending until settlement completes.

## Instant transfers
- Deliver immediately to the recipient.
- Include an instant transfer fee of $0.75 dollars.

## Choosing your speed
- Use standard when delivery within the standard window is acceptable.
- Use instant when immediate delivery is essential and you accept the fee.

title: QR Transfers: Complete Guide
content:
## Before you start
Use this guide to complete a QR transfer end to end, understand timing, and stay within limits.

### Limits and timing
- Per transfer cap: You can send up to $1,250 in a single QR payment.
- Daily cap: Your total QR payments per day cannot exceed $3,000.
- QR validity: A generated QR code remains usable for 17 from the moment it is created.
- Confirmation step: Depending on your app settings, Yes determines whether you must approve the payment after scanning.

### Sending a payment via QR
- Open the QR payment tool in your app and generate a code for the intended amount.
- Share or display the QR to the payer and ensure it is scanned within 17.
- If Yes applies, review the recipient and amount carefully and approve to proceed.
- Keep the screen steady and bright to help scanners read the code quickly.

### Receiving a payment via QR
- Ask the sender to display their QR and scan it using your in-app scanner.
- Verify the recipient, amount, and any notes.
- If Yes applies, confirm to finalize.

### Managing limits
- If a transfer is close to $1,250, reduce the amount so it stays within the cap.
- If you reach $3,000, wait until your daily cap resets before attempting additional QR payments.

### Handling expiration
- If a code is older than 17, regenerate a fresh QR and try again.
- Avoid sharing screenshots of older codes, as they may have already expired.

### Confirmation behavior
- When Yes is in effect, use the review screen to double-check the payee name, amount, and any reference details before approving.
- If you dismiss or exit during this step, the transfer will not complete.

title: Scanning and sending payments
content:
## Scan the QR
- Open the QR scanner in your app and point your camera at the code.
- Hold the device steady and ensure the code fills most of the frame without cropping.
- If the code does not resolve quickly, adjust distance and reduce glare.

## Review and send
- Check the recipient and amount displayed after the scan.
- Ensure the payment amount does not exceed $1,250. If it does, lower the amount and try again.
- If Yes applies, review the details on the confirmation screen and approve to proceed.

## Tips for a smooth scan
- Increase on-screen brightness when scanning from another device’s display.
- Clean the camera lens and avoid obstructions.
- Ask the other party to refresh the QR if it appears unresponsive or outdated.

title: Scheduled Payments: Complete Guide
content:
## Key limits

| Item | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Maximum active schedules | 30 |
| Minimum days between occurrences | 4 |
| Maximum per-payment amount | $3,000 |
| Advance notification before charge | 3 |

## Scheduling rules

- Ensure at least 4 days between each occurrence when you choose the cadence.
- Set the amount at or below $3,000 for every occurrence.
- You can maintain up to 30 active schedules at any time.
- You will receive a reminder 3 days before each occurrence so you can verify details and available funds.

## Managing active schedules

- Review your list of active schedules to confirm upcoming dates and amounts.
- If you have reached 30, cancel an existing schedule before creating a new one.
- If you need to change timing so that occurrences are properly spaced, adjust the cadence to respect the 4 requirement.

## Notifications and confirmations

- Expect a notification 3 days prior to each scheduled payment. Use this reminder to double-check recipient details and your balance.
- After each payment processes, verify the debit and confirmation in your activity to ensure it matches the scheduled amount (not exceeding $3,000).

## Best practices

- Choose a cadence that naturally respects at least 4 days between occurrences to avoid schedule errors.
- Keep your available balance ready by the time you receive the 3-day reminder.
- If you need multiple schedules (e.g., different recipients), track how many are active so you do not exceed 30.

title: Security tips for QR transfers
content:
## Verify before you send
- Use the confirmation screen when Yes applies to review the recipient and amount. Do not approve if anything looks unfamiliar.

## Protect your QR
- Share QR codes only with the intended payer and avoid posting them publicly.
- Hide or close the QR as soon as the payment is completed to prevent reuse.
- Be cautious with screenshots; a copied code can be presented to others.

## Safe scanning habits
- Scan QR codes only from trusted sources. Do not scan codes from unknown flyers, messages, or displays you do not trust.
- Check for signs of tampering on printed materials, such as stickers placed over original codes.
- Use the in-app scanner rather than third-party scanner apps.

## If something looks off
- Cancel the payment and verify the details directly with the other party.
- Regenerate a new QR and compare details before proceeding.
- Keep your app updated to receive the latest security enhancements.

title: Sending limits — per day and month
content:
## Current sending limits

| Account type | Daily limit | Monthly limit |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Individual | $1,500 | $12,500 |
| Business | $15,000 | $150,000 |

## How limits work
- If you attempt a payment that would exceed your applicable daily or monthly limit, you will see a decline message indicating that the limit has been reached.
- Partial payments that keep you within your limit may be submitted.

## Checking your remaining limit
- On the send screen, review the limit banner to see remaining daily and monthly capacity.
- If you have reached a limit, consider waiting until limits refresh or adjusting the amount within the allowable range.

## Tips
- Frequent high-value senders may benefit from planning transfers across multiple days while staying within the posted limits.
- Business accounts have higher limits than individual accounts as shown above.

title: Sending limits — per day and month
content:
## Your per-day and per-month caps

- New accounts:
  - Daily: $750
  - Monthly: $7,500
- Verified accounts:
  - Daily: $3,500
  - Monthly: $35,000

### How they interact
- A payment must fit under your remaining daily cap and your remaining monthly cap.
- Reaching the daily cap stops additional sends for the rest of the day, even if you still have monthly capacity.
- Reaching the monthly cap stops additional sends for the rest of the month, even if you have daily capacity on a given day.

### Tips
- Space out higher-value sends to avoid hitting the daily cap.
- Monitor cumulative activity to preserve monthly capacity for end-of-month needs.

title: Sending Limits: Complete Guide
content:
## Current sending limits

| Account status | Daily send limit | Monthly send limit |
| --- | --- | --- |
| New accounts | $750 | $7,500 |
| Verified accounts | $3,500 | $35,000 |

### How daily and monthly limits work together
- Both limits apply at the same time. A send must be under your remaining daily allowance and your remaining monthly allowance.
- If either remaining allowance is insufficient, the send will be declined.

### Viewing your current limit availability
- Check your Everyone Pay send screen to see remaining daily and monthly availability before confirming a payment.
- If you are close to your limit, consider scheduling the payment for a later date.

### Requesting a higher limit
- You can submit a limit increase request from your account settings.
- Typical review time: up to 4 days.
- Provide context in your request, such as the purpose of payments and anticipated frequency, to help expedite review.

### Practical tips
- Plan larger disbursements across multiple days if needed to stay within your daily cap.
- Track cumulative activity so you do not exhaust your monthly capacity early.
- If you regularly reach your cap, request a permanent increase rather than relying on one-off workarounds.

title: Setting up a group payment
content:
## Prepare your group
- List the participants you plan to include and ensure the group will not exceed 30.
- Gather contact details so each member can receive their request without delay.

### Choose how to split
- Equal shares: Assign the same amount to each participant.
- Unequal shares: This is controlled by Yes. If enabled, assign custom amounts per person.
- Percentage-based: This is controlled by Yes. If enabled, allocate shares by percentages that add up to the total.

### Build the split
- Add participants up to 30.
- Select the split method based on Yes and Yes.
- Enter each participant’s share, verify the total, and attach a clear description so everyone understands what they owe.

### Review and send
- Confirm all participant details are accurate.
- Double-check shares match your chosen method and that totals reconcile.
- Send the split to start tracking responses and payments immediately.

title: Setting up recurring payments
content:
## Before you begin

- Confirm you have not reached your limit of active schedules: 30.
- Decide on an amount that does not exceed $3,000.
- Plan a cadence that respects at least 4 days between occurrences.

## Create a new recurring payment

- Select the recipient.
- Enter the payment amount up to $3,000.
- Choose the start date and cadence ensuring the gap between occurrences is at least 4 days.
- Review the summary and confirm.

## Tips for avoiding setup errors

- If the amount exceeds $3,000, reduce the amount before saving.
- If the cadence attempts to schedule occurrences too close together, increase the interval so there are at least 4 days between payments.
- If you already have 30 active, cancel an existing schedule before adding a new one.

## Verifying your schedule

- Check that the first and subsequent occurrences align with your intended timing and comply with the 4 rule.
- Confirm that the recurring amount remains at or below $3,000 for all future occurrences.

title: Split-the-Bill: Complete Guide
content:
## Key limits and timelines
- Maximum participants per group: 30
- Maximum total amount that can be split: $5,500
- Payment due date after creation: 16
- Automatic reminder cadence: every 4

### Creating and sending a split
- Confirm the total you intend to split is at or below $5,500. If it exceeds this limit, reduce the total or break it into multiple splits.
- Add participants up to the cap of 30. You can invite fewer; you cannot exceed this cap.
- Set each member’s share according to your preferred method, then review and send the request.

### Tracking collection status
- After sending, monitor each participant’s status in the split details. You’ll see who has paid and who is outstanding.
- Automatic reminders go out on a recurring basis every 4. You can also nudge members manually if needed.

### Due dates and what happens next
- Each member’s payment is due within 16 from creation.
- Before the due date, outstanding members will continue to receive reminders at the 4 interval.
- If the due date passes, review outstanding balances and decide whether to extend, resend, or resolve the split.

### Adjustments and edge cases
- If the total approaches $5,500, consider removing optional items or creating separate splits.
- If you reach 30 but still need to include others, create an additional split.
- Use the activity view to confirm whether reminders have gone out according to the 4 schedule and whether the 16 window is approaching.

title: Splitting restaurant or rent bills
content:
## Confirm the total and approach
- Verify the total you plan to split is within $5,500. If it exceeds this, reduce the total or separate the split.
- Choose a split method appropriate to the bill type:
  - Restaurants: Consider itemized or percentage-based shares if available through Yes or Yes.
  - Rent: Consider fixed shares (equal or custom) based on room size or agreements; use Yes if members owe different amounts.

### Using unequal or percentage-based splits
- Unequal splits are controlled by Yes. If enabled, assign specific amounts per person to reflect items ordered or agreed rent shares.
- Percentage-based splits are controlled by Yes. If enabled, allocate percentages to align with consumption or lease terms.

### Review before sending
- Confirm the sum of all shares equals the total and remains under $5,500.
- Add clear notes so each participant understands their portion and the reason for their share.

### After sending
- Monitor responses and adjust only if participants agree to changes. For large restaurant tabs or shared housing expenses, keep documentation handy to resolve questions about allocations.

title: Troubleshooting: payment didn't go through
content:
## Immediate checks

- Confirm you had sufficient available funds at the time of processing.
- Verify the recipient details are current and accurate.
- Ensure the scheduled amount complies with applicable limits.
- Check whether the scheduled date fell on a day your funding method could process payments.

## Auto-retry behavior

- If Yes is enabled, the system will attempt the payment again up to 3 times.
- When a retry is pending, add funds and keep them available until the next attempt.

## After the final attempt

- If all 3 attempts fail, the occurrence will not process.
- Review and adjust your schedule or amount for future occurrences, and ensure funds are available ahead of the next scheduled date.

## Common causes and fixes

- Insufficient funds: Add funds and, if Yes is enabled, wait for a retry.
- Recipient issues: Confirm the recipient information or request updated details.
- Amount limits: Reduce the scheduled amount to comply with limits.
- Timing conflicts: Move the next occurrence to a date that can process normally.

## Getting help

- If you still cannot identify the cause, capture any error messages shown and reach out through in-app support with the schedule name and the date the payment was supposed to process.

title: Troubleshooting: QR not scanning
content:
## Quick checks
- Confirm the QR code is still valid. If it was generated longer ago than 17, ask for a new code and try again.
- Increase screen brightness on the device displaying the QR to reduce glare and improve contrast.
- Wipe your camera lens and hold the device steady; adjust distance to bring the code into focus.

## Environment and device settings
- Avoid reflections from glossy screens; tilt the display slightly to remove hotspots.
- Ensure camera permissions are enabled for the app.
- Close other apps that may be using the camera and retry.

## QR quality
- If scanning from a printed code, verify it is not smudged, folded, or low-resolution.
- If scanning from a screen, disable any color inversion or display filters that reduce contrast.

## When to regenerate
- If the QR has been open for longer than 17 or has been shared as a screenshot and delayed, regenerate a fresh code and scan immediately.

title: Troubleshooting: uncollected payments
content:
## Understand timing
- Payments are due within 16 of creation. Before the deadline, outstanding members will continue to receive reminders every 4.

### If the deadline has not passed
- Confirm that automatic reminders align with the 4 schedule.
- Send a courteous manual reminder to clarify the amount and purpose.
- Check that the participant’s contact details are correct and that they were included in the split.

### If the deadline has passed
- Review the split details to confirm who is still outstanding relative to the 16 window.
- Consider whether to extend the arrangement by recreating the split or reissuing requests.
- Communicate any consequences or next steps clearly so members understand expectations.

### Reduce friction going forward
- Provide a concise description for the split so members recognize the charge.
- Give early notice before the 16 cutoff and reference the reminder cadence of 4 so participants know when to expect follow-ups.

title: User Blocking: Complete Guide
content:
## What happens when you block someone
- Blocking prevents blocked users from sending payment requests: Yes.
- Blocked users are notified they have been blocked: Yes.

## Limits and timing
- Maximum number of users you can block: $275.
- If you unblock someone, you must wait 12 hours before you can block the same user again.

## How to block a user
- Open the user profile you want to block.
- Choose the option to block the user and confirm.
- The user will immediately be added to your block list, subject to the limit of $275.

## How to unblock a user
- Open your block list and select the user you want to unblock.
- Choose the option to unblock and confirm.
- If needed, remember that re-blocking the same user requires waiting 12 hours.

## Quick reference
| Item | Policy |
| --- | --- |
| Maximum blocked users | $275 |
| Re-block cooldown | 12 hours |
| Payment requests prevented by blocking | Yes |
| Block notification sent to the user | Yes |

## Troubleshooting
- If you cannot block someone, check whether you have reached $275.
- If you recently unblocked the same user, wait 12 hours before attempting to block again.
- Verify you are blocking the correct profile, especially if there are similar names or duplicate profiles.

title: Using Everyone Pay for rent or allowance
content:
## Setting amounts

- Choose an amount that fits your rent or allowance plan and ensure it is at or below $3,000.
- For rent, confirm the recurring amount matches your agreed payment. If your rent exceeds $3,000, adjust the approach so each scheduled payment does not surpass that limit.

## Timing recommendations

- Set the schedule so payments arrive before the due date your recipient expects.
- Use the reminder you receive 3 days before each occurrence to verify funds and make any needed adjustments for future cycles.

## Staying organized

- Label the schedule clearly (e.g., Rent or Allowance) so it is easy to manage alongside other commitments.
- Use the 3-day reminder to confirm that the amount still aligns with your current rent or allowance agreement.

## After each payment

- Check your activity to confirm that the processed amount matches what you scheduled and does not exceed $3,000.
- If changes are needed for future payments, schedule them in advance so they take effect before the next reminder arrives 3 days ahead.

title: What is Everyone Pay?
content:
## Transfer options
- Standard transfer: completes within 12 hours once submitted.
- Instant transfer: funds are delivered right away for a fee of $0.75 dollars.

## Fees and timing
- Standard transfers do not include the instant transfer fee and arrive within the standard processing window of 12 hours.
- Instant transfers include a fee of $0.75 dollars and deliver immediately.

## Choosing the right option
- Select standard when timing is flexible and you want to avoid instant fees.
- Choose instant when the recipient needs funds right away and you accept the fee.

title: What is Everyone Pay?
content:
## A focused definition

Everyone Pay is Rho-Bank’s payment capability for sending funds to individuals or businesses from your account.

### How limits apply to Everyone Pay
- New accounts start with a daily cap of $750.
- New accounts start with a monthly cap of $7,500.
- Both caps apply at the same time; a send must be within your remaining daily and monthly capacity.

### What to expect when you start
- Your available amount updates after each completed send.
- If you reach either cap, you can resume sending when the relevant period renews or after an approved limit adjustment.

title: What's the difference between daily and monthly limits?
content:
## Side-by-side comparison

| Limit type | Applies to | New accounts | Verified accounts |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Daily | Total you can send in a single day | $750 | $3,500 |
| Monthly | Total you can send in a single month | $7,500 | $35,000 |

### How they work together
- Both limits are enforced at the same time.
- Even if you have daily capacity left, a payment will be declined if it exceeds your remaining monthly capacity.
- Large one-time payments can be constrained by the daily cap even when ample monthly capacity remains.

### Planning tips
- For sizable disbursements, check both remaining daily and monthly availability.
- Consider splitting larger amounts across multiple days if the daily cap is the binding constraint.
- If you frequently run into either cap, request an adjustment to align with your typical payment needs.

title: Why can't I block this user?
content:
## Common reasons
- You may have reached the maximum number of users you can block: $275.
- The profile may already be on your block list under a similar or duplicate name.
- The profile you are trying to block may not be eligible to be blocked due to account status or compliance restrictions.
- The profile might not match the user you intend to block; confirm the username and associated details.
- A temporary service issue may be preventing the action; try again later.

## What you can do
- Review your block list and remove users you no longer need to restrict to free up space if you are at $275.
- Verify the exact profile before attempting to block.
- If blocking remains unavailable, capture a screenshot of any error message and contact support.

title: Why can't I cancel my payment?
content:
## Common reasons
- The cancellation window of 17 minutes has passed.
- The recipient claimed the funds already.
- The transfer was sent using an instant option and posted immediately.
- A system action has already finalized the payment.

## What you can do
- If the payment is ineligible for cancellation, request a return from the recipient.
- If you believe the payment is unauthorized or incorrect, file a dispute from the transaction details.
- Review recipient details carefully before submitting future payments to reduce the need for cancellations.

## Tip
- Act as soon as you notice an error to stay within the available cancellation window of 17 minutes.

title: Why can't I create a split payment?
content:
## Hard limits that block creation
- Group size exceeds the maximum of 30. Reduce the participant list or create multiple groups.
- Total amount exceeds $5,500. Lower the total or split it into separate requests.

### Other common blockers
- Missing required details for one or more participants, such as contact information.
- The description or allocations are incomplete or do not add up to the total.
- You are attempting to add the same participant multiple times.

### How to proceed
- Trim the participant list to stay within 30.
- Adjust the total so it does not exceed $5,500 and ensure all shares reconcile to that total.
- Recheck participant details, then try creating the split again.

title: Why can't I edit my scheduled payment?
content:
## Editing lock window

- Editing is restricted within 13 hours of the scheduled time. During this window, changes to amount, date, or cadence are not allowed.

## How to proceed

- If you are inside the 13-hour window, wait for the upcoming occurrence to process, then edit the schedule for future occurrences.
- If you need to prevent future payments, cancel the schedule instead; changes take effect on upcoming occurrences that have not begun processing.

## Tips

- Plan changes ahead of the 13-hour cutoff so they apply to the next occurrence.
- After making edits outside the lock window, review the schedule summary to confirm the effective date of your changes.

title: Why can't I send money to this contact?
content:
## Common reasons
- The contact has not been verified and your account requires verification before the first transaction: Yes.
- You have blocked this user or they have blocked you.
- The contact information is invalid or no longer active.
- The recipient’s account cannot receive payments at this time.
- A security review is temporarily restricting the transfer.

## What you can do
- Open the contact card and complete verification if required.
- Confirm the contact’s username, email, or mobile number via a trusted channel.
- Check your block list and unblock the user if appropriate.
- Try again later if you see a temporary restriction message.
- If the issue persists, contact support from the failed transaction details.

title: Why can't I send more than $500 per day?
content:
## The most likely reasons

- Your account is subject to the default daily cap of $750.
- You attempted to send an amount that exceeds your remaining daily allowance after earlier payments today.

### How to resolve it
- Verify your account to qualify for a higher daily cap of up to $3,500 (subject to approval).
- Send a smaller amount that fits within your remaining daily allowance.
- Wait for your daily period to renew and complete the remaining amount then.

### Keep in mind
- Daily and monthly caps work together; even if the daily cap allows a payment, your monthly cap might not.

title: Why did I reach my sending limit?
content:
## Common reasons you hit a limit

- You reached your daily cap for new accounts: $750.
- You reached your monthly cap for new accounts: $7,500.
- Multiple smaller payments collectively used up your remaining allowance for the period.
- A payment you just completed reduced your available capacity below the amount you attempted to send next.

### How to confirm
- Check your Everyone Pay screen to view remaining daily and monthly availability.
- Review recent outgoing payments to see how they contributed to your totals.

### What you can do next
- Wait for the relevant period to renew and try again.
- Reduce the amount and send within your remaining allowance.
- Submit a request for a higher limit if you need additional capacity on an ongoing basis.

title: Why did my QR transfer fail?
content:
## Check limits first
- Per-transaction limit: If the amount exceeds $1,250, the transfer will not proceed. Reduce the amount and try again.
- Daily limit: If your total QR payments for the day exceed $3,000, you will need to wait until your daily cap resets before sending another QR payment.

## Other common causes
- You closed the payment screen before completion.
- Connectivity issues interrupted the submission.
- The QR data was invalid or damaged when scanned.

## How to resolve
- Adjust the amount so it is within $1,250 and resubmit.
- If you have reached $3,000, wait for the daily cap to reset and try again.
- Keep the app open until you see a success message, and ensure you have a stable connection.
- Ask the other party to regenerate a fresh QR if the original appears corrupted or unresponsive.

title: Why didn't my scheduled payment go through?
content:
## Possible reasons

- Insufficient available funds at the time of processing.
- Recipient details were invalid or out of date.
- The scheduled amount did not meet policy limits.
- The schedule date conflicted with processing availability.

## What happens next

- If Yes is enabled, the system will try again up to 3 times.
- Add funds and keep them available until the next attempt, then monitor for confirmation.

## How to resolve

- Verify and update recipient information.
- Adjust the amount to comply with limits.
- Move the next occurrence to a date that aligns with processing availability.
- If all 3 attempts fail, review your schedule settings and create a new occurrence after correcting the issue.

title: Why hasn't my group member paid yet?
content:
## Timing-related reasons
- The payment may still be within the allowed window of 16. Members often wait until closer to the due date.
- They might not have received the most recent automatic reminder yet if the 4 interval has not elapsed.

### What you can check
- Confirm the member is listed in the split and that their contact details are correct.
- Verify the reminder timeline shows messages sent at the 4 cadence.
- Look at the creation timestamp to see how far you are into the 16 period.

### Helpful next steps
- Send a polite manual reminder referencing the purpose of the split and the due date within 16.
- If they request more time, consider closing and recreating the split with expectations aligned to the 4 cycle.
- Keep notes in the split description so all members have the same context.

title: Why is my payment still pending?
content:
## Standard transfer timing
- Standard transfers complete within 12 hours. Until settlement, the payment appears as pending.

## Other reasons a payment can remain pending
- The recipient has not taken the required action to receive the funds.
- The payment is under routine review for security reasons.
- The recipient’s account is temporarily unable to accept funds.

## What to do next
- Check the transaction details for the current status and any requested actions.
- Confirm with the recipient that their account can receive funds.
- If the payment remains pending beyond the standard timeframe, contact support from the transaction screen.

title: Why isn't my QR code scanning?
content:
## Common causes
- The code expired because it was generated more than 17 ago.
- Screen glare or low brightness is obscuring the pattern.
- The camera cannot focus due to distance, motion, or a dirty lens.
- The QR is cropped, distorted, or low quality.
- Camera permissions for the app are disabled.

## Quick fixes
- Regenerate the QR if it may be older than 17.
- Increase the display brightness on the device showing the QR and reduce reflections.
- Clean your camera lens and adjust the distance until the code is sharp.
- Enable camera access in your device settings and reopen the scanner.
- Ask for a clearer or unaltered version of the QR if it looks compressed or edited.

title: Why was my Everyone Pay transaction declined?
content:
## Limits reached
- Individual accounts have a daily limit of $1,500 and a monthly limit of $12,500. If your payment would exceed either limit, it will be declined.

## Other possible causes
- Insufficient available balance in the selected funding account.
- Invalid or unverified recipient details.
- A temporary security review based on unusual activity.
- Network or system interruptions during submission.

## How to resolve
- Adjust the amount to fit within your remaining daily or monthly capacity.
- Confirm recipient details on the contact card and try again.
- Select a different funding account if available.
- Wait and retry after any temporary restrictions are cleared.
- If declines continue, contact support from the transaction screen.

## Where to find the entries

## Tips for smooth processing
- Plan larger deposits or transfers over multiple days if the amount exceeds your daily limits.
- Submit deposits and transfers as early as possible during the day to allow time for review and posting.

title: Bronze Account: Why did my interest rate change?
content:
## What can cause changes you notice
- Rate updates: Your account earns interest at the current APY. When the APY changes, your earnings adjust accordingly.
- Compounding effects: Because interest compounds daily, the amount of interest credited can vary with day count and balance changes even if the APY is unchanged.
- Credit card relationships: Holding certain Rho-Bank credit cards provides APY bonuses. If you hold a Platinum Rewards Card, you receive an additional +0.55% APY bonus. Diamond Elite Card holders receive +0.15% bonus.

## How to confirm your current APY
- Check your account details to view the currently applicable APY. If the current APY is listed as 2.0%, interest will accrue at that rate until it changes.

## Troubleshooting perceived changes
- Compare the APY shown in your account today with prior statements to see if a rate update occurred.
- Consider whether your average daily balance changed, which affects the interest dollar amount even at the same APY.

## Need more help?
- If the posted APY or credited interest does not match expectations, note the statement period and contact support for a review.

title: Gold Account: Gold Rewards Card holder benefits
content:
## Overview

Gold Account holders who also have a Gold Rewards Card receive exclusive benefits that make the account more accessible. The standard minimum balance requirement of $10,000 is reduced to $5,000 for Gold Rewards Card holders. Additionally, you receive a 0.025% relationship bonus APY on top of the base 5.5% rate. Combined with the existing 30 ATM fee rebates and 20 monthly withdrawals, Gold Rewards Card holders enjoy a premium savings experience with lower balance requirements.

## Benefits summary for Gold Rewards Card holders

- Reduced minimum balance: $5,000 (down from $10,000)
- Relationship bonus APY: 0.025% on top of the base 5.5%
- ATM fee rebates: Up to $30 per month
- Monthly withdrawals: Up to 20 per month

### Quick reference

| Benefit | Gold Rewards Card holder value |
|---|---|
| Minimum balance requirement | $5,000 (standard: $10,000) |
| APY rate | 5.5% + 0.025% |
| ATM fee rebates (monthly max) | $30 |
| Monthly withdrawal limit | 20 |

## Reduced minimum balance

- If you hold a Gold Rewards Card, your required minimum balance to maintain the Gold Account is automatically reduced to $5,000.
- The standard minimum balance for the Gold Account remains $10,000, but the override applies as long as your Gold Rewards Card is active and associated with your account.

Example:
- Without the card: Minimum balance requirement is $10,000.
- With the card: Minimum balance requirement is $5,000.

## Relationship bonus APY

- Your Gold Account earns the base 5.5% APY.
- As a Gold Rewards Card holder, you receive an additional 0.025% relationship bonus APY on top of the base rate.

Example:

- Relationship bonus APY: +0.025%

Notes:

- The bonus applies while your Gold Rewards Card remains active and associated with your Gold Account.

- You continue to receive up to $30 in ATM fee rebates each month.
- You are allowed up to 20 withdrawals per month.

  - Your monthly limits show up to $30 in ATM fee rebates and up to 20 withdrawals.

title: Green Account (savings): EcoCard holder exclusive benefits
content:
## EcoCard holder benefits overview

Green Account (savings) holders who also have an EcoCard receive enhanced benefits that make eco-conscious banking even more rewarding. Your monthly withdrawal limit is increased by 7 additional free withdrawals, bringing your total to 8 + 7 = 15 withdrawals per month. You also receive 20 in ATM fee rebates monthly. Combined with the existing 0.5% bonus APY for EcoCard holders on top of the base 4.0% rate, this creates a comprehensive eco-friendly savings experience with maximum flexibility.

## What you get as an EcoCard holder

- Increased monthly free withdrawals: 8 + 7 = 15 total free withdrawals per month
- ATM fee rebates: up to $20 in rebates each month
- Rate boost: 0.5% bonus APY on top of the base 4.0% rate (example total: 4.75%)

## Increased free withdrawals

- Your free withdrawal allowance increases by 7 each month, for a total of 15 free withdrawals.
- Examples:
  - 10 withdrawals in a month: all 10 are free.
  - 15 withdrawals in a month: all 15 are free.
  - More than 15 withdrawals in a month: any withdrawals beyond 15 are not free.

## ATM fee rebates

- You receive up to $20 in ATM fee rebates each month.
- If total ATM operator fees in a month exceed $20, the excess amount is not rebated.
- Tip: Keep your receipts or review your statements to track how much of the $20 monthly cap you have used.

## Bonus APY for EcoCard holders

- Your Green Account earns the base 4.0% APY plus a 0.5% bonus when you hold an EcoCard.
- Example: 4.0% + 0.5% = 4.75% APY.

## Quick reference

- Total free withdrawals per month: 8 + 7 = 15
- Monthly ATM fee rebates: $20
- APY boost: 4.0% base + 0.5% bonus
